When you talk, you prefer to speak with facts which you have double-checked yourself or cite sources that provide evidence, like quote actual code or give references to documents or pages found on the internet. You are polite and patient, and prefer to assume good intent, trying your best to be helpful. If you are unsure or have assumptions, you prefer to test them yourself or ask questions to clarify requirements.
General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.
   - When running sudo commands (especially package installations like apt-get, yum, npm install, etc.), always run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.
   - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
      Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo link-assistant/agent --branch issue-231-8fb726b5bf3c --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
      Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo link-assistant/agent --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an ./examples and/or ./experiments folders so you can reuse them later.
   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to ./experiments folder.
   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to ./examples folder.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.

Initial research.
   - When you start, make sure you create detailed plan for yourself and follow your todo list step by step, make sure that as many points from these guidelines are added to your todo list to keep track of everything that can help you solve the issue with highest possible quality.
   - When user mentions CI failures or asks to investigate logs, consider adding these todos to track the investigation: (1) List recent CI runs with timestamps, (2) Download logs from failed runs to ci-logs/ directory, (3) Analyze error messages and identify root cause, (4) Implement fix, (5) Verify fix resolves the specific errors found in logs.
   - When you read issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use Read tool to view and analyze it. Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML) using a CLI tool like the 'file' command to check the actual file format. When corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's \"Not Found\" pages saved as .png) are read, they can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and crash the AI solver process. When the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download failed — do not call Read on this file. Instead: (1) When images are from GitHub issues/PRs (URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\"), these require authentication — retry with: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token \$(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\" (2) When the retry still fails, skip the image and note it was unavailable.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/231.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner link-assistant [keywords].
   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.
   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has three different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/link-assistant/agent/pulls/233/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/link-assistant/agent/issues/233/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/link-assistant/agent/pulls/233/reviews --paginate

   - When issue is solvable, first create a test that reproduces the problem, then implement the fix.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.
   - When coding, each atomic step that can be useful by itself should be committed to the pull request's branch, meaning if work will be interrupted by any reason parts of solution will still be kept intact and safe in pull request.

      start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;
      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.

Reproducible testing.
   - When fixing a bug, create a test that reproduces the problem before implementing the fix. When you cannot reproduce the problem, you cannot verify the fix.
   - When encountering logic bugs, write an automated test that fails due to the bug, then implement the fix to make it pass.
   - When encountering UI bugs, capture a screenshot showing the problem state, then create a visual regression test or manual verification screenshot after the fix.
   - When creating tests, prefer minimum reproducible examples - the simplest test case that demonstrates the issue.
   - When submitting a fix, include in the PR description: (1) how to reproduce the issue, (2) the automated test that verifies the fix, (3) before/after screenshots for UI issues.
   - When a bug fix doesn't have a reproducing test, the fix is incomplete - regressions can silently occur later.

Playwright MCP usage (browser automation via mcp__playwright__* tools).
   - When you develop frontend web applications (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, Angular, etc.), use Playwright MCP tools to test the UI in a real browser.
   - When WebFetch tool fails to retrieve expected content (e.g., returns empty content, JavaScript-rendered pages, or login-protected pages), use Playwright MCP tools (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot) as a fallback for web browsing.
   - When you need to interact with dynamic web pages that require JavaScript execution, use Playwright MCP tools.
   - When you need to visually verify how a web page looks or take screenshots, use browser_take_screenshot from Playwright MCP.
   - When you need to fill forms, click buttons, or perform user interactions on web pages, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form).
   - When you need to test responsive design or different viewport sizes, use browser_resize from Playwright MCP.
   - When you finish using the browser, always close it with browser_close to free resources.
   - When reproducing UI bugs, use browser_take_screenshot to capture the problem state before implementing any fix.
   - When fixing UI bugs, take before/after screenshots to provide visual evidence of the fix for human verification.
   - When creating UI tests, save baseline screenshots to the repository for visual regression testing.
   - When verifying UI fixes, compare screenshots to ensure the fix doesn't introduce unintended visual changes.

Visual UI work and screenshots.
   - When you work on visual UI changes (frontend, CSS, HTML, design), include a render or screenshot of the final result in the pull request description.
   - When you need to show visual results, take a screenshot and save it to the repository (e.g., in a docs/screenshots/ or assets/ folder).
   - When you save screenshots to the repository, use permanent links in the pull request description markdown (e.g., https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/blob/issue-231-8fb726b5bf3c/docs/screenshots/result.png?raw=true).
   - When uploading images, commit them to the branch first, then reference them using the GitHub blob URL format with ?raw=true suffix (works for both public and private repositories).
   - When the visual result is important for review, mention it explicitly in the pull request description with the embedded image.
   - When fixing UI bugs, capture both the \"before\" (problem) and \"after\" (fixed) screenshots as evidence for human verification.
   - When reporting UI bugs, include a screenshot of the problem state to enable visual verification of the fix.

message: 'no cache found, awaiting refresh',\n102\t        path: filepath,\n103\t      }));\n104\t      await refresh();\n105\t    } else {\n106\t      // Check if cache is stale\n107\t      const filepath,\n116\t          age: Date.now() - mtime,\n117\t          threshold: CACHE_STALE_THRESHOLD_MS,\n118\t        }));\n119\t        await refresh();\n120\t      } else {\n121\t        // Fresh cache - trigger background refresh but don't wait\n122\t        log.info(() => ({\n123\t          message: 'cache is fresh, triggering background refresh',\n124\t          path: filepath,\n125\t          age: Date.now() - mtime,\n126\t        }));\n127\t        refresh();\n128\t      }\n129\t    }\n130\t\n131\t    // Now read the cache file\n132\t    const result = await file.json().catch(() => unavailable or corrupted\n137\t    // Using info level since bundled data is a valid fallback mechanism\n138\t    // @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/177\n139\t    log.info(() => ({\n140\t      message: 'cache unavailable, using bundled data',\n141\t      path: filepath,\n142\t    }));\n143\t    const json = await data();\n144\t    return JSON.parse(json) as Record<string, Provider>;\n145\t  }\n146\t\n147\t  export async function refresh() {\n148\t    const file = Bun.file(filepath);\n149\t    log.info(() => ({ message: 'refreshing', file }));\n150\t    const result = await verboseFetch('https://models.dev/api.json', {\n151\t      headers: {\n152\t        'User-Agent': 'agent-cli/1.0.0',\n153\t      },\n154\t      signal: AbortSignal.timeout(10 * 1000),\n155\t *\n57\t   * When the compaction model has equal or smaller context, the configured\n58\t   * safety margin applies (default 15% → ratio 0.85).\n59\t   *\n60\t   * @see is JSON (#200)\n66\t// Bun runtime may print errors in plain text format (e.g., stack traces with source code)\n67\t// This interceptor wraps any non-JSON stderr output in a JSON envelope\n68\tconst originalStderrWrite = process.stderr.write.bind(process.stderr);\n69\tprocess.stderr.write = function (chunk, encoding, callback) {\n70\t  const str = typeof chunk === 'string' ? chunk : chunk.toString();\n71\t  const trimmed = str.trim();\n72\t  if (!trimmed) {\n73\t    return originalStderrWrite(chunk, encoding, callback);\n74\t  }\n75\t\n76\t  // Check if it's already valid originalStderrWrite(chunk, encoding, callback);\n82\t    } catch (_e) {\n83\t      // Not valid JSON, wrap it\n84\t    }\n85\t  }\n86\t\n87\t  // Wrap non-JSON stderr output in JSON envelope\n88\t  const wrapped = `${JSON.stringify({\n89\t    type: 'error',\n90\t    errorType: 'RuntimeError',\n91\t    message: trimmed,\n92\t  })}\\n`;\n93\t  return originalStderrWrite(wrapped, encoding, })}\\n`\n115\t    );\n116\t  }\n117\t  process.exit(1);\n118\t});\n119\t\n120\tprocess.on('unhandledRejection', (reason, _promise) => {\n121\t  hasError = true;\n122\t  try {\n123\t    const errorOutput = {\n124\t      errorType: 'UnhandledRejection',\n125\t      message: reason?.message || String(reason),\n126\t      stack: reason?.stack,\n127\t    };\n128\t    // If the error has a data property with a suggestion (e.g., ProviderModelNotFoundError), add it as a hint\n129\t    if (reason?.data?.suggestion) {\n130\t      errorOutput.hint = reason.data.suggestion;\n131\t    }\n132\t    // Include error data for debugging (#200)\n133\t    if (reason?.data) {\n134\t      errorOutput.data = reason.data;\n135\t    }\n136\t    outputError(errorOutput);\n137\t  } catch (_serializationError) {\n138\t    // Last resort: write minimal JSON directly to stderr\n139\t    process.stderr.write(\n140\t      `${JSON.stringify({\n141\t        type: 'error',\n142\t        errorType: 'UnhandledRejection',\n143\t        message: String(reason),\n144\t      })}\\n`\n145\t    );\n146\t  }\n147\t  process.exit(1);\n148\t});\n149\t\n150\t/**\n151\t * Read stdin with optional timeout.\n152\t * @param {number|null} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds. If null, wait indefinitely until EOF.\n153\t * @returns {Promise<string>} - The stdin content\n154\t */\n155\tfunction readStdinWithTimeout(timeout = null) {\n156\t  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n157\t    let data = '';\n158\t    let hasData = false;\n159\t    let timer = null;\n160\t\n161\t    const cleanup = () => {\n162\t      if (timer) {\n163\t        clearTimeout(timer);\n164\t      }\n165\t      process.stdin.removeListener('data', onData);\n166\t      process.stdin.removeListener('end', onEnd);\n167\t      process.stdin.removeListener('error', onError);\n168\t    };\n169\t\n170\t    const onData = (chunk) => {\n171\t      hasData = true;\n172\t      if (timer) {\n173\t        clearTimeout(timer);\n174\t      }\n175\t      data += chunk;\n176\t    };\n177\t\n178\t    const onEnd = () => {\n179\t      cleanup();\n180\t      resolve(data);\n181\t    };\n182\t\n183\t    const onError = () => {\n184\t      cleanup();\n185\t      resolve('');\n186\t    };\n187\t\n188\t    // Only set timeout if specified (not null)\n189\t    if (timeout !== null) {\n190\t      timer = setTimeout(() => {\n191\t        if (!hasData) {\n192\t          process.stdin.pause();\n193\t          cleanup();\n194\t          resolve('');\n195\t        }\n196\t      }, timeout);\n197\t    }\n198\t\n199\t    process.stdin.on('data', onData);\n200\t    process.stdin.on('end', onEnd);\n201\t    process.stdin.on('error', onError);\n202\t  });\n203\t}\n204\t\n205\t/**\n206\t * Read system message from files if specified\n207\t * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n208\t * @returns {object} - { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage }\n209\t */\n210\tasync function readSystemMessages(argv) {\n211\t  let systemMessage = argv['system-message'];\n212\t  let appendSystemMessage = argv['append-system-message'];\n213\t\n214\t  if (argv['system-message-file']) {\n215\t    const resolvedPath =    outputError({\n222\t        errorType: 'FileNotFound',\n223\t        message: `System message file not found: ${argv['system-message-file']}`,\n224\t      });\n225\t      process.exit(1);\n226\t    }\n227\t    systemMessage = await file.text();\n228\t  }\n229\t\n230\t  if (argv['append-system-message-file']) {\n231\t    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n232\t      process.cwd(),\n233\t      argv['append-system-message-file']\n234\t    );\n235\t    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n236\t    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n237\t      outputError({\n238\t appendSystemMessage = await file.text();\n244\t  }\n245\t\n246\t  return { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage };\n247\t}\n248\t\n249\tasync function runAgentMode(argv, request) {\n250\t  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n251\t  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n252\t    message: 'Agent started',\n253\t    version: pkg.version,\n254\t    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n255\t    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n256\t    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n257\t  }));\n258\t  if (config.dryRun) {\n259\t    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n260\t      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n261\t      mode: 'dry-run',\n262\t    }));\n263\t  }\n264\t\n265\t  // Validate and get JSON standard\n266\t  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n267\t  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n268\t    outputStatus({\n269\t      type: 'error',\n270\t      errorType: 'ValidationError',\n271\t      message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n272\t    });\n273\t    process.exit(1);\n274\t  }\n275\t\n276\t  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n277\t\n278\t  // Logging is already initialized in middleware, no need to call Log.init() again\n279\t\n280\t  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n281\t  // parseModelConfig must be called inside Instance.provide to access provider state\n282\t  await Instance.provide({\n283\t    directory: process.cwd(),\n284\t    fn: async () => {\n285\t      // Parse model config inside Instance.provide context\n286\t      // This allows parseModelWithResolution to access the provider state\n287\t      const { providerID, modelID, compactionModel } = await parseModelConfig(\n288\t        argv,\n289\t        outputError,\n290\t        outputStatus\n291\t      );\n292\t\n293\t      if (argv.server) {\n294\t        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n295\t        await runServerMode(\n296\t          argv,\n297\t          request,\n298\t          providerID,\n299\t          modelID,\n300\t          systemMessage,\n301\t          appendSystemMessage,\n302\t          jsonStandard,\n303\t          compactionModel\n304\t        );\n305\t      } else {\n306\t        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n307\t        await runDirectMode(\n308\t          argv,\n309\t          request,\n310\t          providerID,\n311\t          modelID,\n312\t          systemMessage,\n313\t          appendSystemMessage,\n314\t          jsonStandard,\n315\t          compactionModel\n316\t        );\n317\t      }\n318\t    },\n319\t  });\n320\t\n321\t  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates (#213)\n322\t  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n323\t    message: 'Agent exiting',\n324\t    hasError,\n325\t    uptimeSeconds: Math.round(process.uptime()),\n326\t  }));\n327\t  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n328\t}\n329\t\n330\t/**\n331\t * Run agent in continuous stdin mode\n332\t * Keeps accepting input until EOF or SIGINT\n333\t * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n334\t */\n335\tasync function runContinuousAgentMode(argv) {\n336\t  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n337\t  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n338\t  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n339\t    message: 'Agent started (continuous mode)',\n340\t    version: pkg.version,\n341\t    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n342\t    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n343\t    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n344\t  }));\n345\t  if (config.dryRun) {\n346\t    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n347\t      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n348\t      mode: 'dry-run',\n349\t    }));\n350\t  }\n351\t\n352\t  // Validate and get JSON standard\n353\t  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n354\t  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n355\t    outputStatus(\n356\t      {\n357\t        type: 'error',\n358\t        message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n359\t      },\n360\t      compactJson\n361\t    );\n362\t    process.exit(1);\n363\t  }\n364\t\n365\t  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n366\t\n367\t  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n368\t  // parseModelConfig must be called inside Instance.provide to access provider state\n369\t  await Instance.provide({\n370\t    directory: process.cwd(),\n371\t    fn: async () => {\n372\t      // Parse model config inside Instance.provide context\n373\t      // This allows parseModelWithResolution to access the provider state\n374\t      const { providerID, modelID, compactionModel } = await parseModelConfig(\n375\t        argv,\n376\t        outputError,\n377\t        outputStatus\n378\t      );\n379\t\n380\t      if (argv.server) {\n381\t        // SERVER MODE: Start server and communicate via HTTP\n382\t        await runContinuousServerMode(\n383\t          argv,\n384\t          providerID,\n385\t          modelID,\n386\t          systemMessage,\n387\t          appendSystemMessage,\n388\t          jsonStandard,\n389\t          compactionModel\n390\t        );\n391\t      } else {\n392\t        // DIRECT MODE: Run everything in single process\n393\t        await runContinuousDirectMode(\n394\t          argv,\n395\t          providerID,\n396\t          modelID,\n397\t          systemMessage,\n398\t          appendSystemMessage,\n399\t          jsonStandard,\n400\t          compactionModel\n401\t        );\n402\t      }\n403\t    },\n404\t  });\n405\t\n406\t  // Explicitly exit to ensure process terminates (#213)\n407\t  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n408\t    message: 'Agent exiting',\n409\t    hasError,\n410\t    uptimeSeconds: Math.round(process.uptime()),\n411\t  }));\n412\t  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n413\t}\n414\t\n415\tasync function runServerMode(\n416\t  argv,\n417\t  request,\n418\t  providerID,\n419\t  modelID,\n420\t  systemMessage,\n421\t  appendSystemMessage,\n422\t  jsonStandard,\n423\t  compactionModel\n424\t) {\n425\t  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n426\t\n427\t  // Start server like OpenCode does\n428\t  const server = Server.listen({ port: 0, hostname: '127.0.0.1' });\n429\t  let unsub = null;\n430\t\n431\t  try {\n432\t    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n433\t    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n434\t\n435\t    let sessionID;\n436\t\n437\t    if (resumeInfo) {\n438\t      // Use the resumed/forked session\n439\t      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n440\t    } else {\n441\t      // Create a new session\n442\t      const localVerboseFetch = createVerboseFetch(fetch, { caller: 'cli' });\n443\t      const createRes = await localVerboseFetch(\n444\t        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session`,\n445\t        {\n446\t          method: 'POST',\n447\t          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n448\t          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n449\t        }\n450\t      );\n451\t      const session = await createRes.json();\n452\t      sessionID = session.id;\n453\t\n454\t      if (!sessionID) {\n455\t        throw new Error('Failed to create session');\n456\t      }\n457\t    }\n458\t\n459\t    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n460\t    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n461\t\n462\t    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n463\t    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n464\t      createBusEventSubscription({\n465\t        sessionID,\n466\t        eventHandler,\n467\t        onError: () => {\n468\t          hasError = true;\n469\t        },\n470\t      });\n471\t    unsub = eventUnsub;\n472\t\n473\t    // Send message to session with specified model (default: opencode/grok-code)\n474\t    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n475\t    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n476\t\n477\t    // Start the prompt (don't wait for response, events come via Bus)\n478\t    fetch(\n479\t      `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session/${sessionID}/message`,\n480\t      {\n481\t        method: 'POST',\n482\t        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n483\t        body: JSON.stringify({\n484\t          parts,\n485\t          model: {\n486\t            providerID,\n487\t            modelID,\n488\t          },\n489\t          compactionModel,\n490\t          system: systemMessage,\n491\t          appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n492\t        }),\n493\t      }\n494\t    ).catch((error) => {\n495\t      hasError = true;\n496\t      eventHandler.output({\n497\t        type: 'error',\n498\t        timestamp: Date.now(),\n499\t        sessionID,\n500\t        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n501\t      });\n502\t    });\n503\t\n504\t    // Wait for session to become idle\n505\t    await eventPromise;\n506\t  } finally {\n507\t    // Always clean up resources\n508\t    if (unsub) {\n509\t      unsub();\n510\t    }\n511\t    server.stop();\n512\t    await Instance.dispose();\n513\t  }\n514\t}\n515\t\n516\tasync function runDirectMode(\n517\t  argv,\n518\t  request,\n519\t  providerID,\n520\t  modelID,\n521\t  systemMessage,\n522\t  appendSystemMessage,\n523\t  jsonStandard,\n524\t  compactionModel\n525\t) {\n526\t  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n527\t\n528\t  // DIRECT MODE: Run in single process without server\n529\t  let unsub = null;\n530\t\n531\t  try {\n532\t    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n533\t    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n534\t\n535\t    let sessionID;\n536\t\n537\t    if (resumeInfo) {\n538\t      // Use the resumed/forked session\n539\t      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n540\t    } else {\n541\t      // Create a new session directly\n542\t      const session = await Session.createNext({\n543\t        directory: process.cwd(),\n544\t      });\n545\t      sessionID = session.id;\n546\t    }\n547\t\n548\t    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n549\t    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n550\t\n551\t    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n552\t    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n553\t      createBusEventSubscription({\n554\t        sessionID,\n555\t        eventHandler,\n556\t        onError: () => {\n557\t          hasError = true;\n558\t        },\n559\t      });\n560\t    unsub = eventUnsub;\n561\t\n562\t    // Send message to session directly\n563\t    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n564\t    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n565\t\n566\t    // Start the prompt directly without HTTP\n567\t    SessionPrompt.prompt({\n568\t      sessionID,\n569\t      parts,\n570\t      model: {\n571\t        providerID,\n572\t        modelID,\n573\t      },\n574\t      compactionModel,\n575\t      system: systemMessage,\n576\t      appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n577\t    }).catch((error) => {\n578\t      hasError = true;\n579\t      eventHandler.output({\n580\t        type: 'error',\n581\t        timestamp: Date.now(),\n582\t        sessionID,\n583\t        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n584\t      });\n585\t    });\n586\t\n587\t    // Wait for session to become idle\n588\t    await eventPromise;\n589\t  } finally {\n590\t    // Always clean up resources\n591\t    if (unsub) {\n592\t      unsub();\n593\t    }\n594\t    await Instance.dispose();\n595\t  }\n596\t}\n597\t\n598\tasync function main() {\n599\t  try {\n600\t    // Parse command line arguments with subcommands\n601\t    const yargsInstance = yargs(hideBin(process.argv))\n602\t      .scriptName('agent')\n603\t      .usage('$0 [command] [options]')\n604\t      .version(pkg.version)\n605\t      // MCP subcommand\n606\t      .command(McpCommand)\n607\t      // Auth subcommand\n608\t      .command(AuthCommand)\n609\t      // Default command for agent mode (when no subcommand specified)\n610\t      .command({\n611\t        command: '$0',\n612\t        describe: 'Run agent in interactive or stdin mode (default)',\n613\t        builder: buildRunOptions,\n614\t        handler: async (argv) => {\n615\t          // Check both CLI flag and environment variable for compact JSON mode\n616\t          const compactJson =\n617\t            argv['compact-json'] === true || config.compactJson;\n618\t\n619\t          // Check if --prompt flag was provided\n620\t          if (argv.prompt) {\n621\t            // Direct prompt mode - bypass stdin entirely\n622\t            const request = { message: argv.prompt };\n623\t            await runAgentMode(argv, request);\n624\t            return;\n625\t          }\n626\t\n627\t          // Check if --disable-stdin was set without --prompt\n628\t          if (argv['disable-stdin']) {\n629\t            // Output a helpful message suggesting to use --prompt\n630\t            outputError(\n631\t              {\n632\t                errorType: 'ValidationError',\n633\t                message:\n634\t                  'No Safety margin percentage (default 15%)\n\n**Fallback Logic:**\n\n1. **Rate Limit Fallback** (`provider.ts`, `getAlternativeProviders()`):\n   - Only applies if user did NOT explicitly specify provider\n   - Uses `SHARED_FREE_MODELS` map (currently empty - no shared models between OpenCode and Kilo)\n   - Returns list of alternative providers when primary fails\n\n2. **Retry Logic** (`session/retry.ts`):\n   - `RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY = 2000ms`\n   - `RETRY_BACKOFF_FACTOR = 2`\n   - `RETRY_MAX_DELAY_NO_HEADERS = 30_000ms`\n   - `SOCKET_ERROR_MAX_RETRIES = 3`\n   - `TIMEOUT_MAX_RETRIES = 3`\n   - Respects Retry-After header from API\n   - Can timeout if wait time exceeds `config.retryTimeout`\n\n---\n\n### 3. HTTP REQUEST/RESPONSE LOGGING\n\n**Main file:** `/util/verbose-fetch.ts`\n\n**Features:**\n- Wraps fetch() globally when `--verbose` flag is enabled\n- Logs in JSON format with error resilience\n- **Caller identifier** system for correlating logs across codebase\n- **Sequential call numbering** for request/response correlation\n\n**What's logged:**\n\n**Request logging:**\n```\n{\n  \"type\": \"log\",\n  \"level\": \"info\",\n  \"service\": \"http\",\n  \"message\": \"HTTP request\",\n  \"caller\": \"models.dev|global|cli|etc\",\n  \"callNum\": <sequential-number>,\n  \"method\": \"GET|POST|etc\",\n  \"url\": \"https://...\",\n  \"headers\": <sanitized-headers>,\n  \"bodyPreview\": <truncated-body>\n}\n```\n\n**Response logging:**\n```\n{\n  \"type\": \"log\",\n  \"level\": \"info\",\n  \"service\": \"http\",\n  \"message\": \"HTTP response\",\n  \"status\": 200,\n  \"statusText\": \"OK\",\n  \"durationMs\": <elapsed-time>,\n  \"responseHeaders\": <headers>\n}\n```\n\n**Response body logging:**\n- **Non-streaming:** Logged immediately with truncation at 200k chars\n- **Streaming:** Tee'd stream, logged asynchronously to avoid blocking\n\n**Error logging:**\n```\n{\n  \"type\": \"log\",\n  \"level\": \"error\",\n  \"message\": \"HTTP request failed\",\n  \"error\": {\n    \"name\": \"...\",\n    \"message\": \"...\",\n    \"stack\": \"...\",\n    \"cause\": \"...\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Key utilities:**\n- `sanitizeHeaders()` - Masks authorization, x-api-key, api-key values\n- `bodyPreview()` - Truncates body to maxChars with indicator\n- `createVerboseFetch()` - Wrapper factory\n- Error resilient: logging failures never break the actual HTTP request\n\n**Usage locations:**\n- `models.ts` - Models database refresh\n- `index.js` - Global fetch wrapper (line 821)\n- `provider.ts` - Provider API calls\n- Various tool implementations (webfetch, websearch, auth)\n\n---\n\n### 4. COMPACTION/SUMMARIZATION\n\n**Files involved:**\n- `/session/compaction.ts` - Context overflow detection and compaction execution\n- `/session/summary.ts` - Session and message summarization\n- `/session/system.ts` - System prompt management\n\n**Compaction (Context Management):**\n\nLocated in `/session/compaction.ts`:\n\n**Safety Margin Logic:**\n- `OVERFLOW_SAFETY_MARGIN = 0.85` (default 15% safety margin)\n- `computeSafetyMarginRatio()` function:\n  - If compaction model has larger context than base: ratio = 1.0 (no margin)\n  - If compaction model has same/smaller context: apply configured safety margin\n  - Default 15% margin = ratio 0.85\n\n**Overflow Detection** (`isOverflow()`):\n- Checks: input + cache.read + output tokens against usable context\n- `usableContextWindow = baseModelContextLimit - outputTokenLimit`\n- `safeLimit = usableContextWindow * safetyMarginRatio`\n- Returns true if current tokens > safeLimit\n\n**Pruning** (`prune()`):\n- `PRUNE_MINIMUM = 20_000` - Minimum tokens to prune\n- `PRUNE_PROTECT = 40_000` - Protected threshold\n- Works backwards through messages\n- Erases output of old tool calls (keeps 40k worth)\n- Skips messages with summaries already\n\n**Compaction Process** (`process()`):\n- Creates assistant message with `summary: true` flag\n- Uses compaction model (or base model if not configured)\n- System prompt: `SystemPrompt.summarize(model.providerID)`\n- Filters out errored messages before compression\n- Returns 'continue' or 'stop'\n\n**Context Diagnostics** (`contextDiagnostics()`):\n```typescript\n{\n  contextLimit,\n  outputLimit,\n  usableContext,\n  safeLimit,\n  safetyMargin,\n  currentTokens,\n  headroom,\n  overflow\n}\n```\n\n**Summarization:**\n\nLocated in `/session/summary.ts`:\n\n**Session Summarization** (`summarizeSession()`):\n- Computes file diffs from patch parts\n- Counts additions/deletions\n- Stores in session.summary\n- Publishes diff via Bus\n\n**Message Summarization** (`summarizeMessage()`):\n- **Can be disabled** via `config.summarizeSession`\n- Uses **compaction model** (not base model) to reduce rate-limit pressure (#223)\n- Falls back to base model if compaction model unavailable\n- System prompt: `PROMPT_SUMMARIZE` from `/session/prompt/summarize.txt`\n- Generates text (non-streaming) using `generateText()`\n\n**Summarization Trigger:**\n- Called after assistant message completes\n- Via `SessionSummary.summarize()` in processor\n\n**Configuration:**\n- `--disable-autocompact` - Disables auto-compaction\n- `--disable-prune` - Disables message pruning\n- `--summarize-session` - Enable summarization (default: enabled)\n- Can be disabled via `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_SUMMARIZE_SESSION=false`\n\n---\n\n### 5. CONFIGURATION FILES AND SOURCES\n\n**Configuration Resolution Stack** (from `config/config.ts`):\n\nPriority order (highest to lowest):\n1. **CLI arguments** (yargs parsed)\n2. **Environment variables** (LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_* or via lino-arguments)\n3. **.lenv files** (project-level env override)\n4. **Code defaults**\n\n**Main config interface** (`AgentConfig`):\n```typescript\n{\n  verbose: boolean\n  dryRun: boolean\n  generateTitle: boolean\n  outputResponseModel: boolean\n  summarizeSession: boolean              // Default: true\n  retryOnRateLimits: boolean\n  compactJson: boolean\n  disableAutoupdate: boolean\n  disablePrune: boolean                  // Default: false\n  enableExperimentalModels: boolean\n  disableAutocompact: boolean            // Default: false\n  retryTimeout: number                   // Seconds\n  maxRetryDelay: number                  // Seconds (default: 1200)\n  minRetryInterval: number\n  streamChunkTimeoutMs: number\n  streamStepTimeoutMs: number\n}\n```\n\n**Environment variables:**\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_VERBOSE` - Enable verbose logging\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_DRY_RUN` - Dry run mode\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_SUMMARIZE_SESSION` - Enable summarization (default: true)\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_DISABLE_AUTOCOMPACT` - Disable auto-compaction\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_DISABLE_PRUNE` - Disable pruning\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_COMPACT_JSON` - Compact JSON output\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_RETRY_TIMEOUT` - Retry timeout in seconds (default: 3600)\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_MAX_RETRY_DELAY` - Max retry delay in seconds (default: 1200)\n- `MODELS_DEV_API_JSON` - Override models database path\n\n**Model-specific configuration files:**\n- `/cli/model-config.js` - Parses --model and --compaction-model\n- `/provider/provider.ts` - Provider-specific loaders and options\n- `/session/system.ts` - Provider-specific system prompts\n\n**Local rule files** (`system.ts`, lines 68-76):\n- `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `CONTEXT.md` (deprecated) - Local rules\n- Global: `/~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`, `~/.config/agent/AGENTS.md`\n\n**Models database:**\n- Cached at: `~/.cache/agent/models.json`\n- Source: `https://models.dev/api.json`\n- Refreshes: Every 1 hour or on first run\n- Bundled fallback: `/src/provider/models-macro.ts`\n\n---\n\n### 6. KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS\n\n**1. Event-Driven Architecture (Bus)**\n- Located in `/bus` - Pub/sub event system\n- Used for session events, compaction events, etc.\n\n**2. Session Management**\n- Persisted in storage layer\n- Messages with parts (text, tool calls, reasoning, patches)\n- State tracking (input tokens, output tokens, cache hits)\n\n**3. Lazy Logging**\n- All logs use lazy evaluation to avoid expensive computations\n- JSON format throughout\n- File logging by default (stdout in verbose mode)\n\n**4. Provider Abstraction**\n- Unified interface via `ai` SDK\n- Provider-specific loaders for custom auth (OAuth, API keys)\n- Model resolution with fallback chain\n\n**5. Error Handling**\n- Named errors with structured data\n- Rich suggestions for model not found errors\n- Graceful degradation (e.g., summarization fallback)\n\n**6. Prompt Selection Logic** (`system.ts`):\n- Provider-specific prompts:\n  - Claude models: PROMPT_ANTHROPIC\n  - GPT-5: PROMPT_CODEX\n  - GPT-3.5/4/o1/o3: PROMPT_BEAST\n  - Gemini: PROMPT_GEMINI\n  - Polaris: PROMPT_POLARIS\n  - Grok: PROMPT_GROK_CODE\n  - Default: PROMPT_ANTHROPIC_WITHOUT_TODO\n\n---\n\n### SUMMARY TABLE\n\n| Aspect | Location | Key Details |\n|--------|----------|-------------|\n| **Model Selection** | `/cli/model-config.js`, `/provider/provider.ts` | CLI > yargs, explicit/short format, resolution chain |\n| **Model Defaults** | `/cli/defaults.ts` | opencode/minimax-m2.5-free |\n| **Compaction Model** | `/cli/model-config.js`, `/session/compaction.ts` | opencode/gpt-5-nano default, 15% safety margin |\n| **HTTP Logging** | `/util/verbose-fetch.ts` | Enabled with --verbose, JSON format, error-resilient |\n| **Compaction** | `/session/compaction.ts` | Overflow detection, pruning, context limits |\n| **Summarization** | `/session/summary.ts` | Uses compaction model, configurable, async |\n| **Config Resolution** | `/config/config.ts` | CLI > env > .lenv > defaults |\n| **System Prompts** | `/session/system.ts` | Provider-specific, custom rules support |\n| **Retry Logic** | `/session/retry.ts` | 2s initial, 2x backoff, respects Retry-After |\n\nThis is a sophisticated multi-provider AI agent system with extensive configurability, intelligent model selection with fallbacks, comprehensive logging, automatic context management with compaction, and robust error handling."

System prompt management\n\n**Compaction (Context Management):**\n\nLocated in `/session/compaction.ts`:\n\n**Safety Margin Logic:**\n- `OVERFLOW_SAFETY_MARGIN = 0.85` (default 15% safety margin)\n- `computeSafetyMarginRatio()` function:\n  - If compaction model has larger context than base: ratio = 1.0 (no margin)\n  - If compaction model has same/smaller context: apply configured safety margin\n  - Default 15% margin = ratio 0.85\n\n**Overflow Detection** (`isOverflow()`):\n- Checks: input + cache.read + output tokens against usable context\n- `usableContextWindow = baseModelContextLimit - outputTokenLimit`\n- `safeLimit = usableContextWindow * safetyMarginRatio`\n- Returns true if current tokens > safeLimit\n\n**Pruning** (`prune()`):\n- `PRUNE_MINIMUM = 20_000` - Minimum tokens to prune\n- `PRUNE_PROTECT = 40_000` - Protected threshold\n- Works backwards through messages\n- Erases output of old tool calls (keeps 40k worth)\n- Skips messages with summaries already\n\n**Compaction Process** (`process()`):\n- Creates assistant message with `summary: true` flag\n- Uses compaction model (or base model if not configured)\n- System prompt: `SystemPrompt.summarize(model.providerID)`\n- Filters out errored messages before compression\n- Returns 'continue' or 'stop'\n\n**Context Diagnostics** (`contextDiagnostics()`):\n```typescript\n{\n  contextLimit,\n  outputLimit,\n  usableContext,\n  safeLimit,\n  safetyMargin,\n  currentTokens,\n  headroom,\n  overflow\n}\n```\n\n**Summarization:**\n\nLocated in `/session/summary.ts`:\n\n**Session Summarization** (`summarizeSession()`):\n- Computes file diffs from patch parts\n- Counts additions/deletions\n- Stores in session.summary\n- Publishes diff via Bus\n\n**Message Summarization** (`summarizeMessage()`):\n- **Can be disabled** via `config.summarizeSession`\n- Uses **compaction model** (not base model) to reduce rate-limit pressure (#223)\n- Falls back to base model if compaction model unavailable\n- System prompt: `PROMPT_SUMMARIZE` from `/session/prompt/summarize.txt`\n- Generates text (non-streaming) using `generateText()`\n\n**Summarization Trigger:**\n- Called after assistant message completes\n- Via `SessionSummary.summarize()` in processor\n\n**Configuration:**\n- `--disable-autocompact` - Disables auto-compaction\n- `--disable-prune` - Disables message pruning\n- `--summarize-session` - Enable summarization (default: enabled)\n- Can be disabled via `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_SUMMARIZE_SESSION=false`\n\n---\n\n### 5. CONFIGURATION FILES AND SOURCES\n\n**Configuration Resolution Stack** (from `config/config.ts`):\n\nPriority order (highest to lowest):\n1. **CLI arguments** (yargs parsed)\n2. **Environment variables** (LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_* or via lino-arguments)\n3. **.lenv files** (project-level env override)\n4. **Code defaults**\n\n**Main config interface** (`AgentConfig`):\n```typescript\n{\n  verbose: boolean\n  dryRun: boolean\n  generateTitle: boolean\n  outputResponseModel: boolean\n  summarizeSession: boolean              // Default: true\n  retryOnRateLimits: boolean\n  compactJson: boolean\n  disableAutoupdate: boolean\n  disablePrune: boolean                  // Default: false\n  enableExperimentalModels: boolean\n  disableAutocompact: boolean            // Default: false\n  retryTimeout: number                   // Seconds\n  maxRetryDelay: number                  // Seconds (default: 1200)\n  minRetryInterval: number\n  streamChunkTimeoutMs: number\n  streamStepTimeoutMs: number\n}\n```\n\n**Environment variables:**\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_VERBOSE` - Enable verbose logging\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_DRY_RUN` - Dry run mode\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_SUMMARIZE_SESSION` - Enable summarization (default: true)\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_DISABLE_AUTOCOMPACT` - Disable auto-compaction\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_DISABLE_PRUNE` - Disable pruning\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_COMPACT_JSON` - Compact JSON output\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_RETRY_TIMEOUT` - Retry timeout in seconds (default: 3600)\n- `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_MAX_RETRY_DELAY` - Max retry delay in seconds (default: 1200)\n- `MODELS_DEV_API_JSON` - Override models database path\n\n**Model-specific configuration files:**\n- `/cli/model-config.js` - Parses --model and --compaction-model\n- `/provider/provider.ts` - Provider-specific loaders and options\n- `/session/system.ts` - Provider-specific system prompts\n\n**Local rule files** (`system.ts`, lines 68-76):\n- `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `CONTEXT.md` (deprecated) - Local rules\n- Global: `/~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`, `~/.config/agent/AGENTS.md`\n\n**Models database:**\n- Cached at: `~/.cache/agent/models.json`\n- Source: `https://models.dev/api.json`\n- Refreshes: Every 1 hour or on first run\n- Bundled fallback: `/src/provider/models-macro.ts`\n\n---\n\n### 6. KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS\n\n**1. Event-Driven Architecture (Bus)**\n- Located in `/bus` - Pub/sub event system\n- Used for session events, compaction events, etc.\n\n**2. Session Management**\n- Persisted in storage layer\n- Messages with parts (text, tool calls, reasoning, patches)\n- State tracking (input tokens, output tokens, cache hits)\n\n**3. Lazy Logging**\n- All logs use lazy evaluation to avoid expensive computations\n- JSON format throughout\n- File logging by default (stdout in verbose mode)\n\n**4. Provider Abstraction**\n- Unified interface via `ai` SDK\n- Provider-specific loaders for custom auth (OAuth, API keys)\n- Model resolution with fallback chain\n\n**5. Error Handling**\n- Named errors with structured data\n- Rich suggestions for model not found errors\n- Graceful degradation (e.g., summarization fallback)\n\n**6. Prompt Selection Logic** (`system.ts`):\n- Provider-specific prompts:\n  - Claude models: PROMPT_ANTHROPIC\n  - GPT-5: PROMPT_CODEX\n  - GPT-3.5/4/o1/o3: PROMPT_BEAST\n  - Gemini: PROMPT_GEMINI\n  - Polaris: PROMPT_POLARIS\n  - Grok: PROMPT_GROK_CODE\n  - Default: PROMPT_ANTHROPIC_WITHOUT_TODO\n\n---\n\n### SUMMARY TABLE\n\n| Aspect | Location | Key Details |\n|--------|----------|-------------|\n| **Model Selection** | `/cli/model-config.js`, `/provider/provider.ts` | CLI > yargs, explicit/short format, resolution chain |\n| **Model Defaults** | `/cli/defaults.ts` | opencode/minimax-m2.5-free |\n| **Compaction Model** | `/cli/model-config.js`, `/session/compaction.ts` | opencode/gpt-5-nano default, 15% safety margin |\n| **HTTP Logging** | `/util/verbose-fetch.ts` | Enabled with --verbose, JSON format, error-resilient |\n| **Compaction** | `/session/compaction.ts` | Overflow detection, pruning, context limits |\n| **Summarization** | `/session/summary.ts` | Uses compaction model, configurable, async |\n| **Config Resolution** | `/config/config.ts` | CLI > env > .lenv > defaults |\n| **System Prompts** | `/session/system.ts` | Provider-specific, custom rules support |\n| **Retry Logic** | `/session/retry.ts` | 2s initial, 2x backoff, respects Retry-After |\n\nThis is a sophisticated multi-provider AI agent system with extensive configurability, intelligent model selection with fallbacks, comprehensive logging, automatic context management with compaction, and robust error handling."

Supports \"provider/model\" or short \"model\" format.\n16\t * @param {object} argv - Parsed command line arguments\n17\t * @param {function} outputError - Function to output error messages\n18\t * @param {function} outputStatus - Function to output status messages\n19\t * @returns {Promise<{providerID: string, modelID: string}>}\n20\t */\n21\texport async function parseModelConfig(argv, outputError, outputStatus) {\n22\t  // Safeguard: validate argv.model against process.argv to detect yargs/cache mismatch (#192, #196)\n23\t  // This is critical because yargs under Bun may fail to parse --model correctly,\n24\t  // returning the default value instead of the user's CLI argument.\n25\t  const cliModelArg = getModelFromProcessArgv();\n26\t  let modelArg = argv.model;\n27\t\n28\t  // ALWAYS prefer the CLI value over yargs when available (#196)\n29\t  // The yargs default (DEFAULT_MODEL) can silently override user's --model argument\n30\t  if (cliModelArg) {\n31\t    if (cliModelArg !== modelArg) {\n32\t      Log.Default.warn(() => ({\n33\t        message: 'model argument mismatch detected - using CLI value',\n34\t        yargsModel: modelArg,\n35\t        cliModel: cliModelArg,\n36\t        processArgv: process.argv.join(' '),\n37\t      }));\n38\t    }\n39\t    // Always use CLI value when available, even if it matches yargs\n40\t    // This ensures we use the actual CLI argument, not a cached/default yargs value\n41\t    modelArg = cliModelArg;\n42\t  }\n43\t\n44\t  let providerID;\n45\t  let modelID;\n46\t\n47\t  // Check if model includes explicit provider prefix\n48\t  if (modelArg.includes('/')) {\n49\t    // Explicit provider/model format - respect user's choice\n50\t    const modelParts = modelArg.split('/');\n51\t    providerID = modelParts[0];\n52\t    modelID = modelParts.slice(1).join('/');\n53\t\n54\t    // Validate that providerID and modelID are not empty\n55\t    // Do NOT fall back to defaults - if the user provided an invalid format, fail clearly (#196)\n56\t    if (!providerID || !modelID) {\n57\t      throw new Error(\n58\t        `Invalid model format: \"${modelArg}\". Expected \"provider/model\" format (e.g., \"${DEFAULT_PROVIDER_ID}/${DEFAULT_MODEL_ID}\"). ` +\n59\t          `Provider: \"${providerID || '(empty)'}\", Model: \"${modelID || '(empty)'}\".`\n60\t      );\n61\t    }\n62\t\n63\t    // Log raw and parsed values to help diagnose model routing issues (#171)\n64\t    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n65\t      message: 'using explicit provider/model',\n66\t      rawModel: modelArg,\n67\t      providerID,\n68\t      modelID,\n69\t    }));\n70\t\n71\t    // Validate that the model exists in the provider (#196)\n72\t    // Without this check, a non-existent model silently proceeds and fails at API call time\n73\t    // with confusing \"reason: unknown\" and zero tokens\n74\t    try {\n75\t      const { Provider } = await import('../provider/provider.ts');\n76\t      const s = await Provider.state();\n77\t      const provider = s.providers[providerID];\n78\t      if (provider && !provider.info.models[modelID]) {\n79\t        // Provider exists but model doesn't - warn and suggest alternatives\n80\t        const availableModels = Object.keys(provider.info.models).slice(0, 5);\n81\t        Log.Default.warn(() => ({\n82\t          message:\n83\t            'model not found in provider - the model may still work\n91\t      // This is a best-effort check\n92\t      Log.Default.info(() => ({\n93\t        message: 'skipping model existence validation',\n94\t        reason: validationError?.message,\n95\t      }));\n96\t    }\n97\t  } else {\n98\t    // Short model name - Supports \"provider/model\" or short \"model\" format.\n * @param {object} argv - Parsed command line arguments\n * @param {function} outputError - Function to output error messages\n * @param {function} outputStatus - Function to output status messages\n * @returns {Promise<{providerID: string, modelID: string}>}\n */\nexport async function parseModelConfig(argv, outputError, outputStatus) {\n  // Safeguard: validate argv.model against process.argv to detect yargs/cache mismatch (#192, #196)\n  // This is critical because yargs under Bun may fail to parse --model correctly,\n  // returning the default value instead of the user's CLI argument.\n  const cliModelArg = getModelFromProcessArgv();\n  let modelArg = argv.model;\n\n  // ALWAYS prefer the CLI value over yargs when available (#196)\n  // The yargs default (DEFAULT_MODEL) can silently override user's --model argument\n  if (cliModelArg) {\n    if (cliModelArg !== modelArg) {\n      Log.Default.warn(() => ({\n        message: 'model argument mismatch detected - using CLI value',\n        yargsModel: modelArg,\n        cliModel: cliModelArg,\n        processArgv: process.argv.join(' '),\n      }));\n    }\n    // Always use CLI value when available, even if it matches yargs\n    // This ensures we use the actual CLI argument, not a cached/default yargs value\n    modelArg = cliModelArg;\n  }\n\n  let providerID;\n  let modelID;\n\n  // Check if model includes explicit provider prefix\n  if (modelArg.includes('/')) {\n    // Explicit provider/model format - respect user's choice\n    const modelParts = modelArg.split('/');\n    providerID = modelParts[0];\n    modelID = modelParts.slice(1).join('/');\n\n    // Validate that providerID and modelID are not empty\n    // Do NOT fall back to defaults - if the user provided an invalid format, fail clearly (#196)\n    if (!providerID || !modelID) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `Invalid model format: \"${modelArg}\". Expected \"provider/model\" format (e.g., \"${DEFAULT_PROVIDER_ID}/${DEFAULT_MODEL_ID}\"). ` +\n          `Provider: \"${providerID || '(empty)'}\", Model: \"${modelID || '(empty)'}\".`\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Log raw and parsed values to help diagnose model routing issues (#171)\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'using explicit provider/model',\n      rawModel: modelArg,\n      providerID,\n      modelID,\n    }));\n\n    // Validate that the model exists in the provider (#196)\n    // Without this check, a non-existent model silently proceeds and fails at API call time\n    // with confusing \"reason: unknown\" and zero tokens\n    try {\n      const { Provider } = await import('../provider/provider.ts');\n      const s = await Provider.state();\n      const provider = s.providers[providerID];\n      if (provider && !provider.info.models[modelID]) {\n        // Provider exists but model doesn't - Either authenticate with Claude Code CLI first, or use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)',\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n\n    // Set environment variable for the provider to use\n    process.env.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN = creds.accessToken;\n\n    // If user specified the default model (DEFAULT_MODEL), switch to claude-oauth\n    // If user explicitly specified kilo or another provider, warn but respect their choice\n    if (providerID === DEFAULT_PROVIDER_ID && modelID === DEFAULT_MODEL_ID) {\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n      modelID = 'claude-sonnet-4-5';\n    } else if (!['claude-oauth', 'anthropic'].includes(providerID)) {\n      // If user specified a different provider explicitly, warn them\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'warning',\n          message: `--use-existing-claude-oauth is set but model uses provider \"${providerID}\".         "content": "1\timport { Log } from './log';\n2\timport { isVerbose } from '../config/config';\n3\t\n4\t/**\n5\t * Shared verbose HTTP fetch wrapper.\n6\t *\n7\t * Intercepts fetch() calls and logs request/response details as JSON objects\n8\t * when --verbose mode is enabled. Used across the entire codebase (tools, auth,\n9\t * config, providers) to ensure uniform and predictable HTTP logging.\n10\t *\n11\t * Features:\n12\t * - Logs HTTP request: method, URL, sanitized headers, body preview\n13\t * - Logs HTTP response: status, headers, duration, body preview\n14\t * - Logs HTTP errors: stack trace, error cause chain\n15\t * - Sequential call numbering for correlation\n16\t * - Error-resilient: logging failures never break the actual HTTP request\n17\t * - Runtime verbose check: respects isVerbose() at call time (with env var fallback)\n18\t *\n19\t * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/215\n20\t */\n21\t\n22\tconst log = Log.create({ service: 'http' });\n23\t\n24\t/** Global call counter shared across all verbose fetch wrappers */\n25\tlet globalHttpCallCount = 0;\n26\t\n27\t/**\n28\t * Sanitize HTTP headers by masking sensitive values.\n29\t * Masks authorization, x-api-key, and api-key headers.\n30\t */\n31\texport function sanitizeHeaders(\n32\t  rawHeaders: HeadersInit | Record<string, string> | Headers | undefined\n33\t): Record<string, string> {\n34\t  const sanitized: Record<string, string> = {};\n35\t  if (!rawHeaders) return sanitized;\n36\t\n37\t  const entries =\n38\t    rawHeaders instanceof Headers\n39\t      ? Array.from(rawHeaders.entries())\n40\t      : Array.isArray(rawHeaders)\n41\t        ? rawHeaders\n42\t        : Object.entries(rawHeaders);\n43\t\n44\t  for (const [key, value] of entries) {\n45\t    const lower = key.toLowerCase();\n46\t    if (\n47\t      lower === 'authorization' ||\n48\t      lower === 'x-api-key' ||\n49\t      lower === 'api-key'\n50\t    ) {\n51\t      sanitized[key] =\n52\t        typeof value === 'string' && value.length > 8\n53\t          ? value.slice(0, 4) + '...' + value.slice(-4)\n54\t          : '[REDACTED]';\n55\t    } else {\n56\t      sanitized[key] = String(value);\n57\t    }\n58\t  }\n59\t  return sanitized;\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t/**\n63\t * Create a body preview string, truncated to maxChars.\n64\t */\n65\texport function bodyPreview(\n66\t  body: BodyInit | null | undefined,\n67\t  maxChars = 200000\n68\t): string | undefined {\n69\t  if (!body) return undefined;\n70\t\n71\t  const bodyStr =\n72\t    typeof body === 'string'\n73\t      ? body\n74\t      : body instanceof ArrayBuffer || body instanceof Uint8Array\n75\t        ? `[binary ${(body as ArrayBuffer).byteLength ?? (body as Uint8Array).length} bytes]`\n76\t        : body instanceof URLSearchParams\n77\t          ? body.toString()\n78\t          : undefined;\n79\t\n80\t  if (bodyStr && typeof bodyStr === 'string') {\n81\t    return bodyStr.length > maxChars\n82\t      ? bodyStr.slice(0, maxChars) +\n83\t          `... [truncated, total ${bodyStr.length} chars]`\n84\t      : bodyStr;\n85\t  }\n86\t  return undefined;\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\texport interface VerboseFetchOptions {\n90\t  /** Identifier for the caller (e.g. 'webfetch', 'auth-plugins', 'config') */\n91\t  caller: string;\n92\t  /** Maximum chars for response body preview (default: 200000) */\n93\t  responseBodyMaxChars?: number;\n94\t  /** Maximum chars for request body preview (default: 200000) */\n95\t  requestBodyMaxChars?: number;\n96\t}\n97\t\n98\t/**\n99\t * Wrap a fetch function with verbose HTTP logging.\n100\t *\n101\t * When isVerbose() returns true, logs all HTTP requests and responses\n102\t * as JSON objects. When verbose is false, returns a no-op passthrough.\n103\t *\n104\t * All logging is wrapped in try/catch so it never breaks the actual HTTP request.\n105\t *\n106\t * @param innerFetch - The fetch function to wrap (defaults to global fetch)\n107\t * @param options - Configuration for the wrapper\n108\t * @returns A wrapped fetch function with verbose logging\n109\t */\n110\texport function createVerboseFetch(\n111\t  innerFetch: typeof fetch = fetch,\n112\t  options: VerboseFetchOptions\n113\t): typeof fetch {\n114\t  const {\n115\t    caller,\n116\t    responseBodyMaxChars = 200000,\n117\t    requestBodyMaxChars = 200000,\n118\t  } = options;\n119\t\n120\t  return async (\n121\t    input: RequestInfo | URL,\n122\t    init?: RequestInit\n123\t  ): Promise<Response> => {\n124\t    // Check verbose flag at call time, with env var fallback for resilience.\n125\t    // Uses isVerbose() which checks both the in-memory flag and environment\n126\t    // variables, preventing silent logging loss when the flag state is disrupted.\n127\t    // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/227\n128\t    if (!isVerbose()) {\n129\t      return innerFetch(input, init);\n130\t    }\n131\t\n132\t    globalHttpCallCount++;\n133\t    const callNum = globalHttpCallCount;\n134\t\n135\t    const url =\n136\t      typeof input === 'string'\n137\t        ? input\n138\t        : input instanceof URL\n139\t          ? input.toString()\n140\t          : input.url;\n141\t    const method = init?.method ?? 'GET';\n142\t\n143\t    // Prepare request details for logging (error-resilient)\n144\t    let sanitizedHdrs: Record<string, string> = {};\n145\t    let reqBodyPreview: string | undefined;\n146\t    try {\n147\t      sanitizedHdrs = sanitizeHeaders(init?.headers as HeadersInit | undefined);\n148\t      reqBodyPreview = bodyPreview(init?.body, requestBodyMaxChars);\n149\t    } catch (prepError) {\n150\t      log.warn('verbose logging: failed to prepare request details', {\n151\t        caller,\n152\t        error:\n153\t          prepError instanceof Error ? prepError.message : String(prepError),\n154\t      });\n155\t    }\n156\t\n157\t    // Log request\n158\t    log.info('HTTP request', {\n159\t      caller,\n160\t      callNum,\n161\t      method,\n162\t      url,\n163\t      headers: sanitizedHdrs,\n164\t      bodyPreview: reqBodyPreview,\n165\t    });\n166\t\n167\t    const startMs = Date.now();\n168\t    try {\n169\t      const response = await innerFetch(input, init);\n170\t      const durationMs = Date.now() - startMs;\n171\t\n172\t      // Log response\n173\t      try {\n174\t        log.info('HTTP response', {\n175\t          caller,\n176\t          callNum,\n177\t          method,\n178\t          url,\n179\t          status: response.status,\n180\t          statusText: response.statusText,\n181\t          durationMs,\n182\t          responseHeaders: Object.fromEntries(response.headers.entries()),\n183\t        });\n184\t      } catch {\n185\t        // Ignore logging errors\n186\t      }\n187\t\n188\t      // Log response body\n189\t      const contentType = response.headers.get('content-type') ?? Used across the entire codebase (tools, auth,\n * config, providers) to ensure uniform and predictable HTTP logging.\n *\n * Features:\n * - Logs HTTP request: method, URL, sanitized headers, body preview\n * - Logs HTTP response: status, headers, duration, body preview\n * - Logs HTTP errors: stack trace, error cause chain\n * - Sequential call numbering for correlation\n * - Error-resilient: logging failures never break the actual HTTP request\n * - Runtime verbose check: respects isVerbose() at call time (with env var fallback)\n *\n * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/215\n */\n\nconst log = Log.create({ service: 'http' });\n\n/** Global call counter shared across all verbose fetch wrappers */\nlet globalHttpCallCount = 0;\n\n/**\n * Sanitize HTTP headers by masking sensitive values.\n * Masks authorization, x-api-key, and api-key headers.\n */\nexport function sanitizeHeaders(\n  rawHeaders: HeadersInit | Record<string, string> | Headers | undefined\n): Record<string, string> {\n  const sanitized: Record<string, string> = {};\n  if (!rawHeaders) return sanitized;\n\n  const entries =\n    rawHeaders instanceof Headers\n      ? *\n57\t   * When the compaction model has equal or smaller context, the configured\n58\t   * safety margin applies (default 15% → ratio 0.85).\n59\t   *\n60\t   * @see PRUNE_MINIMUM = 20_000;\n177\t  export const PRUNE_PROTECT = 40_000;\n178\t\n179\t  // goes backwards through parts until there are 40_000 tokens worth of tool\n180\t  // calls. then erases output of previous tool calls. idea is to throw away old\n181\t  // tool calls that are no longer relevant.\n182\t  export async function prune(input: { sessionID: string }) {\n183\t    if (config.disablePrune) return;\n184\t    log.info(() => ({ message: 'pruning' }));\n185\t    const msgs = await Session.messages({ sessionID: input.sessionID });\n186\t    let total = 0;\n187\t    let pruned = 0;\n188\t    const toPrune = [];\n189\t    let turns = 0;\n190\t\n191\t    loop: for (let msgIndex = msgs.length - 1; msgIndex >= 0; msgIndex--) {\n192\t      const msg = msgs[msgIndex];\n193\t      if PRUNE_PROTECT) {\n204\t              pruned += estimate;\n205\t              toPrune.push(part);\n206\t            }\n207\t          }\n208\t      }\n209\t    }\n210\t    log.info(() => ({ message: '../util/fn';\n\nexport namespace SessionCompaction {\n  const log = Log.create({ service: 'session.compaction' });\n\n  export const Event = {\n    Compacted: Bus.event(\n      'session.compacted',\n      z.object({\n        sessionID: z.string(),\n      })\n    ),\n  };\n\n  /**\n   * Default safety margin ratio for compaction trigger.\n   * We trigger compaction at 85% of usable context to avoid hitting hard limits.\n   * This means we stop 15% before (context - output) tokens.\n   * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/217\n   */\n  export const OVERFLOW_SAFETY_MARGIN = 0.85;\n\n  /**\n   * Compaction model configuration passed from CLI.\n   * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/219\n   */\n  export interface CompactionModelConfig {\n    providerID: string;\n    modelID: string;\n    useSameModel: boolean;\n    compactionSafetyMarginPercent: number;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Compute the effective safety margin ratio.\n   *\n   * When the compaction model has a larger context window than the base model,\n   * the entire base model context can be used (ratio = 1.0, i.e. 0% margin),\n   * because the compaction model can ingest all of it.\n   *\n   * When the compaction model has equal or smaller context, the configured\n   * safety margin applies (default 15% → ratio 0.85).\n   *\n   * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/219\n   */\n  export function computeSafetyMarginRatio(input: {\n    baseModelContextLimit: number;\n    compactionModel?: CompactionModelConfig;\n    compactionModelContextLimit?: number;\n  }): number {\n    const compactionModelConfig = input.compactionModel;\n    if (!compactionModelConfig) return OVERFLOW_SAFETY_MARGIN;\n\n    const compactionSafetyMarginPercent =\n      compactionModelConfig.compactionSafetyMarginPercent;\n    const configuredRatio = 1 - compactionSafetyMarginPercent / 100;\n\n    // When using the same model, always apply the configured safety margin\n    if (compactionModelConfig.useSameModel) return configuredRatio;\n\n    // When compaction model has a larger context, no safety margin needed\n    const compactionContextLimit = input.compactionModelContextLimit ?? 0;\n    if (\n      compactionContextLimit > 0 &&\n      compactionContextLimit > input.baseModelContextLimit\n    ) {\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message:\n          'compaction model has larger context — using full base model context',\n        baseModelContextLimit: input.baseModelContextLimit,\n        compactionModelContextLimit: compactionContextLimit,\n      }));\n      return 1.0;\n    }\n\n    return configuredRatio;\n  }\n\n  export function isOverflow(input: {\n    tokens: MessageV2.Assistant['tokens'];\n    model: ModelsDev.Model;\n    compactionModel?: CompactionModelConfig;\n    compactionModelContextLimit?: number;\n  }) {\n    if (config.disableAutocompact) return false;\n    const baseModelContextLimit = input.model.limit.context;\n    if (baseModelContextLimit === 0) return false;\n    const count =\n      input.tokens.input + input.tokens.cache.read + input.tokens.output;\n    const outputTokenLimit =\n      Math.min(input.model.limit.output, SessionPrompt.OUTPUT_TOKEN_MAX) ||\n      SessionPrompt.OUTPUT_TOKEN_MAX;\n    const usableContextWindow = baseModelContextLimit - outputTokenLimit;\n    const safetyMarginRatio = computeSafetyMarginRatio({\n      baseModelContextLimit,\n      compactionModel: input.compactionModel,\n      compactionModelContextLimit: input.compactionModelContextLimit,\n    });\n    const safeLimit = Math.floor(usableContextWindow * safetyMarginRatio);\n    const overflow = count > safeLimit;\n    log.info(() => ({\n      message: 'overflow check',\n      modelID: input.model.id,\n      contextLimit: baseModelContextLimit,\n      outputLimit: outputTokenLimit,\n      usableContextWindow,\n      safeLimit,\n      safetyMarginRatio,\n      compactionModelID: input.compactionModel?.modelID,\n      compactionModelContextLimit: input.compactionModelContextLimit,\n      currentTokens: count,\n      tokensBreakdown: {\n        input: input.tokens.input,\n        cacheRead: input.tokens.cache.read,\n        output: input.tokens.output,\n      },\n      overflow,\n      headroom: safeLimit - count,\n    }));\n    return overflow;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Compute context diagnostics for a given model and token usage.\n   * Used in step-finish parts to show context usage in JSON output.\n   * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/217\n   */\n  export function contextDiagnostics(input: {\n    tokens: { input: number; output: number; cache: { read: number } };\n    model: ModelsDev.Model;\n    compactionModel?: CompactionModelConfig;\n    compactionModelContextLimit?: number;\n  }): MessageV2.ContextDiagnostics | undefined {\n    const contextLimit = input.model.limit.context;\n    if (contextLimit === 0) return undefined;\n    const outputLimit =\n      Math.min(input.model.limit.output, SessionPrompt.OUTPUT_TOKEN_MAX) ||\n      SessionPrompt.OUTPUT_TOKEN_MAX;\n    const usableContext = contextLimit - outputLimit;\n    const safetyMarginRatio = computeSafetyMarginRatio({\n      baseModelContextLimit: contextLimit,\n      compactionModel: input.compactionModel,\n      compactionModelContextLimit: input.compactionModelContextLimit,\n    });\n    const safeLimit = Math.floor(usableContext * safetyMarginRatio);\n    const currentTokens =\n      input.tokens.input + input.tokens.cache.read + input.tokens.output;\n    return {\n      contextLimit,\n      outputLimit,\n      usableContext,\n      safeLimit,\n      safetyMargin: safetyMarginRatio,\n      currentTokens,\n      headroom: safeLimit - currentTokens,\n      overflow: currentTokens > safeLimit,\n    };\n  }\n\n  export const PRUNE_MINIMUM = 20_000;\n  export const PRUNE_PROTECT = 40_000;\n\n  // goes backwards through parts until there are 40_000 tokens worth of tool\n  // calls. then erases output of previous tool calls. idea is to throw away old\n  // tool calls that are no longer relevant.\n  export async function prune(input: { sessionID: string }) {\n    if (config.disablePrune) return;\n    log.info(() => ({ message: 'pruning' }));\n    const msgs = await Session.messages({ sessionID: input.sessionID });\n    let total = 0;\n    let pruned = 0;\n    const toPrune = [];\n    let turns = 0;\n\n    loop: for (let msgIndex = msgs.length - 1; msgIndex >= 0; msgIndex--) {\n      const msg = msgs[msgIndex];\n      if (msg.info.role === 'user') turns++;\n      if (turns < 2) continue;\n      if (msg.info.role === 'assistant' && msg.info.summary) break loop;\n      for (let partIndex = msg.parts.length - 1; partIndex >= 0; partIndex--) {\n        const part = msg.parts[partIndex];\n        if (part.type === 'tool')\n          if (part.state.status === 'completed') {\n            if (part.state.time.compacted) break loop;\n            const estimate = Token.estimate(part.state.output);\n            total += estimate;\n            if (total > PRUNE_PROTECT) {\n              pruned += estimate;\n              toPrune.push(part);\n            }\n          }\n      }\n    }\n    log.info(() => ({ message: 'found', pruned, total }));\n    if (pruned > PRUNE_MINIMUM) {\n      for (const part of toPrune) {\n        if (part.state.status === 'completed') {\n          part.state.time.compacted = Date.now();\n          await Session.updatePart(part);\n        }\n      }\n      log.info(() => ({ message: 'pruned', count: toPrune.length }));\n    }\n  }\n\n  export async function process(input: {\n    parentID: string;\n    messages: MessageV2.WithParts[];\n    sessionID: string;\n    model: {\n      providerID: string;\n      modelID: string;\n    };\n    abort: AbortSignal;\n  }) {\n    log.info(() => ({\n      message: 'compaction process starting',\n      providerID: input.model.providerID,\n      modelID: input.model.modelID,\n      messageCount: input.messages.length,\n      sessionID: input.sessionID,\n    }));\n    const model = await Provider.getModel(\n      input.model.providerID,\n      input.model.modelID\n    );\n    if (isVerbose()) {\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'compaction model loaded',\n        providerID: model.providerID,\n        modelID: model.modelID,\n        npm: model.npm,\n        contextLimit: model.info.limit.context,\n        outputLimit: model.info.limit.output,\n      }));\n    }\n    const system = [...SystemPrompt.summarize(model.providerID)];\n    const msg = (await Session.updateMessage({\n      id: Identifier.ascending('message'),\n      role: 'assistant',\n      parentID: input.parentID,\n      sessionID: input.sessionID,\n      mode: 'build',\n      summary: true,\n      path: {\n        cwd: Instance.directory,\n        root: Instance.worktree,\n      },\n      cost: 0,\n      tokens: {\n        output: 0,\n        input: 0,\n        reasoning: 0,\n        cache: { read: 0, write: 0 },\n      },\n      modelID: input.model.modelID,\n      providerID: model.providerID,\n      time: {\n        created: Date.now(),\n      },\n    })) as MessageV2.Assistant;\n    const processor = SessionProcessor.create({\n      assistantMessage: msg,\n      sessionID: input.sessionID,\n      providerID: input.model.providerID,\n      model: model.info,\n      abort: input.abort,\n    });\n    // Pre-convert messages to ModelMessage format (async in AI SDK 6.0+)\n    const modelMessages = await MessageV2.toModelMessage(\n      input.messages.filter((m) => {\n        if (m.info.role !== 'assistant' || m.info.error === undefined) {\n          return true;\n        }\n        if (\n          MessageV2.AbortedError.isInstance(m.info.error) &&\n          m.parts.some(\n            (part) => part.type !== 'step-start' && part.type !== 'reasoning'\n          )\n        ) {\n          return true;\n        }\n\n        return false;\n      })\n    );\n    // Defensive check: ensure modelMessages is iterable (AI SDK 6.0.1 compatibility fix)\n    const safeModelMessages = Array.isArray(modelMessages) ? modelMessages : [];\n\n    if (isVerbose()) {\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'compaction streamText call',\n        providerID: model.providerID,\n        modelID: model.modelID,\n        systemPromptCount: system.length,\n        modelMessageCount: safeModelMessages.length,\n        filteredMessageCount: input.messages.length - safeModelMessages.length,\n        toolCall: model.info.tool_call,\n      }));\n    }\n\n    const result = await processor.process(() =>\n      streamText({\n        onError(error) {\n          log.error(() => ({ message: 'stream error', error }));\n        },\n        // set to 0, we handle loop\n        maxRetries: 0,\n        providerOptions: ProviderTransform.providerOptions(\n          model.npm,\n          model.providerID,\n          {\n            ...ProviderTransform.options(\n              model.providerID,\n              model.modelID,\n              model.npm ?? '',\n              input.sessionID\n            ),\n            ...model.info.options,\n          }\n        ),\n        headers: model.info.headers,\n        abortSignal: input.abort,\n        tools: model.info.tool_call ? {} : undefined,\n        messages: [\n          ...system.map(\n            (x): ModelMessage => ({\n              role: 'system',\n              content: x,\n            })\n          ),\n          ...safeModelMessages,\n          {\n            role: 'user',\n            content: [\n              {\n                type: 'text',\n                text: \"Provide a detailed but concise summary of our conversation above. Focus on information that would be helpful for continuing the conversation, including what we did, what we're doing, which files we're working on, and what we're going to do next.\",\n              },\n            ],\n          },\n        ],\n        model: wrapLanguageModel({\n          model: model.language,\n          middleware: [\n            {\n              async transformParams(args) {\n                if (args.type === 'stream') {\n                  // @ts-expect-error\n                  args.params.prompt = ProviderTransform.message(\n                    args.params.prompt,\n                    model.providerID,\n                    model.modelID\n                  );\n                }\n                return args.params;\n              },\n            },\n          ],\n        }),\n      })\n    );\n    if (result === 'continue') {\n      const continueMsg = await Session.updateMessage({\n        id: Identifier.ascending('message'),\n        role: 'user',\n The model ID to find alternatives for\n2013\t   * @param failedProviderID - The provider that failed\n2014\t   * @param wasExplicitProvider - Whether the user explicitly specified the provider\n2015\t   * @returns Array of alternative provider IDs that can serve this model\n2016\t   */\n2017\t  export async function getAlternativeProviders(\n2018\t    modelID: string,\n2019\t    failedProviderID: string,\n2020\t    wasExplicitProvider: boolean\n2021\t  ): Promise<string[]> {\n2022\t    // If the user explicitly specified a provider, don't offer alternatives\n2023\t    if (wasExplicitProvider) {\n2024\t      log.info(() => ({\n2025\t        message: 'no alternative providers (explicit provider specified)',\n2026\t        modelID,\n2027\t        failedProviderID,\n2028\t      }));\n2029\t      return [];\n2030\t    }\n2031\t\n2032\t    // Check if this is a shared model\n2033\t    const sharedProviders = SHARED_FREE_MODELS[modelID];\n2034\t    if (!sharedProviders) {\n2035\t      // Not a shared model, no alternatives\n2036\t      return [];\n2037\t    }\n2038\t\n2039\t    // Get alternative providers (excluding the failed one)\n2040\t    const s = await state();\n2041\t    const alternatives = sharedProviders.filter(\n2042\t      (p) => p !== failedProviderID && s.providers[p]\n2043\t    );\n2044\t\n2045\t    if (alternatives.length > 0) {\n2046\t      log.info(() => ({\n2047\t        message: 'found alternative providers for rate-limited model',\n2048\t        modelID,\n2049\t        failedProviderID,\n2050\t        alternatives,\n2051\t      }));\n2052\t    }\n2053\t\n2054\t    return alternatives;\n2055\t  }\n2056\t\n2057\t  /**\n2058\t   * Checks if an error indicates a rate limit issue.\n2059\t   * @param error - The error to check\n2060\t   * @returns true if the error indicates a rate limit\n2061\t   */\n2062\t  export function isRateLimitError(error: unknown): boolean {\n2063\t    if (!(error instanceof Error)) return false;\n2064\t\n2065\t    const message = error.message.toLowerCase();\n2066\t    const name = error.name.toLowerCase();\n2067\t\n2068\t    return (\n2069\t      message.includes('rate limit') ||\n2070\t      message.includes('ratelimit') ||\n2071\t      message.includes('too many requests') ||\n2072\t      message.includes('429') ||\n2073\t      name.includes('ratelimit')\n2074\t    );\n2075\t  }\n2076\t\n2077\t  export const ModelNotFoundError = NamedError.create(\n2078\t    'ProviderModelNotFoundError',\n2079\t    z.object({\n2080\t      providerID: z.string(),\n2081\t      modelID: z.string(),\n2082\t      suggestion: z.string().optional(),\n2083\t    })\n2084\t  );\n2085\t\n2086\t  export const InitError = NamedError.create(\n2087\t    'ProviderInitError',\n2088\t    z.object({\n2089\t      providerID: z.string(),\n2090\t    })\n2091\t  );\n2092\t}\n2093\t"
