      "content": "  export async function resolveShortModelName(\n    modelID: string\n  ): Promise<{ providerID: string; modelID: string } | undefined> {\n    const s = await state();\n\n    // Define model-to-provider mappings for free models\n    // Models unique to Kilo (GLM models from Z.AI are only free on Kilo)\n    const kiloUniqueModels = [\n      'glm-5-free',\n      'glm-4.5-air-free',\n      'minimax-m2.5-free',\n      'giga-potato-free',\n      'trinity-large-preview',\n      'deepseek-r1-free',\n    ];\n\n    // Check if it's a Kilo-unique model\n    if (kiloUniqueModels.includes(modelID)) {\n      const kiloProvider = s.providers['kilo'];\n      if (kiloProvider && kiloProvider.info.models[modelID]) {\n        log.info(() => ({\n          message: 'resolved short model name to kilo (unique)',\n          modelID,\n        }));\n        return { providerID: 'kilo', modelID };\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check if model exists in any provider\n    const matchingProviders: string[] = [];\n    for (const [providerID, provider] of Object.entries(s.providers)) {\n      if (provider.info.models[modelID]) {\n        matchingProviders.push(providerID);\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (matchingProviders.length === 0) {\n      return undefined;\n    }\n\n    if (matchingProviders.length === 1) {\n      const providerID = matchingProviders[0];\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'resolved short model name (single match)',\n        modelID,\n        providerID,\n      }));\n      return { providerID, modelID };\n    }\n\n    // Multiple providers have this model - prefer OpenCode for shared free models\n    // This follows the convention that opencode is the primary free provider\n    if (matchingProviders.includes('opencode')) {\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'resolved short model name to opencode (multiple providers)',\n        modelID,\n        availableProviders: matchingProviders,\n      }));\n      return { providerID: 'opencode', modelID };\n    }\n\n    // Fallback to first matching provider\n    const providerID = matchingProviders[0];\n    log.info(() => ({\n      message: 'resolved short model name (fallback)',\n      modelID,\n      providerID,\n      availableProviders: matchingProviders,\n    }));\n    return { providerID, modelID };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Parse a model string that may or may not include a provider prefix.\n   * If no provider is specified, attempts to resolve the short model name to the appropriate provider.\n   *\n   * IMPORTANT: If the model cannot be resolved, this function throws an error instead of\n   * silently falling back to a default. This ensures the user gets their requested model\n   * or a clear error explaining why it's not available.\n   *\n   * Examples:\n   * - \"kilo/glm-5-free\" -> { providerID: \"kilo\", modelID: \"glm-5-free\" }\n   * - \"glm-5-free\" -> { providerID: \"kilo\", modelID: \"glm-5-free\" } (resolved)\n   * - \"big-pickle\" -> { providerID: \"opencode\", modelID: \"big-pickle\" } (resolved)\n   * - \"nonexistent-model\" -> throws ModelNotFoundError\n   *\n   * @param model - Model string with or without provider prefix\n   * @returns Parsed provider ID and model ID\n   * @throws ModelNotFoundError if the model cannot be found in any provider\n   * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/194\n   */\n  export async function parseModelWithResolution(\n    model: string\n  ): Promise<{ providerID: string; modelID: string }> {\n    // Check if model includes a provider prefix\n    if (model.includes('/')) {\n      // Explicit provider specified - use it directly\n      return parseModel(model);\n    }\n\n    // No provider prefix - try to resolve the short model name\n    const resolved = await resolveShortModelName(model);\n    if (resolved) {\n      return resolved;\n    }\n\n    // Unable to resolve - fail with a clear error instead of silently falling back\n    // This prevents the issue where user requests model X but gets model Y\n    // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/194\n    const s = await state();\n    const availableModels: string[] = [];\n\n    // Collect some available models to suggest\n    for (const [providerID, provider] of Object.entries(s.providers)) {\n      for (const modelID of Object.keys(provider.info.models).slice(0, 3)) {\n        availableModels.push(`${providerID}/${modelID}`);\n      }\n      if (availableModels.length >= 6) break;\n    }\n\n    const suggestion =\n      availableModels.length > 0\n        ? `Model \"${model}\" not found in any provider. Available models include: ${availableModels.join(', ')}. Use --model provider/model-id format for explicit selection.`\n        : `Model \"${model}\" not found. No providers are currently available. Check your API keys or authentication.`;\n\n    log.error(() => ({\n      message: 'model not found - refusing to silently fallback',\n      requestedModel: model,\n      availableModels,\n    }));\n\n    throw new ModelNotFoundError({\n      providerID: 'unknown',\n      modelID: model,\n      suggestion,\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Defines models that are available in multiple free providers.\n   * When one provider hits rate limits, the system can try an alternative.\n   *\n   * Note: This is only used for models without explicit provider specification.\n   * If user specifies \"kilo/deepseek-r1-free\", no fallback will occur.\n   */\n  const SHARED_FREE_MODELS: Record<string, string[]> = {\n    // Currently no shared models between OpenCode and Kilo providers.\n    // Kilo models use different IDs than OpenCode models.\n  };\n\n  /**\n   * Get alternative providers for a model when the primary provider fails (e.g., rate limited).\n   * This function returns a list of alternative providers that offer the same model.\n   *\n   * Note: This only returns alternatives for models without explicit provider specification.\n   * If the original request had an explicit provider (like \"kilo/deepseek-r1-free\"), this returns empty array.\n   *\n   * @param modelID - The model ID to find alternatives for\n   * @param failedProviderID - The provider that failed\n   * @param wasExplicitProvider - Whether the user explicitly specified the provider\n   * @returns Array of alternative provider IDs that can serve this model\n   */\n  export async function getAlternativeProviders(\n    modelID: string,\n    failedProviderID: string,\n    wasExplicitProvider: boolean\n  ): Promise<string[]> {\n    // If the user explicitly specified a provider, don't offer alternatives\n    if (wasExplicitProvider) {\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'no alternative providers (explicit provider specified)',\n        modelID,\n        failedProviderID,\n      }));\n      return [];\n    }\n\n    // Check if this is a shared model\n    const sharedProviders = SHARED_FREE_MODELS[modelID];\n    if (!sharedProviders) {\n      // Not a shared model, no alternatives\n      return [];\n    }\n\n    // Get alternative providers (excluding the failed one)\n    const s = await state();\n    const alternatives = sharedProviders.filter(\n      (p) => p !== failedProviderID && s.providers[p]\n    );\n\n    if (alternatives.length > 0) {\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'found alternative providers for rate-limited model',\n        modelID,\n        failedProviderID,\n        alternatives,\n      }));\n    }\n\n    return alternatives;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Checks if an error indicates a rate limit issue.\n   * @param error - The error to check\n   * @returns true if the error indicates a rate limit\n   */\n  export function isRateLimitError(error: unknown): boolean {\n    if (!(error instanceof Error)) return false;\n\n    const message = error.message.toLowerCase();\n    const name = error.name.toLowerCase();\n\n    return (\n      message.includes('rate limit') ||\n      message.includes('ratelimit') ||\n      message.includes('too many requests') ||\n      message.includes('429') ||\n      name.includes('ratelimit')\n    );\n  }\n\n  export const ModelNotFoundError = NamedError.create(\n    'ProviderModelNotFoundError',\n    z.object({\n      providerID: z.string(),\n      modelID: z.string(),\n      suggestion: z.string().optional(),\n    })\n  );\n\n  export const InitError = NamedError.create(\n    'ProviderInitError',\n    z.object({\n      providerID: z.string(),\n    })\n  );\n}\n",

      "totalLines": 2093
    }

[2026-04-07T13:21:59.127Z] [INFO] {
  "type": "assistant",
  "message": {
    "model": "claude-opus-4-6",
    "id": "msg_01862et4vFhexSufsQW6fJdP",
    "type": "message",
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "tool_use",
        "id": "toolu_01YXDzCDTSco6kRFnjJa2xy8",
        "name": "Write",
        "input": {
          "file_path": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1775567743025/docs/case-studies/issue-231/README.md",
          "content": "# Case Study: Issue #231 — Silent Model Fallback and Missing Compaction Logs\n\n## Summary\n\nAnalysis of execution log from a run where the user requested `kimi-k2.5-free` model but the agent silently used `minimax-m2.5-free` instead. Additionally, HTTP responses for compaction/summarization were not logged.\n\n## Timeline of Events\n\n| Time (UTC) | Event |\n|------------|-------|\n| 12:32:19 | Outer solver starts with `--model kimi-k2.5-free` |\n| 12:33:05 | Agent launched with `--model opencode/kimi-k2.5-free` |\n| 12:33:06 | Agent resolves model as `minimax-m2.5-free` (WRONG — silent substitution) |\n| 12:33:06 | Storage migration failure logged as error (swallowed) |\n| 12:33:06 | `[verbose] HTTP logging active for provider: opencode` written to stderr |\n| 12:33:06 | Compaction model set to `gpt-5-nano` |\n| 12:33:06–12:37:08 | Agent operates using `minimax-m2.5-free` (~4 minutes) |\n| 12:33:44 | 500 error from OpenCode `/responses` endpoint (`input_tokens` undefined) |\n| 12:33:52 | Second 500 error from OpenCode `/responses` endpoint |\n| 12:37:08 | Last compaction request sent — no response received (process exits) |\n| 12:37:09 | Outer solver reports false error detection from verbose log message |\n\n## Root Causes\n\n### Problem 1: Silent Model Substitution (CRITICAL)\n\n**What happened:** User requested `kimi-k2.5-free`, which was expanded to `opencode/kimi-k2.5-free` by the outer solver. The agent's model validation in `model-config.js:78-88` found the model doesn't exist in the opencode provider but only **warned** with the message \"model not found in provider — will attempt anyway.\" Then `provider.ts:1625-1654` created a **fallback minimal model info** for the missing model. The OpenCode API then silently routed the request to `minimax-m2.5-free` (the default model).\n\n**Root cause:** Two layers of permissive fallback:\n1. `model-config.js:78-88` — warns instead of failing when explicit `provider/model` has unknown model\n2. `provider.ts:1625-1654` — creates synthetic model info for unknown models, allowing SDK calls to proceed\n\n**Fix:** When a user explicitly specifies `provider/model` format and the model is not found in the provider catalog (even after cache refresh), throw an error immediately instead of warning and proceeding.\n\n### Problem 2: Missing HTTP Response for Last Compaction Request\n\n**What happened:** The last compaction POST to `/responses` at 12:37:08.713Z was sent 21ms before the agent exited at 12:37:08.734Z. The streaming response logging runs asynchronously (`verbose-fetch.ts:200-229`), and the process terminated before the response could be received/logged.\n\n**Root cause:** The async stream logging in `verbose-fetch.ts` runs as a fire-and-forget IIFE (`(async () => { ... })()`). There's no mechanism to wait for pending log operations before process exit.\n\n**Fix:** This is a systemic issue — the compaction itself may have been interrupted by process exit. The fix should ensure the process doesn't exit while compaction is in progress. For logging specifically, we can track pending log operations and warn at exit if any are incomplete.\n\n### Problem 3: Verbose Log Message Misinterpreted as Error\n\n**What happened:** `provider.ts:1252-1254` writes `[verbose] HTTP logging active for provider: opencode` to stderr. The outer solver scans stderr for error-like patterns and flagged this as an error, setting `errorDetectedInOutput: true` even though the agent exited with code 0.\n\n**Root cause:** Writing diagnostic messages to stderr. While stderr is commonly used for diagnostic output, the outer solver's error detection is too broad.\n\n**Fix:** Change the stderr message format to avoid triggering error detection. Prefix with a clearly non-error marker.\n\n### Problem 4: OpenCode API 500 Errors (External)\n\n**What happened:** Two 500 Internal Server Errors from the OpenCode `/responses` endpoint for `gpt-5-nano` compaction. Error: `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'input_tokens')`.\n\n**Root cause:** Server-side bug in OpenCode API's usage tracking where `input_tokens` is undefined. This is external to this codebase.\n\n**Action:** Report to https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues\n\n### Problem 5: Storage Migration Failure Silently Swallowed\n\n**What happened:** `storage.ts:183-184` catches migration errors and only logs them, then continues execution. The migration counter is incremented even on failure.\n\n**Root cause:** `.catch(() => log.error(...))` swallows the error. The actual error details (message, stack) are not logged, making diagnosis impossible.\n\n**Fix:** Log the actual error details. Consider whether migration failures should be fatal or at least include the error message.\n\n## Affected Files\n\n- `js/src/cli/model-config.js` — Model validation (Problem 1)\n- `js/src/provider/provider.ts` — Model resolution fallback (Problem 1), verbose stderr (Problem 3)\n- `js/src/util/verbose-fetch.ts` — Async response logging (Problem 2)\n- `js/src/storage/storage.ts` — Migration error handling (Problem 5)\n\n## References\n\n- Execution log: `./execution-log.txt` (55,320 lines)\n- Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/231\n"

  "type": "user",
  "message": {
    "role": "user",
    "content": [
      {
        "tool_use_id": "toolu_01YXDzCDTSco6kRFnjJa2xy8",
        "type": "tool_result",
        "content": "File created successfully at: /tmp/gh-issue-solver-1775567743025/docs/case-studies/issue-231/README.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"
      }
    ]
  },
  "parent_tool_use_id": null,
  "session_id": "63230e8b-f514-401d-af90-199d99ff8078",
  "uuid": "0a084edf-3591-432c-9abb-99a2b1fd9526",
  "timestamp": "2026-04-07T13:21:59.176Z",
  "tool_use_result": {
    "type": "create",
    "filePath": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1775567743025/docs/case-studies/issue-231/README.md",
    "content": "# Case Study: Issue #231 — Silent Model Fallback and Missing Compaction Logs\n\n## Summary\n\nAnalysis of execution log from a run where the user requested `kimi-k2.5-free` model but the agent silently used `minimax-m2.5-free` instead. Additionally, HTTP responses for compaction/summarization were not logged.\n\n## Timeline of Events\n\n| Time (UTC) | Event |\n|------------|-------|\n| 12:32:19 | Outer solver starts with `--model kimi-k2.5-free` |\n| 12:33:05 | Agent launched with `--model opencode/kimi-k2.5-free` |\n| 12:33:06 | Agent resolves model as `minimax-m2.5-free` (WRONG — silent substitution) |\n| 12:33:06 | Storage migration failure logged as error (swallowed) |\n| 12:33:06 | `[verbose] HTTP logging active for provider: opencode` written to stderr |\n| 12:33:06 | Compaction model set to `gpt-5-nano` |\n| 12:33:06–12:37:08 | Agent operates using `minimax-m2.5-free` (~4 minutes) |\n| 12:33:44 | 500 error from OpenCode `/responses` endpoint (`input_tokens` undefined) |\n| 12:33:52 | Second 500 error from OpenCode `/responses` endpoint |\n| 12:37:08 | Last compaction request sent — no response received (process exits) |\n| 12:37:09 | Outer solver reports false error detection from verbose log message |\n\n## Root Causes\n\n### Problem 1: Silent Model Substitution (CRITICAL)\n\n**What happened:** User requested `kimi-k2.5-free`, which was expanded to `opencode/kimi-k2.5-free` by the outer solver. The agent's model validation in `model-config.js:78-88` found the model doesn't exist in the opencode provider but only **warned** with the message \"model not found in provider — will attempt anyway.\" Then `provider.ts:1625-1654` created a **fallback minimal model info** for the missing model. The OpenCode API then silently routed the request to `minimax-m2.5-free` (the default model).\n\n**Root cause:** Two layers of permissive fallback:\n1. `model-config.js:78-88` — warns instead of failing when explicit `provider/model` has unknown model\n2. `provider.ts:1625-1654` — creates synthetic model info for unknown models, allowing SDK calls to proceed\n\n**Fix:** When a user explicitly specifies `provider/model` format and the model is not found in the provider catalog (even after cache refresh), throw an error immediately instead of warning and proceeding.\n\n### Problem 2: Missing HTTP Response for Last Compaction Request\n\n**What happened:** The last compaction POST to `/responses` at 12:37:08.713Z was sent 21ms before the agent exited at 12:37:08.734Z. The streaming response logging runs asynchronously (`verbose-fetch.ts:200-229`), and the process terminated before the response could be received/logged.\n\n**Root cause:** The async stream logging in `verbose-fetch.ts` runs as a fire-and-forget IIFE (`(async () => { ... })()`). There's no mechanism to wait for pending log operations before process exit.\n\n**Fix:** This is a systemic issue — the compaction itself may have been interrupted by process exit. The fix should ensure the process doesn't exit while compaction is in progress. For logging specifically, we can track pending log operations and warn at exit if any are incomplete.\n\n### Problem 3: Verbose Log Message Misinterpreted as Error\n\n**What happened:** `provider.ts:1252-1254` writes `[verbose] HTTP logging active for provider: opencode` to stderr. The outer solver scans stderr for error-like patterns and flagged this as an error, setting `errorDetectedInOutput: true` even though the agent exited with code 0.\n\n**Root cause:** Writing diagnostic messages to stderr. While stderr is commonly used for diagnostic output, the outer solver's error detection is too broad.\n\n**Fix:** Change the stderr message format to avoid triggering error detection. Prefix with a clearly non-error marker.\n\n### Problem 4: OpenCode API 500 Errors (External)\n\n**What happened:** Two 500 Internal Server Errors from the OpenCode `/responses` endpoint for `gpt-5-nano` compaction. Error: `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'input_tokens')`.\n\n**Root cause:** Server-side bug in OpenCode API's usage tracking where `input_tokens` is undefined. This is external to this codebase.\n\n**Action:** Report to https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues\n\n### Problem 5: Storage Migration Failure Silently Swallowed\n\n**What happened:** `storage.ts:183-184` catches migration errors and only logs them, then continues execution. The migration counter is incremented even on failure.\n\n**Root cause:** `.catch(() => log.error(...))` swallows the error. The actual error details (message, stack) are not logged, making diagnosis impossible.\n\n**Fix:** Log the actual error details. Consider whether migration failures should be fatal or at least include the error message.\n\n## Affected Files\n\n- `js/src/cli/model-config.js` — Model validation (Problem 1)\n- `js/src/provider/provider.ts` — Model resolution fallback (Problem 1), verbose stderr (Problem 3)\n- `js/src/util/verbose-fetch.ts` — Async response logging (Problem 2)\n- `js/src/storage/storage.ts` — Migration error handling (Problem 5)\n\n## References\n\n- Execution log: `./execution-log.txt` (55,320 lines)\n- Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/231\n",
    "structuredPatch": [],
    "originalFile": null
  }
}

    "originalFile": "import {\n  getModelFromProcessArgv,\n  getCompactionModelFromProcessArgv,\n  getCompactionSafetyMarginFromProcessArgv,\n} from './argv.ts';\nimport { Log } from '../util/log.ts';\nimport {\n  DEFAULT_PROVIDER_ID,\n  DEFAULT_MODEL_ID,\n  DEFAULT_COMPACTION_MODEL,\n  DEFAULT_COMPACTION_SAFETY_MARGIN_PERCENT,\n} from './defaults.ts';\n\n/**\n * Parse model config from argv. Supports \"provider/model\" or short \"model\" format.\n * @param {object} argv - Parsed command line arguments\n * @param {function} outputError - Function to output error messages\n * @param {function} outputStatus - Function to output status messages\n * @returns {Promise<{providerID: string, modelID: string}>}\n */\nexport async function parseModelConfig(argv, outputError, outputStatus) {\n  // Safeguard: validate argv.model against process.argv to detect yargs/cache mismatch (#192, #196)\n  // This is critical because yargs under Bun may fail to parse --model correctly,\n  // returning the default value instead of the user's CLI argument.\n  const cliModelArg = getModelFromProcessArgv();\n  let modelArg = argv.model;\n\n  // ALWAYS prefer the CLI value over yargs when available (#196)\n  // The yargs default (DEFAULT_MODEL) can silently override user's --model argument\n  if (cliModelArg) {\n    if (cliModelArg !== modelArg) {\n      Log.Default.warn(() => ({\n        message: 'model argument mismatch detected - using CLI value',\n        yargsModel: modelArg,\n        cliModel: cliModelArg,\n        processArgv: process.argv.join(' '),\n      }));\n    }\n    // Always use CLI value when available, even if it matches yargs\n    // This ensures we use the actual CLI argument, not a cached/default yargs value\n    modelArg = cliModelArg;\n  }\n\n  let providerID;\n  let modelID;\n\n  // Check if model includes explicit provider prefix\n  if (modelArg.includes('/')) {\n    // Explicit provider/model format - respect user's choice\n    const modelParts = modelArg.split('/');\n    providerID = modelParts[0];\n    modelID = modelParts.slice(1).join('/');\n\n    // Validate that providerID and modelID are not empty\n    // Do NOT fall back to defaults - if the user provided an invalid format, fail clearly (#196)\n    if (!providerID || !modelID) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `Invalid model format: \"${modelArg}\". Expected \"provider/model\" format (e.g., \"${DEFAULT_PROVIDER_ID}/${DEFAULT_MODEL_ID}\"). ` +\n          `Provider: \"${providerID || '(empty)'}\", Model: \"${modelID || '(empty)'}\".`\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Log raw and parsed values to help diagnose model routing issues (#171)\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'using explicit provider/model',\n      rawModel: modelArg,\n      providerID,\n      modelID,\n    }));\n\n    // Validate that the model exists in the provider (#196)\n    // Without this check, a non-existent model silently proceeds and fails at API call time\n    // with confusing \"reason: unknown\" and zero tokens\n    try {\n      const { Provider } = await import('../provider/provider.ts');\n      const s = await Provider.state();\n      const provider = s.providers[providerID];\n      if (provider && !provider.info.models[modelID]) {\n        // Provider exists but model doesn't - warn and suggest alternatives\n        const availableModels = Object.keys(provider.info.models).slice(0, 5);\n        Log.Default.warn(() => ({\n          message:\n            'model not found in provider - will attempt anyway (provider may support unlisted models)',\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          availableModels,\n        }));\n      }\n    } catch (validationError) {\n      // Don't fail on validation errors - the model may still work\n      // This is a best-effort check\n      Log.Default.info(() => ({\n        message: 'skipping model existence validation',\n        reason: validationError?.message,\n      }));\n    }\n  } else {\n    // Short model name - resolve to appropriate provider\n    // Import Provider to use parseModelWithResolution\n    const { Provider } = await import('../provider/provider.ts');\n    const resolved = await Provider.parseModelWithResolution(modelArg);\n    providerID = resolved.providerID;\n    modelID = resolved.modelID;\n\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'resolved short model name',\n      input: modelArg,\n      providerID,\n      modelID,\n    }));\n  }\n\n  // Parse compaction model (#219)\n  const compactionModelResult = await parseCompactionModelConfig(\n    argv,\n    providerID,\n    modelID\n  );\n\n  // Handle --use-existing-claude-oauth option\n  // This reads OAuth credentials from ~/.claude/.credentials.json (Claude Code CLI)\n  // For new authentication, use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)\n  if (argv['use-existing-claude-oauth']) {\n    // Import ClaudeOAuth to check for credentials from Claude Code CLI\n    const { ClaudeOAuth } = await import('../auth/claude-oauth.ts');\n    const creds = await ClaudeOAuth.getCredentials();\n\n    if (!creds?.accessToken) {\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputError(\n        {\n          errorType: 'AuthenticationError',\n          message:\n            'No Claude OAuth credentials found in ~/.claude/.credentials.json. Either authenticate with Claude Code CLI first, or use: agent auth login (select Anthropic > Claude Pro/Max)',\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n\n    // Set environment variable for the provider to use\n    process.env.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN = creds.accessToken;\n\n    // If user specified the default model (DEFAULT_MODEL), switch to claude-oauth\n    // If user explicitly specified kilo or another provider, warn but respect their choice\n    if (providerID === DEFAULT_PROVIDER_ID && modelID === DEFAULT_MODEL_ID) {\n      providerID = 'claude-oauth';\n      modelID = 'claude-sonnet-4-5';\n    } else if (!['claude-oauth', 'anthropic'].includes(providerID)) {\n      // If user specified a different provider explicitly, warn them\n      const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n      outputStatus(\n        {\n          type: 'warning',\n          message: `--use-existing-claude-oauth is set but model uses provider \"${providerID}\". Using specified provider.`,\n        },\n        compactJson\n      );\n      // Don't override - respect user's explicit provider choice\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { providerID, modelID, compactionModel: compactionModelResult };\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse compaction model config from argv.\n * Resolves --compaction-model and --compaction-safety-margin CLI arguments.\n * The special value \"same\" means use the base model for compaction.\n * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/219\n */\nasync function parseCompactionModelConfig(argv, baseProviderID, baseModelID) {\n  // Get compaction model from CLI (safeguard against yargs caching)\n  const cliCompactionModelArg = getCompactionModelFromProcessArgv();\n  const compactionModelArg =\n    cliCompactionModelArg ??\n    argv['compaction-model'] ??\n    DEFAULT_COMPACTION_MODEL;\n\n  // Get safety margin from CLI\n  const cliSafetyMarginArg = getCompactionSafetyMarginFromProcessArgv();\n  const compactionSafetyMarginPercent = cliSafetyMarginArg\n    ? parseInt(cliSafetyMarginArg, 10)\n    : (argv['compaction-safety-margin'] ??\n      DEFAULT_COMPACTION_SAFETY_MARGIN_PERCENT);\n\n  // Special \"same\" alias — use the base model for compaction\n  const useSameModel = compactionModelArg.toLowerCase() === 'same';\n\n  let compactionProviderID;\n  let compactionModelID;\n\n  if (useSameModel) {\n    compactionProviderID = baseProviderID;\n    compactionModelID = baseModelID;\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message:\n        'compaction model set to \"same\" — using base model for compaction',\n      compactionProviderID,\n      compactionModelID,\n    }));\n  } else if (compactionModelArg.includes('/')) {\n    const parts = compactionModelArg.split('/');\n    compactionProviderID = parts[0];\n    compactionModelID = parts.slice(1).join('/');\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'using explicit compaction model',\n      compactionProviderID,\n      compactionModelID,\n    }));\n  } else {\n    // Short name resolution\n    const { Provider } = await import('../provider/provider.ts');\n    const resolved =\n      await Provider.parseModelWithResolution(compactionModelArg);\n    compactionProviderID = resolved.providerID;\n    compactionModelID = resolved.modelID;\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'resolved short compaction model name',\n      input: compactionModelArg,\n      compactionProviderID,\n      compactionModelID,\n    }));\n  }\n\n  return {\n    providerID: compactionProviderID,\n    modelID: compactionModelID,\n    useSameModel,\n    compactionSafetyMarginPercent,\n  };\n}\n",

Environment variable: CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN\n     * 2. Credentials file: ~/.claude/.credentials.json (from Claude Code CLI or our auth command)\n     *\n     * OAuth tokens use Bearer authentication (Authorization header)\n     * instead of x-api-key authentication used by standard API keys.\n     *\n     * To authenticate, run: agent auth claude\n     */\n    'claude-oauth': async (input) => {\n      // Check for OAuth token from environment variable first\n      let oauthToken = process.env['CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN'];\n      let tokenSource = 'environment';\n\n      if (!oauthToken) {\n        // Check for OAuth credentials from credentials file\n        const claudeCreds = await ClaudeOAuth.getCredentials();\n        if (claudeCreds) {\n          oauthToken = claudeCreds.accessToken;\n          tokenSource = `credentials file (${claudeCreds.subscriptionType ?? 'unknown'})`;\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (!oauthToken) {\n        return { autoload: false };\n      }\n\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'using claude oauth credentials',\n        source: tokenSource,\n      }));\n\n      // Create authenticated fetch with Bearer token and OAuth beta header\n      const customFetch = ClaudeOAuth.createAuthenticatedFetch(oauthToken);\n\n      return {\n        autoload: true,\n        options: {\n          // Use a placeholder key to satisfy the SDK's API key requirement\n          // The actual authentication is done via Bearer token in customFetch\n          apiKey: 'oauth-token-placeholder',\n          fetch: customFetch,\n          headers: {\n            'anthropic-beta':\n              'oauth-2025-04-20,claude-code-20250219,interleaved-thinking-2025-05-14,fine-grained-tool-streaming-2025-05-14',\n          },\n        },\n      };\n    },\n    /**\n     * Echo provider - synthetic provider for dry-run testing\n     * Echoes back the user's input without making actual API calls.\n     *\n     * This provider is automatically enabled when --dry-run mode is active.\n     * It can also be used explicitly with: --model link-assistant/echo\n     *\n     * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/89\n     */\n    'link-assistant': async () => {\n      // Echo provider is always available - no external dependencies needed\n      return {\n        autoload: config.dryRun, // Auto-load only in dry-run mode\n        async getModel(_sdk: any, modelID: string) {\n          // Return our custom echo model that implements LanguageModelV1\n          return createEchoModel(modelID);\n        },\n        options: {},\n      };\n    },\n    /**\n     * Cache provider - synthetic provider for caching API responses\n     * Caches responses using links notation for deterministic testing.\n     *\n     * This provider caches API responses and falls back to echo behavior.\n     * It can be used explicitly with: --model link-assistant/cache/opencode/grok-code\n     *\n     * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/89\n     */\n    'link-assistant/cache': async () => {\n      // Cache provider is always available - no external dependencies needed\n      return {\n        autoload: false, // Not auto-loaded\n        async getModel(_sdk: any, modelID: string) {\n          // modelID should be in format \"provider/model\" like \"opencode/grok-code\"\n          const parts = modelID.split('/');\n          if (parts.length < 2) {\n            throw new Error(\n              `Invalid cache model ID: ${modelID}. Expected format: provider/model`\n            );\n          }\n          const [providerId, ...modelParts] = parts;\n          const actualModelId = modelParts.join('/');\n\n          // Return our custom cache model that implements LanguageModelV1\n          return createCacheModel(providerId, actualModelId);\n        },\n        options: {},\n      };\n    },\n  };\n\n  export const state = Instance.state(async () => {\n    using _ = log.time('state');\n    const config = await Config.get();\n    const database = await ModelsDev.get();\n\n    const providers: {\n      [providerID: string]: {\n        source: Source;\n        info: ModelsDev.Provider;\n        getModel?: (\n          sdk: any,\n          modelID: string,\n          options?: Record<string, any>\n        ) => Promise<any>;\n        options: Record<string, any>;\n      };\n    } = {};\n    const models = new Map<\n      string,\n      {\n        providerID: string;\n        modelID: string;\n        info: ModelsDev.Model;\n        language: LanguageModel;\n        npm?: string;\n      }\n    >();\n    const sdk = new Map<number, SDK>();\n    // Maps `${provider}/${key}` to the provider’s actual model ID for custom aliases.\n    const realIdByKey = new Map<string, string>();\n\n    log.info(() => ({ message: 'init' }));\n\n    function mergeProvider(\n      id: string,\n      options: Record<string, any>,\n      source: Source,\n      getModel?: (\n        sdk: any,\n        modelID: string,\n        options?: Record<string, any>\n      ) => Promise<any>\n    ) {\n      const provider = providers[id];\n      if (!provider) {\n        const info = database[id];\n        if (!info) return;\n        if (info.api && !options['baseURL']) options['baseURL'] = info.api;\n        providers[id] = {\n          source,\n          info,\n          options,\n          getModel,\n        };\n        return;\n      }\n      provider.options = mergeDeep(provider.options, options);\n      provider.source = source;\n      provider.getModel = getModel ?? provider.getModel;\n    }\n\n    const configProviders = Object.entries(config.provider ?? {});\n\n    // Add GitHub Copilot Enterprise provider that inherits from GitHub Copilot\n    if (database['github-copilot']) {\n      const githubCopilot = database['github-copilot'];\n      database['github-copilot-enterprise'] = {\n        ...githubCopilot,\n        id: 'github-copilot-enterprise',\n        name: 'GitHub Copilot Enterprise',\n        // Enterprise uses a different API endpoint - will be set dynamically based on auth\n        api: undefined,\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Add Claude OAuth provider that inherits from Anthropic\n    // This allows using Claude Code CLI OAuth credentials with the Anthropic API\n    if (database['anthropic']) {\n      const anthropic = database['anthropic'];\n      database['claude-oauth'] = {\n        ...anthropic,\n        id: 'claude-oauth',\n        name: 'Claude OAuth',\n        // Use CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN environment variable\n        env: ['CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN'],\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Add gemini-3-pro alias for google provider\n    // The actual model in Google's API is gemini-3-pro-preview, but we add gemini-3-pro as an alias for convenience\n    if (database['google']?.models['gemini-3-pro-preview']) {\n      const gemini3ProPreview =\n        database['google'].models['gemini-3-pro-preview'];\n      database['google'].models['gemini-3-pro'] = {\n        ...gemini3ProPreview,\n        id: 'gemini-3-pro-preview',\n        name: 'Gemini 3 Pro',\n      };\n      realIdByKey.set('google/gemini-3-pro', 'gemini-3-pro-preview');\n    }\n\n    // Add link-assistant echo provider for dry-run testing\n    // This synthetic provider echoes back user input without API calls\n    // @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/89\n    database['link-assistant'] = {\n      id: 'link-assistant',\n      name: 'Link Assistant (Echo)',\n      env: [], // No environment variables needed - synthetic provider\n      models: {\n        echo: {\n          id: 'echo',\n          name: 'Echo Model',\n          release_date: '2024-01-01',\n          attachment: false,\n          reasoning: false,\n          temperature: false,\n          tool_call: true,\n          cost: {\n            input: 0,\n            output: 0,\n            cache_read: 0,\n            cache_write: 0,\n          },\n          limit: {\n            context: 1000000, // Virtually unlimited\n            output: 100000,\n          },\n          modalities: {\n            input: ['text'],\n            output: ['text'],\n          },\n          options: {},\n        },\n      },\n    };\n\n    // Add link-assistant/cache provider for caching API responses\n    // This synthetic provider caches responses and falls back to echo\n    // @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/89\n    database['link-assistant/cache'] = {\n      id: 'link-assistant/cache',\n      name: 'Link Assistant (Cache)',\n      env: [], // No environment variables needed - synthetic provider\n      models: {}, // Models are dynamically created based on the provider/model syntax\n            cache_write: 0,\n          },\n          limit: {\n            context: 202800,\n            output: 131072,\n          },\n          modalities: {\n            input: ['text'],\n 'minimax-m2.5-free': {\n          id: 'minimax/minimax-m2.5:free',\n          name: 'MiniMax M2.5 (Free)',\n          release_date: '2026-01-01',\n          attachment: false,\n          reasoning:     context: 163840,\n            output: 163840,\n          },\n          modalities: {\n            input: ['text'],\n            output: ['text'],\n          },\n          options: {},\n        },\n      },\n    };\n\n    for (const [providerID, provider] of configProviders) {\n      const existing = database[providerID];\n      const parsed: ModelsDev.Provider = {\n        id: providerID,\n        npm: provider.npm ?? existing?.npm,\n        name: provider.name ?? existing?.name ?? providerID,\n        env: provider.env ?? existing?.env ?? [],\n        api: provider.api ?? existing?.api,\n        models: existing?.models ?? {},\n      };\n\n      for (const [modelID, model] of Object.entries(provider.models ?? {})) {\n        const existing = parsed.models[model.id ?? modelID];\n        const name = iife(() => {\n          if (model.name) return model.name;\n          if (model.id && model.id !== modelID) return modelID;\n          return existing?.name ?? modelID;\n        });\n        const parsedModel: ModelsDev.Model = {\n          id: modelID,\n          name,\n          release_date: model.release_date ?? existing?.release_date,\n          attachment: model.attachment ?? existing?.attachment ?? false,\n          reasoning: model.reasoning ?? existing?.reasoning ?? false,\n          temperature: model.temperature ?? existing?.temperature ?? false,\n          tool_call: model.tool_call ?? existing?.tool_call ?? true,\n          cost:\n            !model.cost && !existing?.cost\n              ? {\n                  input: 0,\n                  output: 0,\n                  cache_read: 0,\n                  cache_write: 0,\n                }\n              : {\n                  cache_read: 0,\n                  cache_write: 0,\n                  ...existing?.cost,\n                  ...model.cost,\n                },\n          options: {\n            ...existing?.options,\n            ...model.options,\n          },\n          limit: model.limit ??\n            existing?.limit ?? {\n              context: 0,\n              output: 0,\n            },\n          modalities: model.modalities ??\n            existing?.modalities ?? {\n              input: ['text'],\n              output: ['text'],\n            },\n          headers: model.headers,\n          provider: model.provider ?? existing?.provider,\n        };\n        if (model.id && model.id !== modelID) {\n          realIdByKey.set(`${providerID}/${modelID}`, model.id);\n        }\n        parsed.models[modelID] = parsedModel;\n      }\n      database[providerID] = parsed;\n    }\n\n    const disabled = await Config.get().then(\n      (cfg) => new Set(cfg.disabled_providers ?? [])\n    );\n    // load env\n    for (const [providerID, provider] of Object.entries(database)) {\n      if (disabled.has(providerID)) continue;\n      // Find the first truthy env var (supports multiple env var options like Google's\n      // GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY and GEMINI_API_KEY)\n      const apiKey = provider.env\n        .map((item) => process.env[item])\n        .find(Boolean);\n      if (!apiKey) continue;\n      // Always pass the API key - the provider SDK needs it for authentication\n      mergeProvider(providerID, { apiKey }, 'env');\n    }\n\n    // load apikeys\n    for (const [providerID, provider] of Object.entries(await Auth.all())) {\n      if (disabled.has(providerID)) continue;\n      if (provider.type === 'api') {\n        mergeProvider(providerID, { apiKey: provider.key }, 'api');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // load custom\n    for (const [providerID, fn] of Object.entries(CUSTOM_LOADERS)) {\n      if (disabled.has(providerID)) continue;\n      const result = await fn(database[providerID]);\n      if (result && (result.autoload || providers[providerID])) {\n        mergeProvider(\n          providerID,\n          result.options ?? {},\n          'custom',\n          result.getModel\n        );\n      }\n    }\n\n    // load config\n    for (const [providerID, provider] of configProviders) {\n      mergeProvider(providerID, provider.options ?? {}, 'config');\n    }\n\n    for (const [providerID, provider] of Object.entries(providers)) {\n      const filteredModels = Object.fromEntries(\n        Object.entries(provider.info.models)\n          // Filter out blacklisted models\n          .filter(\n            ([modelID]) =>\n              modelID !== 'gpt-5-chat-latest' &&\n              !(providerID === 'openrouter' && modelID === 'openai/gpt-5-chat')\n          )\n          // Filter out experimental models\n          .filter(\n            ([, model]) =>\n              ((!model.experimental && model.status !== 'alpha') ||\n                config.enableExperimentalModels) &&\n              model.status !== 'deprecated'\n          )\n      );\n      provider.info.models = filteredModels;\n\n      if (Object.keys(provider.info.models).length === 0) {\n        delete providers[providerID];\n        continue;\n      }\n      log.info(() => ({ message: 'found', providerID }));\n    }\n\n    return {\n      models,\n      providers,\n      sdk,\n      realIdByKey,\n    };\n  });\n\n  export async function list() {\n    return state().then((state) => state.providers);\n  }\n\n  async function getSDK(provider: ModelsDev.Provider, model: ModelsDev.Model) {\n    return (async () => {\n      using _ = log.time('getSDK', {\n        providerID: provider.id,\n      });\n      const s = await state();\n      const pkg = model.provider?.npm ?? provider.npm ?? provider.id;\n      const options = { ...s.providers[provider.id]?.options };\n      if (\n        pkg.includes('@ai-sdk/openai-compatible') &&\n        options['includeUsage'] === undefined\n      ) {\n        options['includeUsage'] = true;\n      }\n      const key = Bun.hash.xxHash32(JSON.stringify({ pkg, options }));\n      const existing = s.sdk.get(key);\n      if (existing) return existing;\n\n      // Apply timeout wrapper to options if timeout is specified\n      if (options['timeout'] !== undefined && options['timeout'] !== null) {\n        // Preserve custom fetch if it exists, wrap it with timeout logic\n        const customFetch = options['fetch'];\n        options['fetch'] = async (input: any, init?: BunFetchRequestInit) => {\n          const { signal, ...rest } = init ?? {};\n\n          const signals: AbortSignal[] = [];\n          if (signal) signals.push(signal);\n          if (options['timeout'] !== false)\n            signals.push(AbortSignal.timeout(options['timeout']));\n\n          const combined =\n            signals.length > 1 ? AbortSignal.any(signals) : signals[0];\n\n          const fetchFn = customFetch ?? fetch;\n          return fetchFn(input, {\n            ...rest,\n            signal: combined,\n            // @ts-ignore see here: https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues/16682\n            timeout: false,\n          });\n        };\n      }\n\n      // Wrap fetch with retry logic for rate limit handling (HTTP 429)\n      // This ensures the agent's time-based retry (7-week timeout) is respected\n      // instead of the AI SDK's fixed retry count (3 attempts)\n      // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/167\n      const existingFetch = options['fetch'] ?? fetch;\n      options['fetch'] = RetryFetch.wrap(existingFetch, {\n        sessionID: provider.id,\n      });\n\n      // Verbose HTTP logging for provider API calls.\n      // This provider-level wrapper logs HTTP requests/responses independently\n      // of the global fetch monkey-patch. Both mechanisms are kept active to\n      // maximize HTTP observability — the global patch may miss calls if the\n      // AI SDK captures/resolves fetch references before it is installed,\n      // while this wrapper is injected directly into the SDK's fetch option.\n      // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/221\n      // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/217\n      // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/215\n      {\n        const innerFetch = options['fetch'];\n        let verboseWrapperConfirmed = false;\n        let httpCallCount = 0;\n\n        log.info('provider SDK fetch chain configured', {\n          providerID: provider.id,\n          pkg,\n          globalVerboseFetchInstalled:\n            !!globalThis.__agentVerboseFetchInstalled,\n          verboseAtCreation: isVerbose(),\n        });\n\n        options['fetch'] = async (\n          input: RequestInfo | URL,\n          init?: RequestInit\n        ): Promise<Response> => {\n          // Check verbose flag at call time — not at SDK creation time.\n          // Uses isVerbose() with env var fallback for resilience against\n          // flag state loss in subprocess/module-reload scenarios.\n          // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/206\n          // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/227\n          if (!isVerbose()) {\n            return innerFetch(input, init);\n          }\n\n          httpCallCount++;\n          const callNum = httpCallCount;\n\n          // Log a one-time confirmation that the verbose wrapper is active for this provider.\n          // This diagnostic breadcrumb confirms the wrapper is in the fetch chain.\n          // Also write to stderr as a redundant channel — stdout JSON may be filtered by wrappers.\n          // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/215\n          if (!verboseWrapperConfirmed) {\n            verboseWrapperConfirmed = true;\n            log.info('verbose HTTP logging active', {\n              providerID: provider.id,\n            });\n            // Redundant stderr confirmation — visible even if stdout is piped/filtered\n            process.stderr.write(\n              `[verbose] HTTP logging active for provider: ${provider.id}\\n`\n            );\n          }\n\n          const url =\n            typeof input === 'string'\n              ? input\n              : input instanceof URL\n                ? input.toString()\n                : input.url;\n          const method = init?.method ?? 'GET';\n\n          // Wrap all verbose logging in try/catch so it never breaks the actual HTTP request.\n          // If logging fails, the request must still proceed — logging is observability, not control flow.\n          // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/215\n          let sanitizedHeaders: Record<string, string> = {};\n          let bodyPreview: string | undefined;\n          try {\n            // Sanitize headers - mask authorization values\n            const rawHeaders = init?.headers;\n            if (rawHeaders) {\n              const entries =\n                rawHeaders instanceof Headers\n                  ? Array.from(rawHeaders.entries())\n                  : Array.isArray(rawHeaders)\n                    ? rawHeaders\n                    : Object.entries(rawHeaders);\n              for (const [key, value] of entries) {\n                const lower = key.toLowerCase();\n                if (\n                  lower === 'authorization' ||\n                  lower === 'x-api-key' ||\n                  lower === 'api-key'\n                ) {\n                  sanitizedHeaders[key] =\n                    typeof value === 'string' && value.length > 8\n                      ? value.slice(0, 4) + '...' + value.slice(-4)\n                      : '[REDACTED]';\n                } else {\n                  sanitizedHeaders[key] = String(value);\n                }\n              }\n            }\n\n            // Log request body preview (truncated)\n            if (init?.body) {\n              const bodyStr =\n                typeof init.body === 'string'\n                  ? init.body\n                  : init.body instanceof ArrayBuffer ||\n                      init.body instanceof Uint8Array\n                    ? `[binary ${(init.body as ArrayBuffer).byteLength ?? (init.body as Uint8Array).length} bytes]`\n                    : undefined;\n              if (bodyStr && typeof bodyStr === 'string') {\n                bodyPreview =\n                  bodyStr.length > 200000\n                    ? bodyStr.slice(0, 200000) +\n                      `... [truncated, total ${bodyStr.length} chars]`\n                    : bodyStr;\n              }\n            }\n          } catch (prepError) {\n            // If header/body processing fails, log the error but continue with the request\n            log.warn('verbose logging: failed to prepare request details', {\n              providerID: provider.id,\n              error:\n                prepError instanceof Error\n                  ? prepError.message\n                  : String(prepError),\n            });\n          }\n\n          // Use direct (non-lazy) logging for HTTP request/response to ensure output\n          // is not lost when piped through external process wrappers (e.g., solve.mjs).\n          // The verbose check is already done above, so lazy evaluation is not needed here.\n          // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/211\n          log.info('HTTP request', {\n            caller: `provider/${provider.id}`,\n            providerID: provider.id,\n            callNum,\n            method,\n            url,\n            headers: sanitizedHeaders,\n            bodyPreview,\n          });\n\n          const startMs = Date.now();\n          try {\n            // Pass Bun-specific verbose:true to get detailed connection debugging\n            // (prints request/response headers to stderr on socket errors).\n            // This is a no-op on non-Bun runtimes.\n            // See: https://bun.sh/docs/api/fetch\n            // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/215\n            const verboseInit = { ...init, verbose: true } as RequestInit;\n            const response = await innerFetch(input, verboseInit);\n            const durationMs = Date.now() - startMs;\n\n            // Use direct (non-lazy) logging to ensure HTTP response details are captured\n            // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/211\n            log.info('HTTP response', {\n              caller: `provider/${provider.id}`,\n              providerID: provider.id,\n              callNum,\n              method,\n              url,\n              status: response.status,\n              statusText: response.statusText,\n              durationMs,\n              responseHeaders: Object.fromEntries(response.headers.entries()),\n            });\n\n            // Log response body for debugging provider failures\n            // For streaming responses (SSE/event-stream), tee() the stream so the AI SDK\n            // still receives the full stream while we asynchronously log a preview.\n            // For non-streaming responses, buffer the body and reconstruct the Response.\n            // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/204\n            const responseBodyMaxChars = 200000;\n            const contentType = response.headers.get('content-type') ?? '';\n            const isStreaming =\n              contentType.includes('event-stream') ||\n              contentType.includes('octet-stream');\n\n            if (response.body) {\n              if (isStreaming) {\n                // Tee the stream: one copy for AI SDK, one for logging\n                const [sdkStream, logStream] = response.body.tee();\n\n                // Consume log stream asynchronously (does not block SDK)\n                (async () => {\n                  try {\n                    const reader = logStream.getReader();\n                    const decoder = new TextDecoder();\n                    let bodyPreview = '';\n                    let truncated = false;\n                    while (true) {\n                      const { done, value } = await reader.read();\n                      if (done) break;\n                      if (!truncated) {\n                        const chunk = decoder.decode(value, { stream: true });\n                        bodyPreview += chunk;\n                        if (bodyPreview.length > responseBodyMaxChars) {\n                          bodyPreview = bodyPreview.slice(\n                            0,\n                            responseBodyMaxChars\n                          );\n                          truncated = true;\n                        }\n                      }\n                    }\n                    // Use direct (non-lazy) logging for stream body\n                    // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/211\n                    log.info('HTTP response body (stream)', {\n                      caller: `provider/${provider.id}`,\n                      providerID: provider.id,\n                      callNum,\n                      url,\n                      bodyPreview: truncated\n                        ? bodyPreview + `... [truncated]`\n                        : bodyPreview,\n                    });\n                  } catch {\n                    // Ignore logging errors — do not affect the SDK stream\n                  }\n                })();\n\n                // Return response with the SDK's copy of the stream\n                return new Response(sdkStream, {\n                  status: response.status,\n                  statusText: response.statusText,\n                  headers: response.headers,\n                });\n              } else {\n                // Non-streaming: buffer body, log it, reconstruct Response\n                const bodyText = await response.text();\n                const bodyPreview =\n                  bodyText.length > responseBodyMaxChars\n                    ? bodyText.slice(0, responseBodyMaxChars) +\n                      `... [truncated, total ${bodyText.length} chars]`\n                    : bodyText;\n                // Use direct (non-lazy) logging for non-streaming body\n                // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/211\n                log.info('HTTP response body', {\n                  caller: `provider/${provider.id}`,\n                  providerID: provider.id,\n                  callNum,\n                  url,\n                  bodyPreview,\n                });\n                return new Response(bodyText, {\n                  status: response.status,\n                  statusText: response.statusText,\n                  headers: response.headers,\n                });\n              }\n            }\n\n            return response;\n          } catch (error) {\n            const durationMs = Date.now() - startMs;\n            // Use direct (non-lazy) logging for error path\n            // Include stack trace and error cause for better debugging of socket errors\n            // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/211\n            // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/215\n            log.error('HTTP request failed', {\n              caller: `provider/${provider.id}`,\n              providerID: provider.id,\n              callNum,\n              method,\n              url,\n              durationMs,\n              error:\n                error instanceof Error\n                  ? {\n                      name: error.name,\n                      message: error.message,\n                      stack: error.stack,\n                      cause:\n                        error.cause instanceof Error\n                          ? error.cause.message\n                          : error.cause\n                            ? String(error.cause)\n                            : undefined,\n                    }\n                  : String(error),\n            });\n            throw error;\n          }\n        };\n      }\n\n      // Check if we have a bundled provider first - this avoids runtime package installation\n      // which can hang or timeout due to known Bun issues\n      // @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/173\n      // @see https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues/5831\n      const bundledFn = BUNDLED_PROVIDERS[pkg];\n      if (bundledFn) {\n        log.info(() => ({\n          message: 'using bundled provider (no installation needed)',\n          providerID: provider.id,\n          pkg,\n        }));\n        const loaded = bundledFn({\n          name: provider.id,\n          ...options,\n        });\n        s.sdk.set(key, loaded);\n        return loaded as SDK;\n      }\n\n      // Fall back to dynamic installation for non-bundled providers\n      let installedPath: string;\n      if (!pkg.startsWith('file://')) {\n        log.info(() => ({\n          message: 'installing provider package (not bundled)',\n          providerID: provider.id,\n          pkg,\n          version: 'latest',\n        }));\n        installedPath = await BunProc.install(pkg, 'latest');\n        log.info(() => ({\n          message: 'provider package installed successfully',\n          providerID: provider.id,\n          pkg,\n          installedPath,\n        }));\n      } else {\n        log.info(() => ({ message: 'loading local provider', pkg }));\n        installedPath = pkg;\n      }\n\n      // The `google-vertex-anthropic` provider points to the `@ai-sdk/google-vertex` package.\n      // Ref: https://github.com/sst/models.dev/blob/0a87de42ab177bebad0620a889e2eb2b4a5dd4ab/providers/google-vertex-anthropic/provider.toml\n      // However, the actual export is at the subpath `@ai-sdk/google-vertex/anthropic`.\n      // Ref: https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/google-vertex#google-vertex-anthropic-provider-usage\n      // In addition, Bun's dynamic import logic does not support subpath imports,\n      // so we patch the import path to load directly from `dist`.\n      const modPath =\n        provider.id === 'google-vertex-anthropic'\n          ? `${installedPath}/dist/anthropic/index.mjs`\n          : installedPath;\n      const mod = await import(modPath);\n\n      const fn = mod[Object.keys(mod).find((key) => key.startsWith('create'))!];\n      const loaded = fn({\n        name: provider.id,\n        ...options,\n      });\n      s.sdk.set(key, loaded);\n      return loaded as SDK;\n    })().catch((e) => {\n      log.error(() => ({\n        message: 'provider initialization failed',\n        providerID: provider.id,\n        pkg: model.provider?.npm ?? provider.npm ?? provider.id,\n        error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e),\n        stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined,\n        cause: e instanceof Error && e.cause ? String(e.cause) : undefined,\n      }));\n      throw new InitError({ providerID: provider.id }, { cause: e });\n    });\n  }\n\n  export async function getProvider(providerID: string) {\n    return state().then((s) => s.providers[providerID]);\n  }\n\n  export async function getModel(providerID: string, modelID: string) {\n    const key = `${providerID}/${modelID}`;\n    const s = await state();\n    if (s.models.has(key)) return s.models.get(key)!;\n\n    log.info(() => ({ message: 'getModel', providerID, modelID }));\n\n    const provider = s.providers[providerID];\n    if (!provider) {\n      // Check if this model ID might exist in another provider (e.g., OpenRouter)\n      // This helps users who use formats like \"z-ai/glm-4.7\" instead of \"openrouter/z-ai/glm-4.7\"\n      const fullModelKey = `${providerID}/${modelID}`;\n      let suggestion: string | undefined;\n\n      for (const [knownProviderID, knownProvider] of Object.entries(\n        s.providers\n      )) {\n        if (knownProvider.info.models[fullModelKey]) {\n          suggestion = `Did you mean: ${knownProviderID}/${fullModelKey}?`;\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n\n      throw new ModelNotFoundError({ providerID, modelID, suggestion });\n    }\n\n    // For synthetic providers (like link-assistant/echo and link-assistant/cache), skip SDK loading\n    // These providers have a custom getModel function that creates the model directly\n    const isSyntheticProvider =\n      providerID === 'link-assistant' || providerID === 'link-assistant/cache';\n\n    // For synthetic providers, we don't need model info from the database\n    let info = isSyntheticProvider ? null : provider.info.models[modelID];\n    if (!isSyntheticProvider && !info) {\n      // Model not in provider's known list - try refreshing the cache first (#200)\n      // This handles stale bundled data or expired cache (1-hour TTL)\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'model not in catalog - refreshing models cache',\n        providerID,\n        modelID,\n      }));\n      try {\n        await ModelsDev.refresh();\n        const freshDB = await ModelsDev.get();\n        const freshProvider = freshDB[providerID];\n        if (freshProvider?.models[modelID]) {\n          // Model found after refresh - update provider info and use it\n          provider.info.models[modelID] = freshProvider.models[modelID];\n          info = freshProvider.models[modelID];\n          log.info(() => ({\n            message: 'model found after cache refresh',\n            providerID,\n            modelID,\n          }));\n        }\n      } catch (refreshError) {\n        log.warn(() => ({\n          message: 'cache refresh failed',\n          error:\n            refreshError instanceof Error\n              ? refreshError.message\n              : String(refreshError),\n        }));\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (!isSyntheticProvider && !info) {\n      // Still not found after refresh - create fallback info and try anyway\n      // Provider may support unlisted models\n      const availableInProvider = Object.keys(provider.info.models).slice(\n        0,\n        10\n      );\n      log.warn(() => ({\n        message:\n          'model not in provider catalog after refresh - attempting anyway (may be unlisted)',\n        providerID,\n        modelID,\n        availableModels: availableInProvider,\n        totalModels: Object.keys(provider.info.models).length,\n      }));\n\n      // Create a minimal fallback model info so SDK loading can proceed\n      // Use sensible defaults - the provider will reject if the model truly doesn't exist\n      info = {\n        id: modelID,\n        name: modelID,\n        release_date: '',\n        attachment: false,\n        reasoning: false,\n        temperature: true,\n        tool_call: true,\n        cost: { input: 0, output: 0 },\n        limit: { context: 128000, output: 16384 },\n        options: {},\n      } as ModelsDev.Model;\n    }\n\n    try {\n      const keyReal = `${providerID}/${modelID}`;\n      const realID = s.realIdByKey.get(keyReal) ?? (info ? info.id : modelID);\n\n      let language: LanguageModel;\n      if (isSyntheticProvider && provider.getModel) {\n        // For synthetic providers, call getModel directly without SDK\n        language = await provider.getModel(null, realID, provider.options);\n      } else {\n        // For regular providers, load the SDK first\n        const sdk = await getSDK(provider.info, info!);\n        language = provider.getModel\n          ? await provider.getModel(sdk, realID, provider.options)\n          : sdk.languageModel(realID);\n      }\n      log.info(() => ({ message: 'found', providerID, modelID }));\n      s.models.set(key, {\n        providerID,\n        modelID,\n        info,\n        language,\n        npm: isSyntheticProvider\n          ? provider.info.npm\n          : (info.provider?.npm ?? provider.info.npm),\n      });\n      return {\n        modelID,\n        providerID,\n        info,\n        language,\n        npm: info.provider?.npm ?? provider.info.npm,\n      };\n    } catch (e) {\n      if (e instanceof NoSuchModelError)\n        throw new ModelNotFoundError(\n          {\n            modelID: modelID,\n            providerID,\n          },\n          { cause: e }\n        );\n      throw e;\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get a small/cheap model for auxiliary tasks like title generation and summarization.\n   * This is NOT the primary model for user requests - it's used for background tasks.\n   *\n   * Note: Logs from this function may show a different model than what the user specified.\n   * This is by design - we use cheaper models for auxiliary tasks to save tokens/costs.\n   *\n   * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/179\n   */\n  export async function getSmallModel(providerID: string) {\n    const cfg = await Config.get();\n\n    if (cfg.small_model) {\n      const parsed = parseModel(cfg.small_model);\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'using configured small_model for auxiliary task',\n        modelID: parsed.modelID,\n        providerID: parsed.providerID,\n      }));\n      return getModel(parsed.providerID, parsed.modelID);\n    }\n\n    const provider = await state().then((state) => state.providers[providerID]);\n    if (!provider) return;\n    let priority = [\n      'claude-haiku-4-5',\n      'claude-haiku-4.5',\n      '3-5-haiku',\n      '3.5-haiku',\n      'gemini-2.5-flash',\n      'gpt-5-nano',\n    ];\n    // claude-haiku-4.5 is considered a premium model in github copilot, we shouldn't use premium requests for title gen\n    if (providerID === 'github-copilot') {\n      priority = priority.filter((m) => m !== 'claude-haiku-4.5');\n    }\n    if (providerID === 'opencode' || providerID === 'local') {\n      priority = ['minimax-m2.5-free', 'gpt-5-nano', 'big-pickle'];\n    }\n    if (providerID === 'kilo') {\n      priority = [\n        'glm-5-free',\n        'glm-4.5-air-free',\n        'minimax-m2.5-free',\n        'deepseek-r1-free',\n        'giga-potato-free',\n      ];\n    }\n    for (const item of priority) {\n      for (const model of Object.keys(provider.info.models)) {\n        if (model.includes(item)) {\n          log.info(() => ({\n            message: 'selected small model for auxiliary task',\n            modelID: model,\n            providerID,\n            hint: 'This model is used for title/summary generation, not primary requests',\n          }));\n          return getModel(providerID, model);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  const priority = [\n    'glm-5-free',\n    'minimax-m2.5-free',\n    'gpt-5-nano',\n    'big-pickle',\n    'gpt-5',\n    'claude-sonnet-4',\n    'gemini-3-pro',\n  ];\n  export function sort(models: ModelsDev.Model[]) {\n    return sortBy(\n      models,\n      [\n        (model) => priority.findIndex((filter) => model.id.includes(filter)),\n        'desc',\n      ],\n      [(model) => (model.id.includes('latest') ? 0 : 1), 'asc'],\n      [(model) => model.id, 'desc']\n    );\n  }\n\n  export async function defaultModel() {\n    const cfg = await Config.get();\n\n    // In dry-run mode, use the echo provider by default\n    // This allows testing round-trips and multi-turn conversations without API costs\n    // @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/89\n    if (config.dryRun) {\n      log.info('dry-run mode enabled, using echo provider as default');\n      return {\n        providerID: 'link-assistant',\n        modelID: 'echo',\n      };\n    }\n\n    if (cfg.model) return parseModel(cfg.model);\n\n    // Prefer opencode provider if available\n    const providers = await list().then((val) => Object.values(val));\n    const opencodeProvider = providers.find((p) => p.info.id === 'opencode');\n    if (opencodeProvider) {\n      const [model] = sort(Object.values(opencodeProvider.info.models));\n      if (model) {\n        log.info(() => ({\n          message: 'using opencode provider as default',\n          provider: opencodeProvider.info.id,\n          model: model.id,\n        }));\n        return {\n          providerID: opencodeProvider.info.id,\n          modelID: model.id,\n        };\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Fall back to any available provider if opencode is not available\n    const provider = providers.find(\n      (p) => !cfg.provider || Object.keys(cfg.provider).includes(p.info.id)\n    );\n    if (!provider) throw new Error('no providers found');\n    const [model] = sort(Object.values(provider.info.models));\n    if (!model) throw new Error('no models found');\n    return {\n      providerID: provider.info.id,\n      modelID: model.id,\n    };\n  }\n\n  export function parseModel(model: string) {\n    const [providerID, ...rest] = model.split('/');\n    return {\n      providerID: providerID,\n      modelID: rest.join('/'),\n    };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Resolve a short model name (without provider prefix) to the appropriate provider.\n   * This function finds which provider should handle a model when no explicit provider is specified.\n   *\n   * Priority for free models:\n   * 1. If model is uniquely available in one provider, use that provider\n   * 2. If model is available in multiple providers, prioritize based on free model availability:\n   *    - kilo: glm-5-free, glm-4.5-air-free, minimax-m2.5-free, giga-potato-free, deepseek-r1-free (unique to Kilo)\n   *    - opencode: big-pickle, gpt-5-nano (unique to OpenCode)\n   * 3. For shared models, prefer OpenCode first, then fall back to Kilo on rate limit\n   *\n   * @param modelID - Short model name without provider prefix\n   * @returns Provider ID that should handle this model, or undefined if not found\n   */\n  export async function resolveShortModelName(\n    modelID: string\n  ): Promise<{ providerID: string; modelID: string } | undefined> {\n    const s = await state();\n\n    // Define model-to-provider mappings for free models\n    // Models unique to Kilo (GLM models from Z.AI are only free on Kilo)\n    const kiloUniqueModels = [\n      'glm-5-free',\n      'glm-4.5-air-free',\n      'minimax-m2.5-free',\n      'giga-potato-free',\n      'trinity-large-preview',\n      'deepseek-r1-free',\n    ];\n\n    // Check if it's a Kilo-unique model\n    if (kiloUniqueModels.includes(modelID)) {\n      const kiloProvider = s.providers['kilo'];\n      if (kiloProvider && kiloProvider.info.models[modelID]) {\n        log.info(() => ({\n          message: 'resolved short model name to kilo (unique)',\n          modelID,\n        }));\n        return { providerID: 'kilo', modelID };\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check if model exists in any provider\n    const matchingProviders: string[] = [];\n    for (const [providerID, provider] of Object.entries(s.providers)) {\n      if (provider.info.models[modelID]) {\n        matchingProviders.push(providerID);\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (matchingProviders.length === 0) {\n      return undefined;\n    }\n\n    if (matchingProviders.length === 1) {\n      const providerID = matchingProviders[0];\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'resolved short model name (single match)',\n        modelID,\n        providerID,\n      }));\n      return { providerID, modelID };\n    }\n\n    // Multiple providers have this model - prefer OpenCode for shared free models\n    // This follows the convention that opencode is the primary free provider\n    if (matchingProviders.includes('opencode')) {\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'resolved short model name to opencode (multiple providers)',\n        modelID,\n        availableProviders: matchingProviders,\n      }));\n      return { providerID: 'opencode', modelID };\n    }\n\n    // Fallback to first matching provider\n    const providerID = matchingProviders[0];\n    log.info(() => ({\n      message: 'resolved short model name (fallback)',\n      modelID,\n      providerID,\n      availableProviders: matchingProviders,\n    }));\n    return { providerID, modelID };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Parse a model string that may or may not include a provider prefix.\n   * If no provider is specified, attempts to resolve the short model name to the appropriate provider.\n   *\n   * IMPORTANT: If the model cannot be resolved, this function throws an error instead of\n   * silently falling back to a default. This ensures the user gets their requested model\n   * or a clear error explaining why it's not available.\n   *\n   * Examples:\n   * - \"kilo/glm-5-free\" -> { providerID: \"kilo\", modelID: \"glm-5-free\" }\n   * - \"glm-5-free\" -> { providerID: \"kilo\", modelID: \"glm-5-free\" } (resolved)\n   * - \"big-pickle\" -> { providerID: \"opencode\", modelID: \"big-pickle\" } (resolved)\n   * - \"nonexistent-model\" -> throws ModelNotFoundError\n   *\n   * @param model - Model string with or without provider prefix\n   * @returns Parsed provider ID and model ID\n   * @throws ModelNotFoundError if the model cannot be found in any provider\n   * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/194\n   */\n  export async function parseModelWithResolution(\n    model: string\n  ): Promise<{ providerID: string; modelID: string }> {\n    // Check if model includes a provider prefix\n    if (model.includes('/')) {\n      // Explicit provider specified - use it directly\n      return parseModel(model);\n    }\n\n    // No provider prefix - try to resolve the short model name\n    const resolved = await resolveShortModelName(model);\n    if (resolved) {\n      return resolved;\n    }\n\n    // Unable to resolve - fail with a clear error instead of silently falling back\n    // This prevents the issue where user requests model X but gets model Y\n    // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/194\n    const s = await state();\n    const availableModels: string[] = [];\n\n    // Collect some available models to suggest\n    for (const [providerID, provider] of Object.entries(s.providers)) {\n      for (const modelID of Object.keys(provider.info.models).slice(0, 3)) {\n        availableModels.push(`${providerID}/${modelID}`);\n      }\n      if (availableModels.length >= 6) break;\n    }\n\n    const suggestion =\n      availableModels.length > 0\n        ? `Model \"${model}\" not found in any provider. Available models include: ${availableModels.join(', ')}. Use --model provider/model-id format for explicit selection.`\n        : `Model \"${model}\" not found. No providers are currently available. Check your API keys or authentication.`;\n\n    log.error(() => ({\n      message: 'model not found - refusing to silently fallback',\n      requestedModel: model,\n      availableModels,\n    }));\n\n    throw new ModelNotFoundError({\n      providerID: 'unknown',\n      modelID: model,\n      suggestion,\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Defines models that are available in multiple free providers.\n   * When one provider hits rate limits, the system can try an alternative.\n   *\n   * Note: This is only used for models without explicit provider specification.\n   * If user specifies \"kilo/deepseek-r1-free\", no fallback will occur.\n   */\n  const SHARED_FREE_MODELS: Record<string, string[]> = {\n    // Currently no shared models between OpenCode and Kilo providers.\n    // Kilo models use different IDs than OpenCode models.\n  };\n\n  /**\n   * Get alternative providers for a model when the primary provider fails (e.g., rate limited).\n   * This function returns a list of alternative providers that offer the same model.\n   *\n   * Note: This only returns alternatives for models without explicit provider specification.\n   * If the original request had an explicit provider (like \"kilo/deepseek-r1-free\"), this returns empty array.\n   *\n   * @param modelID - The model ID to find alternatives for\n   * @param failedProviderID - The provider that failed\n   * @param wasExplicitProvider - Whether the user explicitly specified the provider\n   * @returns Array of alternative provider IDs that can serve this model\n   */\n  export async function getAlternativeProviders(\n    modelID: string,\n    failedProviderID: string,\n    wasExplicitProvider: boolean\n  ): Promise<string[]> {\n    // If the user explicitly specified a provider, don't offer alternatives\n    if (wasExplicitProvider) {\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'no alternative providers (explicit provider specified)',\n        modelID,\n        failedProviderID,\n      }));\n      return [];\n    }\n\n    // Check if this is a shared model\n    const sharedProviders = SHARED_FREE_MODELS[modelID];\n    if (!sharedProviders) {\n      // Not a shared model, no alternatives\n      return [];\n    }\n\n    // Get alternative providers (excluding the failed one)\n    const s = await state();\n    const alternatives = sharedProviders.filter(\n      (p) => p !== failedProviderID && s.providers[p]\n    );\n\n    if (alternatives.length > 0) {\n      log.info(() => ({\n        message: 'found alternative providers for rate-limited model',\n        modelID,\n        failedProviderID,\n        alternatives,\n      }));\n    }\n\n    return alternatives;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Checks if an error indicates a rate limit issue.\n   * @param error - The error to check\n   * @returns true if the error indicates a rate limit\n   */\n  export function isRateLimitError(error: unknown): boolean {\n    if (!(error instanceof Error)) return false;\n\n    const message = error.message.toLowerCase();\n    const name = error.name.toLowerCase();\n\n    return (\n      message.includes('rate limit') ||\n      message.includes('ratelimit') ||\n      message.includes('too many requests') ||\n      message.includes('429') ||\n      name.includes('ratelimit')\n    );\n  }\n\n  export const ModelNotFoundError = NamedError.create(\n    'ProviderModelNotFoundError',\n    z.object({\n      providerID: z.string(),\n      modelID: z.string(),\n      suggestion: z.string().optional(),\n    })\n  );\n\n  export const InitError = NamedError.create(\n    'ProviderInitError',\n    z.object({\n      providerID: z.string(),\n    })\n  );\n}\n",
