    "originalFile": "import { Log } from './log';\nimport { isVerbose } from '../config/config';\n\n/**\n * Shared verbose HTTP fetch wrapper.\n *\n * Intercepts fetch() calls and logs request/response details as JSON objects\n * when --verbose mode is enabled. Used across the entire codebase (tools, auth,\n * config, providers) to ensure uniform and predictable HTTP logging.\n *\n * Features:\n * - Logs HTTP request: method, URL, sanitized headers, body preview\n * - Logs HTTP response: status, headers, duration, body preview\n * - Logs HTTP errors: stack trace, error cause chain\n * - Sequential call numbering for correlation\n * - Error-resilient: logging failures never break the actual HTTP request\n * - Runtime verbose check: respects isVerbose() at call time (with env var fallback)\n *\n * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/215\n */\n\nconst log = Log.create({ service: 'http' });\n\n/** Global call counter shared across all verbose fetch wrappers */\nlet globalHttpCallCount = 0;\n\n/**\n * Track pending async stream log operations (#231).\n * When the process exits while stream logging is in progress, we log a warning\n * so missing HTTP response bodies are visible in the logs rather than silently lost.\n */\nlet pendingStreamLogs = 0;\n\n/**\n * Get the current count of pending stream log operations.\n * Useful for diagnostics and testing.\n */\nexport function getPendingStreamLogCount(): number {\n  return pendingStreamLogs;\n}\n\n/**\n * Sanitize HTTP headers by masking sensitive values.\n * Masks authorization, x-api-key, and api-key headers.\n */\nexport function sanitizeHeaders(\n  rawHeaders: HeadersInit | Record<string, string> | Headers | undefined\n): Record<string, string> {\n  const sanitized: Record<string, string> = {};\n  if (!rawHeaders) return sanitized;\n\n  const entries =\n    rawHeaders instanceof Headers\n      ? Array.from(rawHeaders.entries())\n      : Array.isArray(rawHeaders)\n        ? rawHeaders\n        : Object.entries(rawHeaders);\n\n  for (const [key, value] of entries) {\n    const lower = key.toLowerCase();\n    if (\n      lower === 'authorization' ||\n      lower === 'x-api-key' ||\n      lower === 'api-key'\n    ) {\n      sanitized[key] =\n        typeof value === 'string' && value.length > 8\n          ? value.slice(0, 4) + '...' + value.slice(-4)\n          : '[REDACTED]';\n    } else {\n      sanitized[key] = String(value);\n    }\n  }\n  return sanitized;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a body preview string, truncated to maxChars.\n */\nexport function bodyPreview(\n  body: BodyInit | null | undefined,\n  maxChars = 200000\n): string | undefined {\n  if (!body) return undefined;\n\n  const bodyStr =\n    typeof body === 'string'\n      ? body\n      : body instanceof ArrayBuffer || body instanceof Uint8Array\n        ? `[binary ${(body as ArrayBuffer).byteLength ?? (body as Uint8Array).length} bytes]`\n        : body instanceof URLSearchParams\n          ? body.toString()\n          : undefined;\n\n  if (bodyStr && typeof bodyStr === 'string') {\n    return bodyStr.length > maxChars\n      ? bodyStr.slice(0, maxChars) +\n          `... [truncated, total ${bodyStr.length} chars]`\n      : bodyStr;\n  }\n  return undefined;\n}\n\nexport interface VerboseFetchOptions {\n  /** Identifier for the caller (e.g. 'webfetch', 'auth-plugins', 'config') */\n  caller: string;\n  /** Maximum chars for response body preview (default: 200000) */\n  responseBodyMaxChars?: number;\n  /** Maximum chars for request body preview (default: 200000) */\n  requestBodyMaxChars?: number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Wrap a fetch function with verbose HTTP logging.\n *\n * When isVerbose() returns true, logs all HTTP requests and responses\n * as JSON objects. When verbose is false, returns a no-op passthrough.\n *\n * All logging is wrapped in try/catch so it never breaks the actual HTTP request.\n *\n * @param innerFetch - The fetch function to wrap (defaults to global fetch)\n * @param options - Configuration for the wrapper\n * @returns A wrapped fetch function with verbose logging\n */\nexport function createVerboseFetch(\n  innerFetch: typeof fetch = fetch,\n  options: VerboseFetchOptions\n): typeof fetch {\n  const {\n    caller,\n    responseBodyMaxChars = 200000,\n    requestBodyMaxChars = 200000,\n  } = options;\n\n  return async (\n    input: RequestInfo | URL,\n    init?: RequestInit\n  ): Promise<Response> => {\n    // Check verbose flag at call time, with env var fallback for resilience.\n    // Uses isVerbose() which checks both the in-memory flag and environment\n    // variables, preventing silent logging loss when the flag state is disrupted.\n    // See: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/227\n    if (!isVerbose()) {\n      return innerFetch(input, init);\n    }\n\n    globalHttpCallCount++;\n    const callNum = globalHttpCallCount;\n\n    const url =\n      typeof input === 'string'\n        ? input\n        : input instanceof URL\n          ? input.toString()\n          : input.url;\n    const method = init?.method ?? 'GET';\n\n    // Prepare request details for logging (error-resilient)\n    let sanitizedHdrs: Record<string, string> = {};\n    let reqBodyPreview: string | undefined;\n    try {\n      sanitizedHdrs = sanitizeHeaders(init?.headers as HeadersInit | undefined);\n      reqBodyPreview = bodyPreview(init?.body, requestBodyMaxChars);\n    } catch (prepError) {\n      log.warn('verbose logging: failed to prepare request details', {\n        caller,\n        error:\n          prepError instanceof Error ? prepError.message : String(prepError),\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Log request\n    log.info('HTTP request', {\n      caller,\n      callNum,\n      method,\n      url,\n      headers: sanitizedHdrs,\n      bodyPreview: reqBodyPreview,\n    });\n\n    const startMs = Date.now();\n    try {\n      const response = await innerFetch(input, init);\n      const durationMs = Date.now() - startMs;\n\n      // Log response\n      try {\n        log.info('HTTP response', {\n          caller,\n          callNum,\n          method,\n          url,\n          status: response.status,\n          statusText: response.statusText,\n          durationMs,\n          responseHeaders: Object.fromEntries(response.headers.entries()),\n        });\n      } catch {\n        // Ignore logging errors\n      }\n\n      // Log response body\n      const contentType = response.headers.get('content-type') ?? '';\n      const isStreaming =\n        contentType.includes('event-stream') ||\n        contentType.includes('octet-stream');\n\n      if (response.body) {\n        try {\n          if (isStreaming) {\n            const [sdkStream, logStream] = response.body.tee();\n\n            // Consume log stream asynchronously, tracking pending operations (#231)\n            pendingStreamLogs++;\n            (async () => {\n              try {\n                const reader = logStream.getReader();\n                const decoder = new TextDecoder();\n                let preview = '';\n                let truncated = false;\n                while (true) {\n                  const { done, value } = await reader.read();\n                  if (done) break;\n                  if (!truncated) {\n                    const chunk = decoder.decode(value, { stream: true });\n                    preview += chunk;\n                    if (preview.length > responseBodyMaxChars) {\n                      preview = preview.slice(0, responseBodyMaxChars);\n                      truncated = true;\n                    }\n                  }\n                }\n                log.info('HTTP response body (stream)', {\n                  caller,\n                  callNum,\n                  url,\n                  bodyPreview: truncated\n                    ? preview + `... [truncated]`\n                    : preview,\n                });\n              } catch {\n                // Ignore logging errors\n              } finally {\n                pendingStreamLogs--;\n              }\n            })();\n\n            return new Response(sdkStream, {\n              status: response.status,\n              statusText: response.statusText,\n              headers: response.headers,\n            });\n          } else {\n            const bodyText = await response.text();\n            const preview =\n              bodyText.length > responseBodyMaxChars\n                ? bodyText.slice(0, responseBodyMaxChars) +\n                  `... [truncated, total ${bodyText.length} chars]`\n                : bodyText;\n            log.info('HTTP response body', {\n              caller,\n              callNum,\n              url,\n              bodyPreview: preview,\n            });\n            return new Response(bodyText, {\n              status: response.status,\n              statusText: response.statusText,\n              headers: response.headers,\n            });\n          }\n        } catch {\n          // If body logging fails, return original response\n          return response;\n        }\n      }\n\n      return response;\n    } catch (error) {\n      const durationMs = Date.now() - startMs;\n      try {\n        log.error('HTTP request failed', {\n          caller,\n          callNum,\n          method,\n          url,\n          durationMs,\n          error:\n            error instanceof Error\n              ? {\n                  name: error.name,\n                  message: error.message,\n                  stack: error.stack,\n                  cause:\n                    error.cause instanceof Error\n                      ? error.cause.message\n                      : error.cause\n                        ? String(error.cause)\n                        : undefined,\n                }\n              : String(error),\n        });\n      } catch {\n        // Ignore logging errors\n      }\n      throw error;\n    }\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the current global HTTP call count (for testing).\n */\nexport function getHttpCallCount(): number {\n  return globalHttpCallCount;\n}\n\n/**\n * Reset the global HTTP call count (for testing).\n */\nexport function resetHttpCallCount(): void {\n  globalHttpCallCount = 0;\n}\n",

{number|null} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Wrap in Instance.provide for OpenCode infrastructure\n  // parseModelConfig must be called inside Instance.provide to access provider state\n  await Instance.provide({\n    directory: process.cwd(),\n    fn: HTTP\n        await runContinuousServerMode(\n          argv,\n          providerID,\n          modelID,\n          systemMessage,\n          appendSystemMessage,\n          jsonStandard,\n terminates (#213)\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent exiting',\n    hasError,\n    uptimeSeconds: Math.round(process.uptime()),\n  }));\n  process.exit(hasError ? 1 : 0);\n}\n\nasync function runServerMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard,\n  compactionModel\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // Start server like OpenCode does\n  const server = Server.listen({ port: 0, hostname: '127.0.0.1' });\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session\n      const localVerboseFetch = createVerboseFetch(fetch, { caller: 'cli' });\n      const createRes = await localVerboseFetch(\n        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session`,\n        {\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n        }\n      );\n      const session = await createRes.json();\n      sessionID = session.id;\n\n      if (!sessionID) {\n        throw new Error('Failed to create session');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session with specified model (default: opencode/grok-code)\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt (don't wait for response, events come via Bus)\n    fetch(\n      `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session/${sessionID}/message`,\n      {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          parts,\n          model: {\n            providerID,\n            modelID,\n          },\n          compactionModel,\n          system: systemMessage,\n          appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n        }),\n      }\n    ).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    server.stop();\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function runDirectMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard,\n  compactionModel\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // DIRECT MODE: Run in single process without server\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session directly\n      const session = await Session.createNext({\n        directory: process.cwd(),\n      });\n      sessionID = session.id;\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session directly\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt directly without HTTP\n    SessionPrompt.prompt({\n      sessionID,\n      parts,\n      model: {\n        providerID,\n        modelID,\n      },\n      compactionModel,\n      system: systemMessage,\n      appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n    }).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  try {\n    // Parse command line arguments with subcommands\n    const yargsInstance = yargs(hideBin(process.argv))\n      .scriptName('agent')\n      .usage('$0 [command] [options]')\n      .version(pkg.version)\n      // MCP subcommand\n      .command(McpCommand)\n      // Auth subcommand\n      .command(AuthCommand)\n      // Default command for agent mode (when no subcommand specified)\n      .command({\n        command: '$0',\n        describe: 'Run agent in interactive or stdin mode (default)',\n        builder: buildRunOptions,\n        handler: async (argv) => {\n          // Check both CLI flag and environment variable for compact JSON mode\n          const compactJson =\n            argv['compact-json'] === true || config.compactJson;\n\n          // Check if --prompt flag was provided\n          if (argv.prompt) {\n            // Direct prompt mode - bypass stdin entirely\n            const request = { message: argv.prompt };\n            await runAgentMode(argv, request);\n            return;\n          }\n\n          // Check if --disable-stdin was set without --prompt\n          if (argv['disable-stdin']) {\n            // Output a helpful message suggesting to use --prompt\n            outputError(\n              {\n                errorType: 'ValidationError',\n                message:\n                  'No prompt provided. Use -p/--prompt to specify a message, or remove --disable-stdin to read from stdin.',\n                hint: 'Example: agent -p \"Hello, how are you?\"',\n              },\n              compactJson\n            );\n            process.exit(1);\n          }\n\n          // Check if stdin is a TTY (interactive terminal)\n          if (process.stdin.isTTY) {\n            // Enter interactive terminal mode with continuous listening\n            const isInteractive = argv.interactive !== false;\n            const autoMerge = argv['auto-merge-queued-messages'] !== false;\n            const alwaysAcceptStdin = argv['always-accept-stdin'] !== false;\n\n            // Exit if --no-always-accept-stdin is set (single message mode not supported in TTY)\n            if (!alwaysAcceptStdin) {\n              outputError(\n                {\n                  errorType: 'ValidationError',\n                  message:\n                    'Single message mode (--no-always-accept-stdin) is not supported in interactive terminal mode.',\n                  hint: 'Use piped input or --prompt for single messages.',\n                },\n                compactJson\n              );\n              process.exit(1);\n            }\n\n            outputStatus(\n              {\n                type: 'status',\n                mode: 'interactive-terminal',\n                message:\n                  'Agent CLI in interactive terminal mode. Type your message and press Enter.',\n                hint: 'Press CTRL+C to exit. Use --help for options.',\n                acceptedFormats: isInteractive\n                  ? ['JSON object with \"message\" field', 'Plain text']\n                  : ['JSON object with \"message\" field'],\n                options: {\n                  interactive: isInteractive,\n                  autoMergeQueuedMessages: autoMerge,\n                  alwaysAcceptStdin,\n                  compactJson,\n                },\n              },\n              compactJson\n            );\n\n            // Use continuous mode for interactive terminal\n            await runContinuousAgentMode(argv);\n            return;\n          }\n\n          // stdin is piped - enter stdin listening mode\n          const isInteractive = argv.interactive !== false;\n          const autoMerge = argv['auto-merge-queued-messages'] !== false;\n          const alwaysAcceptStdin = argv['always-accept-stdin'] !== false;\n\n          outputStatus(\n            {\n              type: 'status',\n              mode: 'stdin-stream',\n              message: alwaysAcceptStdin\n                ? Accepts JSON and plain text input.',\n              hint: 'Press CTRL+C to exit. Use --help for options.',\n              acceptedFormats: isInteractive\n                ? In non-interactive mode (--no-interactive), only JSON input is accepted.',\n                  hint: 'Use --interactive to accept plain text, or provide valid JSON: {\"message\": \"your text\"}',\n                },\n                compactJson\n              );\n              process.exit(1);\n            }\n            // In interactive mode, treat as plain text message\n            request = {\n              message: trimmedInput,\n            };\n          }\n\n          // Output input confirmation in JSON format\n          outputInput(\n            {\n              raw: trimmedInput,\n              parsed: request,\n              format: isInteractive ? JSON envelope\nconst originalStderrWrite = process.stderr.write.bind(process.stderr);\nprocess.stderr.write = function (chunk, encoding, callback) {\n  const str = typeof chunk === 'string' ? chunk : chunk.toString();\n  const trimmed = str.trim();\n  if (!trimmed) {\n    return originalStderrWrite(chunk, encoding, callback);\n  }\n\n  // Check if it's already valid JSON\n  if process.exit(1);\n});\n\n/**\n * Read stdin with optional timeout.\n * @param {number|null} timeout - Timeout in milliseconds. If null, wait indefinitely until EOF.\n * @returns {Promise<string>} - The stdin content\n */\nfunction readStdinWithTimeout(timeout = null) {\n  return new Promise((resolve) => {\n    let data = '';\n    let hasData = false;\n    let timer = null;\n\n    const cleanup = () => {\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      process.stdin.removeListener('data', onData);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('end', onEnd);\n      process.stdin.removeListener('error', onError);\n    };\n\n    const onData = (chunk) => {\n      hasData = true;\n      if (timer) {\n        clearTimeout(timer);\n      }\n      data += chunk;\n    };\n\n    const onEnd = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve(data);\n    };\n\n    const onError = () => {\n      cleanup();\n      resolve('');\n    };\n\n    // Only set timeout if specified (not null)\n    if (timeout !== null) {\n      timer = setTimeout(() => {\n        if (!hasData) {\n          process.stdin.pause();\n          cleanup();\n          resolve('');\n        }\n      }, timeout);\n    }\n\n    process.stdin.on('data', onData);\n    process.stdin.on('end', onEnd);\n    process.stdin.on('error', onError);\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Read system message from files if specified\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n * @returns {object} - { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage }\n */\nasync function readSystemMessages(argv) {\n  let systemMessage = argv['system-message'];\n  let appendSystemMessage = argv['append-system-message'];\n\n  if (argv['system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      outputError({\n        errorType: 'FileNotFound',\n        message: `System message file not found: ${argv['system-message-file']}`,\n      });\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    systemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  if (argv['append-system-message-file']) {\n    const resolvedPath = require('path').resolve(\n      process.cwd(),\n      argv['append-system-message-file']\n    );\n    const file = Bun.file(resolvedPath);\n    if (!(await file.exists())) {\n      outputError({\n        errorType: 'FileNotFound',\n        message: `Append system message file not found: ${argv['append-system-message-file']}`,\n      });\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n    appendSystemMessage = await file.text();\n  }\n\n  return { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage };\n}\n\nasync function runAgentMode(argv, request) {\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (config.dryRun) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus({\n      type: 'error',\n      errorType: 'ValidationError',\n      message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. 1 : 0);\n}\n\n/**\n * Run agent in continuous stdin mode\n * Keeps accepting input until EOF or SIGINT\n * @param {object} argv - Command line arguments\n */\nasync function runContinuousAgentMode(argv) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n  // Log version and command info in verbose mode using lazy logging\n  Log.Default.info(() => ({\n    message: 'Agent started (continuous mode)',\n    version: pkg.version,\n    command: process.argv.join(' '),\n    workingDirectory: process.cwd(),\n    scriptPath: import.meta.path,\n  }));\n  if (config.dryRun) {\n    Log.Default.info(() => ({\n      message: 'Dry run mode enabled',\n      mode: 'dry-run',\n    }));\n  }\n\n  // Validate and get JSON standard\n  const jsonStandard = argv['json-standard'];\n  if (!isValidJsonStandard(jsonStandard)) {\n    outputStatus(\n      {\n        type: 'error',\n        message: `Invalid JSON standard: ${jsonStandard}. Use \"opencode\" or \"claude\".`,\n      },\n      compactJson\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  const { systemMessage, appendSystemMessage } = await readSystemMessages(argv);\n\n  // Wrap in function runServerMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard,\n  compactionModel\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // Start server like OpenCode does\n  const server = Server.listen({ port: 0, hostname: '127.0.0.1' });\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const resumeInfo = await resolveResumeSession(argv, compactJson);\n\n    let sessionID;\n\n    if (resumeInfo) {\n      // Use the resumed/forked session\n      sessionID = resumeInfo.sessionID;\n    } else {\n      // Create a new session\n      const localVerboseFetch = createVerboseFetch(fetch, { caller: 'cli' });\n      const createRes = await localVerboseFetch(\n        `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session`,\n        {\n          method: 'POST',\n          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n        }\n      );\n      const session = await createRes.json();\n      sessionID = session.id;\n\n      if (!sessionID) {\n        throw new Error('Failed to create session');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: eventUnsub, idlePromise: eventPromise } =\n      createBusEventSubscription({\n        sessionID,\n        eventHandler,\n        onError: () => {\n          hasError = true;\n        },\n      });\n    unsub = eventUnsub;\n\n    // Send message to session with specified model (default: opencode/grok-code)\n    const message = request.message || 'hi';\n    const parts = [{ type: 'text', text: message }];\n\n    // Start the prompt (don't wait for response, events come via Bus)\n    fetch(\n      `http://${server.hostname}:${server.port}/session/${sessionID}/message`,\n      {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n          parts,\n          model: {\n            providerID,\n            modelID,\n          },\n          compactionModel,\n          system: systemMessage,\n          appendSystem: appendSystemMessage,\n        }),\n      }\n    ).catch((error) => {\n      hasError = true;\n      eventHandler.output({\n        type: 'error',\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        sessionID,\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n    });\n\n    // Wait for session to become idle\n    await eventPromise;\n  } finally {\n    // Always clean up resources\n    if (unsub) {\n      unsub();\n    }\n    server.stop();\n    await Instance.dispose();\n  }\n}\n\nasync function runDirectMode(\n  argv,\n  request,\n  providerID,\n  modelID,\n  systemMessage,\n  appendSystemMessage,\n  jsonStandard,\n  compactionModel\n) {\n  const compactJson = argv['compact-json'] === true;\n\n  // DIRECT MODE: Run in single process without server\n  let unsub = null;\n\n  try {\n    // Check if we should resume an existing session\n    const {\n      // Create a new session directly\n      const session = await Session.createNext({\n        directory: process.cwd(),\n      });\n      sessionID = session.id;\n    }\n\n    // Create event handler for the selected JSON standard\n    const eventHandler = createEventHandler(jsonStandard, sessionID);\n\n    // Subscribe to all bus events and output in selected format\n    const { unsub: compactJson =\n            argv['compact-json'] === true || config.compactJson;\n\n          // Check if --prompt flag was provided\n          if (argv.prompt) {\n            // Direct prompt mode - bypass stdin entirely\n            const request = { message: argv.prompt };\n            await runAgentMode(argv, request);\n            return;\n          }\n\n          // Check if --disable-stdin errorType: 'ValidationError',\n                message:\n                  'No prompt provided. Use -p/--prompt to specify a message, or remove --disable-stdin to read from stdin.',\n                hint: 'Example: agent -p \"Hello, how are you?\"',\n              },\n              compactJson\n            );\n            process.exit(1);\n          }\n\n          // Check if stdin is a TTY (interactive terminal)\n          if (process.stdin.isTTY) {\n            // Enter interactive terminal mode with continuous listening\n            const isInteractive = argv.interactive !== false;\n            const autoMerge = argv['auto-merge-queued-messages'] !== false;\n            const alwaysAcceptStdin = argv['always-accept-stdin'] !== false;\n\n            // Exit if --no-always-accept-stdin is set (single message mode not supported in TTY)\n            if (!alwaysAcceptStdin) {\n              outputError(\n                {\n                  errorType: 'ValidationError',\n                  message:\n                    'Single message mode (--no-always-accept-stdin) is not supported in interactive terminal mode.',\n                  hint: 'Use piped input or --prompt for single messages.',\n                },\n                compactJson\n              );\n              process.exit(1);\n            }\n\n            outputStatus(\n              {\n                type: 'status',\n                mode: 'interactive-terminal',\n                message:\n                  'Agent CLI in interactive terminal mode. Type your message and press Enter.',\n                hint: 'Press CTRL+C to exit. Use --help for options.',\n                acceptedFormats: isInteractive\n                  ? 'Agent CLI in continuous listening mode. Accepts JSON and plain text input.'\n                : 'Agent CLI in single-message mode. Accepts JSON and plain text input.',\n              hint: 'Press CTRL+C to exit. Use --help for options.',\n              acceptedFormats: isInteractive\n                ? informational, not an error\n        // Display help text on stdout (industry standard: git, gh, npm all use stdout for help)\n        if (msg) {\n          outputHelp({\n            message: msg,\n            hint: stripAnsi(yargs.help()),\n          });\n          process.exit(0);\n        }\n      })\n      .help();\n\n    // Parse arguments (handlers will be called automatically)\n    await yargsInstance.argv;\n  } catch (error) {\n    hasError = true;\n    outputError({\n      timestamp: Date.now(),\n      errorType: error instanceof Error ? error.name : 'Error',\n      message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      stack: error instanceof Error ? error.stack : undefined,\n    });\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n}\n\nmain();\n",

The agent's retry logic does not currently retry 500 errors on the compaction endpoint.\n\n## References\n\n- Agent issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/231\n- Agent PR: https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/pull/233\nEOF\n)\""

      "content": "import { test, expect, describe, mock, beforeAll, afterAll } from 'bun:test';\n\n/**\n * Unit tests for strict model validation\n *\n * Issue #194: AI agent terminated prematurely with \"reason\": \"unknown\"\n * Issue #196: Model substitution and zero-token provider failures\n *\n * Root causes (Issue #194):\n * 1. Model requested (`glm-4.7-free`) was not found, but system silently fell back to default\n * 2. finishReason was undefined, causing \"unknown\" which triggered premature loop exit\n *\n * Root causes (Issue #196):\n * 1. Yargs under Bun returned default model instead of CLI argument (model substitution)\n * 2. Provider returned zero tokens with \"unknown\" finish reason (silent API failure)\n * 3. No validation of model existence for explicit provider/model format\n *\n * Fixes implemented:\n * 1. parseModelWithResolution now throws ModelNotFoundError instead of silent fallback\n * 2. processor.ts infers 'tool-calls' finish reason from pending tool calls\n * 3. prompt.ts continues loop when finish is 'unknown' but tool calls were made\n * 4. parseModelConfig always prefers CLI value over yargs (#196)\n * 5. parseModelConfig throws on invalid provider/model format instead of defaulting (#196)\n * 6. prompt.ts detects zero-token unknown responses as provider failures (#196)\n * 7. parseModelConfig warns when model not found in explicit provider (#196)\n *\n * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/194\n * @see https://github.com/link-assistant/agent/issues/196\n */\n\ndescribe('Model validation - parseModelWithResolution', () => {\n  // Note: These tests would require mocking the Instance.state and Provider module\n  // For now, we document the expected behavior\n\n  test.todo(\n    'should throw ModelNotFoundError for non-existent short model name'\n  );\n  test.todo('should resolve known short model names to correct provider');\n  test.todo('should parse explicit provider/model format correctly');\n  test.todo('should include helpful suggestion in error message');\n});\n\ndescribe('Finish reason inference - processor.ts', () => {\n  // These tests document the expected behavior of the finish reason inference\n\n  test('should infer tool-calls when finishReason is undefined but tool calls exist', () => {\n    // The processor.ts code infers 'tool-calls' when:\n    // 1. rawFinishReason === undefined\n    // 2. Object.keys(toolcalls).length > 0 (pending tool calls exist)\n\n    // This is a documentation test - the actual logic is in processor.ts:\n    // if (rawFinishReason === undefined) {\n    //   const pendingToolCallCount = Object.keys(toolcalls).length;\n    //   if (pendingToolCallCount > 0) {\n    //     rawFinishReason = 'tool-calls';\n    //   }\n    // }\n    expect(true).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  test('should preserve original finishReason when defined', () => {\n    // If finishReason is defined (e.g., 'stop'), don't override it\n    expect(true).toBe(true);\n  });\n});\n\ndescribe('Loop exit condition - prompt.ts', () => {\n  // These tests document the expected behavior of the loop exit condition\n\n  test('should continue loop when finish is unknown but tool calls exist', () => {\n    // The prompt.ts code continues the loop when:\n    // 1. lastAssistant.finish === 'unknown'\n    // 2. Parts include tool parts with status 'completed' or 'running'\n    //\n    // This is a documentation test - the actual logic is in prompt.ts:\n    // if (lastAssistant.finish === 'unknown') {\n    //   const hasToolCalls = lastAssistantParts?.some(\n    //     (p) => p.type === 'tool' && (p.state.status === 'completed' || p.state.status === 'running')\n    //   );\n    //   if (hasToolCalls) {\n    //     // Don't break - continue the loop\n    //   }\n    // }\n    expect(true).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  test('should exit loop when finish is unknown and no tool calls', () => {\n    // If finish is 'unknown' but there are no tool calls, exit the loop\n    expect(true).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  test('should exit loop immediately when finish is unknown with zero tokens (#196)', () => {\n    // Issue #196: When provider returns zero tokens with unknown finish reason,\n    // this indicates a complete API communication failure.\n    // The code now detects this condition BEFORE checking for tool calls:\n    //\n    // if (tokens.input === 0 && tokens.output === 0 && tokens.reasoning === 0) {\n    //   log.error({ message: 'provider returned zero tokens...' });\n    //   break;\n    // }\n    //\n    // This is important because:\n    // 1. Zero tokens means the provider didn't process the request at all\n    // 2. Any \"tool calls\" in the message would be from a previous step, not this one\n    // 3. Continuing the loop would just waste time and potentially cause infinite loops\n\n    // Simulate the zero-token check\n    const tokens = {\n      input: 0,\n      output: 0,\n      reasoning: 0,\n      cache: { read: 0, write: 0 },\n    };\n    const isZeroTokenResponse =\n      tokens.input === 0 && tokens.output === 0 && tokens.reasoning === 0;\n    expect(isZeroTokenResponse).toBe(true);\n\n    // Non-zero tokens should not trigger this check\n    const normalTokens = {\n      input: 1500,\n      output: 200,\n      reasoning: 0,\n      cache: { read: 0, write: 0 },\n    };\n    const isNormalResponse =\n      normalTokens.input === 0 &&\n      normalTokens.output === 0 &&\n      normalTokens.reasoning === 0;\n    expect(isNormalResponse).toBe(false);\n  });\n\n  test('should exit loop when finish is stop/end-turn/etc', () => {\n    // Normal completion reasons should exit the loop\n    const exitReasons = ['stop', 'end-turn', 'max_tokens', 'content_filter'];\n    expect(exitReasons.includes('stop')).toBe(true);\n    expect(exitReasons.includes('end-turn')).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  test('should continue loop when finish is tool-calls', () => {\n    // 'tool-calls' finish reason means we need to execute tools and continue\n    expect('tool-calls' !== 'stop').toBe(true);\n  });\n});\n\ndescribe('ModelNotFoundError', () => {\n  // Test the error structure\n\n  test('should include providerID and modelID in error data', () => {\n    // The error should contain:\n    // - Kimi K2.5 makes tool calls but returns undefined finishReason\n    // 5. toFinishReason converts undefined -> 'unknown'\n    // 6. Loop exits because 'unknown' !== 'tool-calls'\n    // 7. User can choose correct model\n\n    // After fix - Scenario 2: Provider returns undefined finishReason\n    // 1. User runs: agent --model opencode/kimi-k2.5-free\n    // 2. Kimi K2.5 makes tool calls, returns undefined finishReason\n    // 3. processor.ts: Detects pending tool calls -> infers 'tool-calls'\n    // 4. Loop continues to execute tools\n    // 5. Agent completes tasks successfully\n\n    // After fix - Scenario 3: finishReason inference fails but tool calls exist\n    // 1. User runs: agent --model some-provider/some-model\n    // 2. Model makes tool calls, returns undefined finishReason\n    // 3. processor.ts: No pending tool calls tracked -> finishReason stays 'unknown'\n    // 4. prompt.ts: Detects tool parts in message -> continues loop anyway\n    // 5. Agent completes tasks successfully\n    expect(true).toBe(true);\n  });\n});\n\ndescribe('Provider.state export - Issue #198', () => {\n  test('Provider.state should be exported and callable', async () => {\n    // Issue #198: Provider.state was not exported from the namespace,\n    // causing model-config.js validation to always fail silently.\n    // After fix: state is exported with `export const state = ...`\n    const { Provider } = await import('../src/provider/provider.ts');\n    expect(typeof Provider.state).toBe('function');\n  });\n\n  test('zero-token error should include model info and publish error event', () => {\n    // Issue #198: When provider returns zero tokens, the error message\n    // should include the requested model ID, provider ID, and responded model ID.\n    // It should also publish a Bus error event for JSON standard output.\n    const model = {\n      providerID: 'opencode',\n      requestedModelID: 'kimi-k2.5-free',\n      respondedModelID: 'moonshotai/kimi-k2.5',\n    };\n    const errorMessage =\n      `Provider returned zero tokens with unknown finish reason. ` +\n      `Requested model: ${model.requestedModelID} ` +\n      `(provider: ${model.providerID}). ` +\n      `Responded model: ${model.respondedModelID}. ` +\n      `This usually indicates the provider failed to process the request. ` +\n      `Check provider status, model availability, and API keys.`;\n\n    expect(errorMessage).toContain('kimi-k2.5-free');\n    expect(errorMessage).toContain('opencode');\n    expect(errorMessage).toContain('moonshotai/kimi-k2.5');\n    expect(errorMessage).toContain('Check provider status');\n  });\n});\n\ndescribe('Integration scenarios - Issue #196', () => {\n  test('documents the model substitution failure', () => {\n    // Timeline from real incident:\n    // 1. User runs: solve --model glm-4.7-free\n    // 2. Solve script executes: agent --model opencode/glm-4.7-free\n    // 3. Yargs under Bun parses --model but returns DEFAULT 'opencode/kimi-k2.5-free'\n    // 4. getModelFromProcessArgv() should catch this but apparently returned null\n    // 5. Agent sends request to opencode with kimi-k2.5-free (wrong model!)\n    // 6. Provider returns zero tokens with \"reason: unknown\"\n    // 7. Agent exits silently without doing any work\n\n    // With our fix:\n    // Step 3-4: getModelFromProcessArgv() always overrides yargs when available\n    // Step 5: Model existence validation warns if model not found in provider\n    // Step 6: Zero-token detection logs clear error about provider failure\n    expect(true).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  test('documents zero-token provider failure detection', () => {\n    // When provider returns:\n    //   { reason: \"unknown\", tokens: { input: 0, output: 0, reasoning: 0 } }\n    // This means the provider completely failed to process the request.\n    //\n    // Before fix: Agent silently exits the loop\n    // After fix: Agent logs error with:\n    //   - Clear message about provider failure\n    //   - Token counts\n    //   - Model information\n    //   - Hint about checking provider status\n    //   - Link to issue #196\n    expect(true).toBe(true);\n  });\n});\n",
      "numLines": 318,
      "startLine": 1,
      "totalLines": 318
    }
  }

sanitizeHeaders', () => {\n  test('masks long authorization header values', () => {\n    const result = sanitizeHeaders({\n      Authorization: 'Bearer sk-abcdefgh12345678',\n      'Content-Type': 'application/json',\n    });\n    expect(result['Authorization']).toBe('Bear...5678');\n    expect(result['Content-Type']).toBe('application/json');\n  });\n\n  test('masks x-api-key header', () => {\n    const result = sanitizeHeaders({\n      'x-api-key': 'sk-an**********************-here',\n    });\n    expect(result['x-api-key']).toBe('sk-a...here');\n  });\n\n  test('masks api-key header', () => {\n    const result = sanitizeHeaders({\n      'api-key': 'super-secret-key-12345',\n    });\n    expect(result['api-key']).toBe('supe...2345');\n  });\n\n  test('redacts short keys entirely', () => {\n    const result = sanitizeHeaders({\n      Authorization: 'short',\n    });\n    expect(result['Authorization']).toBe('[REDACTED]');\n  });\n\n  test('preserves non-sensitive headers', () => {\n    const result = sanitizeHeaders({\n      'Content-Type': 'application/json',\n      'User-Agent': 'test-agent/1.0',\n      Accept: 'text/html',\n    });\n    expect(result['Content-Type']).toBe('application/json');\n    expect(result['User-Agent']).toBe('test-agent/1.0');\n    expect(result['Accept']).toBe('text/html');\n  });\n\n  test('handles Headers object', () => {\n    const headers = new Headers();\n    headers.set('authorization', 'Bearer long-token-value-here');\n    headers.set('content-type', 'application/json');\n    const result = sanitizeHeaders(headers);\n    expect(result['authorization']).toBe('Bear...here');\n    expect(result['content-type']).toBe('application/json');\n  });\n\n  test('handles undefined headers', () => {\n    const result = sanitizeHeaders(undefined);\n    expect(result).toEqual({});\n  });\n\n  test('handles empty object', () => {\n    const result = sanitizeHeaders({});\n    expect(result).toEqual({});\n  });\n});\n\ndescribe('verbose-fetch - [truncated, total 250000 chars]');\n    expect(result!.length).toBeLessThan(250000);\n  });\n\n  test('respects custom maxChars', () => {\n    const body = 'x'.repeat(200);\n    const result = bodyPreview(body, 100);\n    expect(result).toContain('... [truncated, total 200 chars]');\n  });\n\n  test('handles undefined body', () => {\n    expect(bodyPreview(undefined)).toBeUndefined();\n  });\n\n  test('handles null body', () => {\n expect(getHttpCallCount()).toBe(2);\n  });\n\n  test('logs request and response when verbose is enabled', async () => {\n    setVerbose(true);\n    const mockFetch = async (input: any, init?: any) =>  new Response(null, { status: 204, statusText: 'No Content' });\n    const vf = createVerboseFetch(mockFetch as typeof fetch, {\n      caller: 'test-nobody',\n    });\n    const response = await    // After fix: \"[debug] verbose HTTP logging active for provider: opencode\"\n    //\n    // The outer solver's error detection pattern matches \"[verbose]\" messages\n    // on stderr as errors, causing false positive error detection.\n\n    const oldMessage = '[verbose] HTTP logging active for provider: opencode';\n    const newMessage =\n      '[debug] verbose HTTP logging active for provider: opencode';\n\n    // The old message might be matched by broad error detection\n    expect(oldMessage.startsWith('[verbose]')).toBe(true);\n\n    // The new message uses [debug] prefix\n .catch((migrationError) => log.error({\n    //   message: 'failed to run migration',\n    //   index,\n    //   error: { name, message, stack }\n    // }))\n    //\n    // This ensures migration failures are diagnosable from logs.\n\n    const migrationError = new Error('ENOENT: no such file or directory');\n    const logEntry = {\n      message: 'failed to run migration',\n      index: 0,\n Agent operates with wrong model for entire session\n\n    // With fixes:\n    // Step 3-4: model-config.js now THROWS instead of warning\n    // Step 5-6: provider.ts now THROWS ModelNotFoundError\n    // Result: User sees clear error message and can fix the model argument\n    expect(true).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  test('documents the missing compaction response log', () => {\n    // Original issue:\n    // 1. Agent sends compaction POST at 12:37:08.713Z\n    // 2. Agent exits at 12:37:08.734Z (21ms later)\n    // 3. Async stream logging IIFE never completes\n    // 4. HTTP response body never logged\n\n    // With fix:\n    // - pendingStreamLogs counter tracks active async log operations\n    // - Process exit handler warns about any pending operations\n    // - This makes the gap visible in logs instead of silently lost\n    expect(true).toBe(true);\n  });\n});\n"

    error:\n        migrationError instanceof Error\n          ? {\n              name: migrationError.name,\n              message: migrationError.message,\n              stack: migrationError.stack,\n            }\n          : String(migrationError),\n    };\n\n    expect(logEntry.error).toHaveProperty('name', 'Error');\n    expect(logEntry.error).toHaveProperty(\n      'message',\n      'ENOENT: no such file or directory'\n    );\n    expect(logEntry.error).toHaveProperty('stack');\n  });\n\n  test('should handle non-Error migration failures', () => {\n    const migrationError = 'string error';\n    const logEntry = {\n      message: 'failed to run migration',\n      index: 0,\n      error:\n        migrationError instanceof Error\n          ? {\n              name: migrationError.name,\n              message: migrationError.message,\n              stack: migrationError.stack,\n            }\n          : String(migrationError),\n    };\n\n    expect(logEntry.error).toBe('string error');\n  });\n});\n\ndescribe('Integration scenario - Issue #231 full reproduction', () => {\n  test('documents the original kimi-k2.5-free substitution timeline', () => {\n    // Original issue timeline:\n    // 1. Outer solver: --model kimi-k2.5-free\n    // 2. Solver expands to: --model opencode/kimi-k2.5-free\n    // 3. model-config.js: provider \"opencode\" found, but model \"kimi-k2.5-free\" NOT found\n    // 4. model-config.js: WARNS \"will attempt anyway\" (BUG — should have thrown)\n    // 5. provider.ts getModel(): model not in catalog after refresh\n    // 6. provider.ts: creates synthetic fallback info (BUG — should have thrown)\n    // 7. SDK sends request with model \"kimi-k2.5-free\" to OpenCode API\n    // 8. OpenCode API silently routes to \"minimax-m2.5-free\" (default)\n    // 9. Agent operates with wrong model for entire session\n\n    // With fixes:\n    // Step 3-4: model-config.js now THROWS instead of warning\n    // Step 5-6: provider.ts now THROWS ModelNotFoundError\n    // Result: User sees clear error message and can fix the model argument\n    expect(true).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  test('documents the missing compaction response log', () => {\n    // Original issue:\n    // 1. Agent sends compaction POST at 12:37:08.713Z\n    // 2. Agent exits at 12:37:08.734Z (21ms later)\n    // 3. Async stream logging IIFE never completes\n    // 4. HTTP response body never logged\n\n    // With fix:\n    // - pendingStreamLogs counter tracks active async log operations\n    // - Process exit handler warns about any pending operations\n    // - This makes the gap visible in logs instead of silently lost\n    expect(true).toBe(true);\n  });\n});\n",
    "structuredPatch": [],
    "originalFile": null
  }

    "originalFile": "# @link-assistant/agent\n\n## 0.19.0\n\n### Minor Changes\n\n- effbcfb: feat: centralize agent config with lino-arguments, always log resolved config (#227)\n  - Added `lino-arguments` for unified env var resolution (case-insensitive, .lenv support)\n  - Created centralized `AgentConfig` module as single source of truth for all configuration\n  - All env vars resolved via `getenv()` from lino-arguments (CLI args > env vars > .lenv > defaults)\n  - Always log resolved configuration as JSON at startup for debugging\n  - Moved all direct `process.env` reads (MCP, read tool) into centralized Flag module\n  - `--verbose` is now the most reliable flag: triple-checked via in-memory, AgentConfig, and env var\n  - Removed all `OPENCODE_*` env var support; use `LINK_ASSISTANT_AGENT_*` exclusively\n\n## 0.18.3\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- b3989c2: fix: use compaction model (--compaction-model) for summarization to avoid doubling rate-limit pressure on free-tier main models (#223)\n\n## 0.18.1\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- e66584f: fix: enable dual HTTP logging (global fetch patch + provider-level wrapper) for complete verbose mode coverage (#221)\n\n## 0.18.0\n\n### Minor Changes\n\n- 74cb3e1: Add --compaction-model (default: opencode/gpt-5-nano) and --compaction-safety-margin (default: 15%) CLI options. When the compaction model has a larger context window than the base model, the safety margin is automatically removed, allowing 100% usage of the base model's usable context. This extends effective working context by ~18% for free tier models at zero cost.\n\n## 0.17.0\n\n### Minor Changes\n\n- 99b34ec: Enable --summarize-session by default and use the same model as --model for session summarization. Add 15% safety margin to compaction overflow detection, context diagnostics to step-finish JSON output, and detailed logging for overflow checks.\n\n## 0.16.18\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 7884084: feat: improve verbose HTTP logging reliability (#215)\n  - Add diagnostic breadcrumb log (\"verbose HTTP logging active\") on first HTTP call per provider to confirm the fetch wrapper is in the chain\n  - Pass Bun's non-standard `verbose: true` option to fetch() when verbose mode is enabled, enabling detailed connection debugging for socket errors\n  - Include stack trace and error.cause in HTTP request failed log entries for better debugging of connection failures\n  - Add case study documenting the \"socket connection closed unexpectedly\" error analysis\n\n## 0.16.17\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 9605cfd: fix: prevent agent process leaks with event loop fixes and ESLint rules (#213)\n  - Fix setTimeout/setInterval in retry-fetch.ts, session/retry.ts, and util/timeout.ts to use .unref() so timers don't prevent process exit\n  - Fix Bun.serve() idleTimeout from 0 (infinite) to 255 (default) to prevent keeping event loop alive\n  - Fix setTimeout in continuous-mode.js waitForPending to use .unref()\n  - Use process.once('SIGINT') instead of process.on('SIGINT') to prevent handler accumulation\n  - Fix missing error listener removal in input-queue.js stop()\n  - Add eslint-plugin-promise for detecting dangling/floating promises\n  - Add no-restricted-syntax ESLint rules to warn on process.on('SIGINT'/'SIGTERM') — prefer process.once()\n  - Remove AGENT_PROCESS_LIFETIME_TIMEOUT (agents can run for hours, global timeout is not appropriate)\n  - Add --retry-on-rate-limits flag (use --no-retry-on-rate-limits to disable AI API rate limit retries)\n  - Move integration tests to tests/integration/ to prevent bulk running; default bun test runs only unit tests\n\n## 0.16.16\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 008aa52: fix: HTTP verbose logging and anthropic provider usage fallback (#211)\n\n  Two fixes for issues observed when running the Agent CLI with `--verbose`\n  mode and the `opencode/minimax-m2.5-free` model:\n  1. **HTTP request/response logging not appearing in verbose mode**: The\n     lazy log callback pattern (`log.info(() => ({...}))`) passed through\n     the `log-lazy` npm package, adding indirection that could lose output\n     when the CLI runs as a subprocess. Changed all 5 HTTP log call sites\n     to use direct calls (`log.info('msg', data)`) since the verbose check\n     is already done at the top of the wrapper.\n  2. **\"Provider returned zero tokens with unknown finish reason\" error**:\n     When using `@ai-sdk/anthropic` SDK with a custom baseURL (opencode\n     proxy), the standard AI SDK usage object is empty but\n     `providerMetadata.anthropic.usage` contains valid token data with\n     snake_case keys (`input_tokens`, `output_tokens`). Added an anthropic\n     metadata fallback in `getUsage()` to extract tokens from this\n     metadata, similar to the existing OpenRouter fallback.\n\n## 0.16.14\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 1c5ffd1: fix: centralize default model constant and update from kimi-k2.5-free to minimax-m2.5-free (#208)\n\n  The yargs default model in `index.js`, the OAuth fallback check in\n  `model-config.js`, and the task tool fallback in `task.ts` referenced\n  `opencode/kimi-k2.5-free`, which was discontinued on the OpenCode Zen\n  provider. Runs that did not pass `--model` explicitly (or where the\n  yargs caching bug #192 caused the CLI argument to be silently dropped)\n  would attempt to use the removed model and fail with a 401 ModelError.\n\n  Additionally, the default model was hardcoded in multiple files, making\n  future updates error-prone (as demonstrated by this issue).\n\n  Changes:\n  - `js/src/cli/defaults.ts`: new file exporting `DEFAULT_MODEL`,\n    `DEFAULT_PROVIDER_ID`, and `DEFAULT_MODEL_ID` constants — the single\n    source of truth for the default model\n  - `js/src/index.js`: import and use `DEFAULT_MODEL` constant for the\n    `--model` yargs default\n  - `js/src/cli/model-config.js`: import and use `DEFAULT_PROVIDER_ID`\n    and `DEFAULT_MODEL_ID` constants in the `--use-existing-claude-oauth`\n    check and error messages\n  - `js/src/tool/task.ts`: import and use `DEFAULT_PROVIDER_ID` and\n    `DEFAULT_MODEL_ID` constants as the fallback model\n\n## 0.16.13\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 4377c1d: fix: detect model-not-supported errors from provider response body (#208)\n\n  When the OpenCode provider (and similar OpenRouter-compatible proxies) removes\n  or restricts access to a model, the API returns HTTP 401 with a response body\n  like `{\"type\":\"error\",\"error\":{\"type\":\"ModelError\",\"message\":\"Model X not\nsupported\"}}`. Without special handling this looks identical to a real\n  authentication failure, making the root cause hard to diagnose.\n\n  The fix adds `SessionProcessor.isModelNotSupportedError()` which parses the\n  response body and detects the `ModelError` pattern from OpenCode/OpenRouter.\n  When detected, a dedicated error log entry is emitted that:\n  - Clearly labels the error as a model-availability issue, NOT an auth error\n  - Includes the providerID, modelID, and full response body\n  - Suggests using `--model <provider>/<model-id>` to specify an alternative\n  - Links to the case study for further investigation\n\n  The fix also adds 11 unit tests covering nested/flat JSON formats, real auth\n  errors (should not be flagged), plain-text fallback detection, and edge cases.\n\n  Root cause documented in `docs/case-studies/issue-208/` with the full\n  1920-line log from the failing run.\n\n## 0.16.12\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- d843fb2: fix: check verbose flag at HTTP call time, not SDK creation time (#206)\n\n  The verbose HTTP logging wrapper now checks `Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE` when each\n  HTTP request is made, instead of when the provider SDK is created. Previously,\n  the wrapper was conditionally installed at SDK creation time using\n  `if (Flag.OPENCODE_VERBOSE)`, which meant that if the SDK was cached before\n  the `--verbose` flag was processed by the CLI middleware, no HTTP logging would\n  occur for the entire session.\n\n  The fix always installs the fetch wrapper but makes it a no-op passthrough\n  (single boolean check) when verbose mode is disabled, ensuring zero overhead\n  in normal operation and reliable logging when `--verbose` is enabled.\n\n## 0.16.11\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 5dbfc0e: feat: log HTTP response body in verbose mode for debugging provider failures (#204)\n\n  When `--verbose` is enabled, the raw HTTP response body from LLM providers is now\n  also logged. For streaming (SSE) responses, the stream is tee'd so the AI SDK receives\n  the full response while a preview (up to 4000 chars) is logged asynchronously. For\n  non-streaming responses, the body is buffered, logged, and the Response is reconstructed\n  transparently.\n\n  This provides the missing visibility needed to diagnose issues like empty responses,\n  malformed SSE events, or error messages from providers like opencode/kimi-k2.5-free.\n\n## 0.16.10\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 8abdac4: Fix model resolution failures and ensure JSON-only output (#200)\n  - No auxiliary models are loaded when all auxiliary tasks are disabled\n  - No extra tokens are consumed for title generation or session summarization\n  - Users can explicitly enable these features when needed\n\n  ### Renamed Fields\n\n  The model info schema now uses clearer field names:\n  - `modelID` → `requestedModelID` (the model you asked for)\n  - `responseModelId` → `respondedModelID` (the actual model that responded)\n\n  This helps clarify the distinction between what was requested and what actually\n  processed the request.\n\n  Fixes #179\n\n## 0.13.5\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 34f4877: Add unit suffixes to all time-related log fields for clarity\n\n  Standardized all time-related log fields in retry-fetch.ts and session/retry.ts\n  to include the Ms (milliseconds) suffix, making logs crystal clear and eliminating\n  confusion about time units.\n\n  Changes:\n  - retry-fetch.ts: Renamed delay→delayMs, elapsed→elapsedMs, remainingTimeout→remainingTimeoutMs,\n    minInterval→minIntervalMs, maxRetryTimeout→maxRetryTimeoutMs, backoffDelay→backoffDelayMs,\n    maxBackoffDelay→maxBackoffDelayMs\n  - session/retry.ts: Renamed elapsedTime→elapsedTimeMs, maxTime→maxTimeMs,\n    backoffDelay→backoffDelayMs, maxBackoffDelay→maxBackoffDelayMs, maxCap→maxCapMs\n\n  This is a logging-only change with no functional impact. All tests pass.\n\n  Fixes #181\n\n- b5d4705: fix: resolve CLI warnings by fixing root causes\n  - Add package staleness check to refresh 'latest' packages after 24 hours\n    - Fixes specificationVersion v2 warning by ensuring @ai-sdk/openai-compatible is updated to v2.x (with v3 spec support)\n  - Change models.dev cache fallback message from 'warn' to 'info' level\n    - Using bundled data is expected fallback behavior, not a warning condition\n\n## 0.13.4\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 08d9e95: Fix rate limit wait being aborted by provider timeout (#183)\n\n  When a rate limit (429) response includes a long retry-after header (e.g., 15 hours),\n  the agent would fail with \"The operation timed out.\" after only 5 minutes. This occurred\n  because the rate limit wait shared the same AbortSignal as the provider timeout.\n\n  Solution: Use isolated AbortController for rate limit waits that only respects the global\n  AGENT_RETRY_TIMEOUT (default 7 days), not provider-level timeouts.\n\n  Key changes:\n  - Added createIsolatedRateLimitSignal() for rate limit waits\n  - Rate limit waits now periodically check for user cancellation (every 10s)\n  - Proper cleanup of event listeners and timers to prevent memory leaks\n  - Added comprehensive timeout hierarchy documentation\n\n## 0.13.3\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- Fix rate limit wait being aborted by provider timeout (#183)\n\n  Problem: When a rate limit (429) response includes a long retry-after header\n  (e.g., 15 hours), the agent would fail with \"The operation timed out.\"\n  after only 5 minutes.\n\n  Solution: Use isolated AbortController for rate limit waits that only respects\n  the global AGENT_RETRY_TIMEOUT (default 7 days), not provider-level timeouts.\n\n  Key changes:\n  - Added createIsolatedRateLimitSignal() for rate limit waits\n  - Rate limit waits now periodically check for user cancellation (every 10s)\n  - Proper cleanup of event listeners and timers to prevent memory leaks\n  - Added comprehensive timeout hierarchy documentation\n\n  Fixes #183\n\n## 0.13.2\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- a58734d: Fix ProviderModelNotFoundError for newly added models like kimi-k2.5-free\n\n  When the models.dev cache was missing or stale, the refresh() call was not awaited,\n  causing the agent to use outdated/empty cache data. This led to ProviderModelNotFoundError\n  for models that exist in the remote API but weren't in the local cache.\n\n  The fix ensures that:\n  - When no cache exists (first run): await refresh() before proceeding\n  - When cache is stale (> 1 hour old): await refresh() to get updated model list\n  - When cache is fresh: trigger background refresh but use cached data immediately\n\n  Fixes #175\n\n## 0.13.1\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 198d941: Fix indefinite hang when using Kilo provider by adding timeout to BunProc.run (#173)\n  - Add DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS (2 minutes) for subprocess commands\n  - Add INSTALL_TIMEOUT_MS (60 seconds) for package installation\n  - Create TimeoutError for better error handling and retry logic\n  - Add retry logic for timeout errors (up to 3 attempts)\n  - Add helpful error messages for timeout and recovery scenarios\n\n  This prevents indefinite hangs caused by known Bun package manager issues:\n  - HTTP 304 response handling (oven-sh/bun#5831)\n  - Failed dependency fetch (oven-sh/bun#26341)\n  - IPv6 configuration issues\n\n## 0.13.0\n\n### Minor Changes\n\n- aa9e354: Fix Kilo provider integration: correct API endpoint, SDK, model IDs, and add device auth support (#171)\n  - Fix base URL from /api/gateway to /api/openrouter\n  - Switch SDK from @ai-sdk/openai-compatible to @openrouter/ai-sdk-provider\n  - Fix all model ID mappings to match actual Kilo API identifiers\n  - Add Kilo device auth plugin for `agent auth login`\n  - Add required Kilo headers (User-Agent, X-KILOCODE-EDITORNAME)\n\n## 0.12.3\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- c7a5154: fix: Skip malformed SSE events instead of crashing (AI_JSONParseError)\n\n  When AI gateways (e.g. OpenCode Zen) corrupt SSE stream chunks when proxying\n  provider responses (e.g. Kimi K2.5), the Vercel AI SDK emits an error event\n  with AI_JSONParseError but continues the stream. Previously, the processor\n  threw on all error events, terminating the session.\n\n  Now, following the OpenAI Codex approach (skip-and-continue), the processor\n  detects JSONParseError in stream error events, logs a warning, and continues\n  processing subsequent valid chunks. This prevents a single corrupted SSE event\n  from terminating an entire session.\n  - Skip JSONParseError in processor.ts stream error handler (Codex approach)\n  - Remove StreamParseError retry infrastructure (skip, don't retry)\n  - Add case study with comparison of 4 CLI agents (Codex, Gemini, Qwen, OpenCode)\n  - Filed upstream issues: vercel/ai#12595, anomalyco/opencode#13579\n\n  Fixes #169\n\n## 0.12.2\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- fix: Retry on stream parse errors (AI_JSONParseError)\n\n  Add StreamParseError as a retryable error type to handle malformed JSON in SSE streams\n  from AI providers. This fixes premature retry failures when providers return corrupted\n  streaming responses (e.g., concatenated SSE chunks, invalid JSON).\n\n  Changes:\n  - Add StreamParseError type with isRetryable: true\n  - Detect AI_JSONParseError, JSON parsing failures, and malformed JSON errors\n  - Retry stream parse errors with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s up to 3 retries)\n  - Add streamParseErrorDelay() function for consistent retry timing\n  - Add comprehensive test coverage for StreamParseError detection\n\n  This ensures the agent's 7-day retry window works for all transient errors,\n  not just HTTP 429 rate limits and socket errors.\n\n  Fixes #169\n\n## 0.12.1\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 9b072ca: Fix explicit provider/model routing for Kilo provider\n\n  When users specify an explicit provider/model combination like `kilo/glm-5-free`, the system now correctly uses that provider instead of silently falling back to the default (opencode).\n  - Add resolveShortModelName() to route short model names to providers\n  - Add parseModelWithResolution() for model string parsing with resolution\n  - Modify prompt.ts to throw error instead of falling back on explicit provider\n  - Add getAlternativeProviders() for rate limit fallback on shared models\n  - Document free model distribution between OpenCode and Kilo\n\n- ed7f9fc: fix: Time-based retry for rate limits at fetch level\n\n  Implement custom fetch wrapper to handle HTTP 429 (rate limit) responses at the HTTP layer,\n  ensuring the agent's time-based retry configuration is respected instead of the AI SDK's\n  fixed retry count (3 attempts).\n\n  Changes:\n  - Add RetryFetch wrapper that intercepts 429 responses before AI SDK's internal retry\n  - Parse retry-after and retry-after-ms headers from server responses\n  - Use exponential backoff when no header is present (up to 20 minutes per retry)\n  - Respect AGENT_RETRY_TIMEOUT (default: 7 weeks) as global timeout\n  - Add AGENT_MIN_RETRY_INTERVAL (default: 30 seconds) to prevent rapid retry attempts\n  - Retry network errors (socket/connection issues) with exponential backoff\n  - Compose with existing custom fetch functions (OAuth, timeout wrappers)\n\n  This fixes the issue where the AI SDK exhausted its 3 retry attempts before the agent's\n  retry logic could wait for the server's retry-after period (e.g., 64 minutes).\n\n  Fixes #167\n\n## 0.12.0\n\n### Minor Changes\n\n- 89e466d: Add Kilo Gateway provider with free models support\n  - This fix uses Bun's FFI to call prctl(PR_SET_NAME) on Linux and pthread_setname_np on macOS, which correctly sets the kernel-level process name visible in top, ps, and htop.\n\n## 0.8.19\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 5ce1b0a: Fix crash when providers return undefined usage data. Handle AI SDK TypeError for input_tokens gracefully and upgrade AI SDK to v6.0.1 which includes upstream fix. Also ensure unhandled rejections exit with code 1 instead of code 0.\n\n## 0.8.18\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 27834ef: Improve ProviderModelNotFoundError with helpful suggestions for OpenRouter models when provider is not found\n\n## 0.8.17\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- c0b2032: Fix ZodError in session processor when tool execution fails\n  - Change tool error status from 'failed' to 'error' in processor.ts to match ToolStateError Zod schema\n  - Fix cleanup loop to use 'error' status consistently with the discriminated union definition\n  - Update event-handler.js to check for 'error' status instead of 'failed'\n  - Add case study analysis for issue #149 documenting root cause and fix\n\n## 0.8.16\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- dc1d090: Improve installation instructions with step-by-step guide and troubleshooting\n  - Add numbered step-by-step installation instructions for JavaScript/Bun version\n  - Add explicit `source ~/.bashrc` step to reload shell configuration after Bun installation\n  - Add verification commands (`bun --version`, `agent --version`) to confirm successful installation\n  - Add comprehensive troubleshooting section covering common installation issues\n  - Add Rust installation prerequisites and verification steps\n  - Add case study documentation analyzing installation UX improvements (issue #136)\n\n## 0.8.15\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 10b2888: Add stream timeout to prevent agent CLI from hanging indefinitely when LLM API connections stall. Configurable via AGENT_STREAM_CHUNK_TIMEOUT_MS (default: 2min) and AGENT_STREAM_STEP_TIMEOUT_MS (default: 10min) environment variables.\n\n## 0.8.14\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 5a9f0de: Fix process name to show as 'agent' instead of 'bun' in process monitoring tools\n\n  This change sets both process.title and process.argv0 to 'agent' at CLI startup,\n  ensuring the process appears as 'agent' instead of 'bun' in monitoring tools like top and ps.\n\n## 0.8.13\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 7cff63f: Add automatic retry for timeout errors with 30s, 60s, 120s intervals\n\n  Previously, when an API request timed out (DOMException TimeoutError from AbortSignal.timeout()),\n  the agent would fail immediately. Now, timeout errors are automatically retried up to 3 times\n  with increasing delays of 30, 60, and 120 seconds.\n\n  This handles all retryable HTTP statuses (408, 409, 429, 500+) via existing APIError retry logic,\n  plus the new TimeoutError for connection-level timeouts.\n\n## 0.8.11\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- f5513da: Add OpenRouter documentation guide with detailed configuration instructions\n- 7dc074b: Add uninstallation instructions to README\n  - Add instructions for removing the agent package (global and local)\n  - Add instructions for completely removing Bun from the system\n  - Include information about cleaning up shell configuration files\n\n## 0.8.10\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 7748404: Fix CLI output streams to follow Unix conventions\n  - Route normal output (status, events, data, logs, warnings) to stdout\n  - Route error messages only to stderr\n  - Add `type` field to all JSON output for easy parsing\n  - Support `--compact-json` flag and `AGENT_CLI_COMPACT` env var for NDJSON format\n  - Flatten log format from `{ \"log\": { ... } }` to `{ \"type\": \"log\", ... }`\n\n## 0.8.9\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 6029187: Handle AI SDK unified/raw format in toFinishReason\n  - Added handling for `unified` field in `toFinishReason()` function\n  - AI SDK may return finishReason as `{unified: \"tool-calls\", raw: \"tool_calls\"}`\n  - Previously this caused JSON.stringify fallback, breaking loop exit condition\n  - Agent now correctly continues processing after tool calls\n\n  Fixes #129\n\n## 0.8.7\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- c92e114: Fix toNumber returning NaN for undefined values and extract nested cache/reasoning tokens\n  - Changed `toNumber()` to return 0 instead of NaN for undefined/null inputs (issue #127)\n  - This fixes alarming debug logs like \"toNumber error - returning NaN\" for optional fields\n  - Added extraction of `cacheRead` from nested `inputTokens` object\n  - Added extraction of `reasoning` from nested `outputTokens` object\n  - This enables proper token tracking for providers like `opencode/grok-code` that nest these values\n\n## 0.8.6\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 8ef7cfa: Handle object types for token counts and finishReason\n  - Enhanced `toNumber()` to extract `total` from objects with that field\n  - Added `toFinishReason()` to safely convert object/string finishReason values to string\n  - Updated `processor.ts` to use the new `toFinishReason()` function\n  - Fixes ZodError crashes on Ubuntu with newer Bun versions (1.3.6+)\n\n  Fixes #125\n\n## 0.8.5\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- d5bbb18: Fix shields.io badge URL in GitHub release notes\n  - Fixed badge URL generation that was broken by tag prefixes (js-, rust-)\n  - The `format-github-release.mjs` script now passes `v${version}` instead of the full tag\n  - This ensures the badge URL contains only the version number (e.g., `0.8.4`) without prefix\n  - See `docs/case-studies/issue-123` for detailed root cause analysis\n\n  Fixes #123\n\n## 0.8.4\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- afcd2f8: Fix GitHub release style to match template repository standards\n  - Fix release name format to use `[js]` prefix instead of `js ` (e.g., `[js] 0.8.4` instead of `js 0.8.4`)\n  - Fix changelog path for js releases to use `js/CHANGELOG.md` instead of root `CHANGELOG.md`\n  - This ensures release descriptions contain actual changelog content with PR links and npm badges\n\n  Fixes #121\n\n## 0.8.3\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- e869df7: Fix DecimalError crash in getUsage() when token data contains objects\n  - Add safe() wrapper function to sanitize numeric inputs before Decimal.js\n  - Wrap all token calculations with safe() to handle NaN, Infinity, and objects\n  - Add try-catch around cost calculation as additional safety measure\n  - Add comprehensive unit tests for edge cases\n\n  Fixes #119\n\n## 0.8.2\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 4146833: Add language-specific README.md files for JavaScript and Rust implementations\n  - Create js/README.md with npm-specific documentation (installation, development, publishing)\n  - Create rust/README.md with cargo-specific documentation (build, features, status)\n  - Update root README.md to serve as common entry point for both implementations\n  - Update package.json files array to include js/README.md for npm package\n\n## 0.8.1\n\n### Patch Changes\n\n- 4f4e6e7: Add publish verification and failure detection to prevent false positives\n\n  The npm publish script now detects failures even when changeset publish exits with code 0. This prevents the CI from falsely reporting success when packages fail to publish.\n\n## 0.8.0\n\n### Minor Changes\n\n- bdb2446: feat: Add Rust CLI implementation with JavaScript reorganization\n  - Reorganized JavaScript files from src/ to js/src/ for clearer project structure\n  - Moved all JS config files to js/ folder for complete isolation\n  - Created separate CI/CD pipelines (js.yml and rust.yml)\n  - Created complete Rust implementation of the agent CLI in rust/ folder\n  - Added rust/changelog.d/ for Rust changelog fragments\n  - Documented lessons learned in docs/case-studies/issue-104/\n",
